asBuilt.prescriptionAggregate:
PrescriptionStatus = EXACTLY TWO values only: NOT_GIVEN("NOT-GIVEN") and GIVEN("GIVEN") (PrescriptionStatus.java:23-26). Hyphenated DB values, mapped via PrescriptionStatusConverter (NOT @Enumerated) — Prescription.java:138-141. There is NO ACCEPTED / HELD / VERIFIED / APPROVED / REJECTED / SOLD status. Those are pharmacy-side lifecycle states that DO NOT EXIST in the clinical enum — inc-08 must NOT assume the clinical module models them.

Lifecycle: NOT_GIVEN (default at save, set in forConsultation/forNonConsultation factories — Prescription.java:413,471) -> GIVEN (terminal, set in issue() — Prescription.java:542). The single transition is Prescription.issue() (Prescription.java:509-547), legacy PatientResource.java:3217-3245.

Per-line payStatus: there is NO payStatus field. Instead there is a clinical-local boolean `settled` (Prescription.java:216-217, column `settled` NOT NULL added by V36) — set at prescribe time: true for INSURANCE/COVERED, false for CASH-OPD/CASH-OUTSIDER (Prescription.java:399,457). There is also `paymentType` VARCHAR (CASH/INSURANCE, Prescription.java:156-157) and `membershipNo` (Prescription.java:160-161) denormalised at prescribe time. markSettled() flips settled=true (Prescription.java:556-558), driven by BillSettledEvent.

Lifecycle audit triplets exist as COLUMNS for schema fidelity but are mostly NEVER WRITTEN: ordered_* written at save (Prescription.java:271-281); accepted_*/held_*/rejected_*/verified_* DECLARED-BUT-NEVER-WRITTEN boilerplate (Prescription.java:283-335); approved_* is THE dispense audit, the ONLY populated group, written in issue() (Prescription.java:339-349, 544-546).

Quantities (legacy double -> BigDecimal NUMERIC(19,6)): qty NOT NULL (Prescription.java:110-112); issued default ZERO (Prescription.java:118-119); balance = qty at create, set to 0 on dispense (Prescription.java:125-126). Dispense is ALL-OR-NOTHING: issuedQty must equal full qty (Prescription.java:529-532, "You can only issue the prescribed qty").

STOCK CHECK IS DEFERRED to inc-08: explicit TODO in Prescription.issue() (Prescription.java:534-537) and PrescriptionPort.issueMedicine javadoc (PrescriptionPort.java:73) — "verify pharmacy has sufficient stock and decrement inventory ... Belongs to the PHARMACY/INVENTORY module." issuePharmacyUid is set on dispense (Prescription.java:541, column Prescription.java:235-236).

Encounter binding: exactly one of consultation (intra-module FK, Prescription.java:246-248) / nonConsultation (FK, Prescription.java:254-256) / admissionUid (loose String, admissions DEFERRED, Prescription.java:262-263). Loose cross-module refs: medicineUid NOT NULL (Prescription.java:171-173), patientUid (V36, Prescription.java:186-187), patientBillUid NOT NULL (Prescription.java:197-199), insurancePlanUid nullable (Prescription.java:228-229), clinicianUserUid nullable (Prescription.java:224-225).

PrescriptionBatch (PrescriptionBatch.java:42-94): near-inert traceability child, NO status/lifecycle, free-text batch `no` @NotBlank (PrescriptionBatch.java:48-50), manufacturedDate/expiryDate nullable, qty BigDecimal, @ManyToOne FK to Prescription. This is where inc-08 would attach batch consumption but legacy has no consuming logic.

PatientPrescriptionChart (PatientPrescriptionChart.java:56-143): inpatient drug-administration chart, DEFERRED write path (needs admission IN-PROCESS, PatientServiceImpl.java:2564-2577). Entity+table mapped for ddl validate only; no create/update endpoints in inc-05/06.

CLINICAL READ SURFACE FOR PHARMACY — CRITICAL GAP: There is NO clinical::api cross-module Prescription read port. PrescriptionPort (PrescriptionPort.java:21) lives in clinical.application (INTRA-module only — the web controller's boundary, mirrors LabTestPort). It exposes getByUid (PrescriptionPort.java:105), byPatient (PrescriptionPort.java:121), pharmacyWorklist (PrescriptionPort.java:131, all NOT-GIVEN FIFO regardless of settled flag), issueMedicine (PrescriptionPort.java:82), addBatch/listBatches. The clinical::api named interface (clinical/api/package-info.java:1-12) exposes ONLY consultation booking + open-work lookup contracts for the registration module — NO prescription types. PrescriptionRepository.findByUid (PrescriptionRepository.java:37) and findByStatusOrderByCreatedAtAsc (PrescriptionRepository.java:143, worklist via partial index idx_prescriptions_pharmacy_worklist) exist but are package-private to clinical. inc-08 will need a NEW clinical::api published read port (read prescription by uid + dispense seam) — it does not exist yet.
asBuilt.billingSeam:
billing::api is the named interface (billing/api/package-info.java:15). recordClinicalCharge EXISTS: BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge(ChargeRequest, TxAuditContext) -> ChargeResult (BillingCommands.java:34). Runs in caller's transaction, propagation REQUIRED, no @Async/REQUIRES_NEW (BillingCommands.java:10-13). ChargeRequest record (ChargeRequest.java:27-38): patientUid, planUid, membershipNo, ServiceKind kind, serviceUid, BigDecimal qty, PaymentMode paymentType, boolean inpatient, boolean followUp — for medicine, kind=MEDICINE, serviceUid=medicineUid, qty=prescribed qty (PrescriptionPort.java:32). ChargeResult record (ChargeResult.java:19-24): billUid, BillStatus status, MoneyDto amount, CoverageStatus coverageStatus.

Also on BillingCommands: cancelCharge(billUid, reference, ctx) — soft-cancel + refund + PENDING credit note (BillingCommands.java:61); approveInvoicesForBills(billUids, ctx) (BillingCommands.java:76).

SETTLED FLAG + SettlementDispatcher PATTERN EXISTS. PatientBill carries boolean `settled` default false (PatientBill.java:162-163) and settledAt (PatientBill.java:166-167); markSettled(Instant) sets both (PatientBill.java:274-276). SettlementDispatcher (SettlementDispatcher.java:46) is called by PaymentService in the SAME tx as the PAID transition (billing->encounter direction only, ADR-0008 §6). onBillPaid(PatientBill, TxAuditContext) (SettlementDispatcher.java:60) step1 marks bill.markSettled, step2 publishes shared.event.BillSettledEvent (BillSettledEvent.java:38, record(billUid, settledAt)). Consumed by clinical ConsultationSettlementListener with @TransactionalEventListener(BEFORE_COMMIT) which flips the local settled flag on Consultation/LabTest/Radiology/Procedure/Prescription matching the bill uid (SettlementDispatcher.java:28-36; PrescriptionRepository.findByPatientBillUid — PrescriptionRepository.java:54). No billing->clinical compile edge; no cycle.

SettlementPolicy (SettlementPolicy.java:22) is the pay-before-service rule published in billing.api: requiresPrepayment(paymentType, inpatient, emergency) returns true ONLY for CASH non-inpatient non-emergency (SettlementPolicy.java:36-47); requireSettled(...) throws PayBeforeServiceException 422 (SettlementPolicy.java:60-65). NOTE: pharmacyWorklist intentionally ignores settled (PrescriptionRepository.java:133-143; PrescriptionPort.java:124-131) — pharmacist validates payment physically.

BillingQueries.getBillStatus READ SEAM EXISTS (added inc-06A C4). BillingQueries.getBillStatus(billUid) -> BillStatus, throws NotFoundException (BillingQueries.java:36). Published in billing::api (billing/api/package-info.java:6-9). ADR-0008 §6 scoped relaxation: clinical reads LIVE PatientBill.status for add_report bill-gate parity ONLY (admits PAID|COVERED|VERIFIED); settlement gates must keep using local settled flag (BillingQueries.java:12-25). BillStatus enum values: UNPAID, VERIFIED, COVERED, PAID, CANCELED (single-L) (BillStatus.java:27-51). Payable states = UNPAID, VERIFIED (BillStatus.java:17). inc-08 dispense gate can reuse getBillStatus to check live PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED before decrementing stock.

extract.lane: Prescription dispensing lifecycle + CASH pay gate
extract.summary: There is NO dedicated "Prescription" aggregate inside the pharmacy module. Dispensing runs off two parallel record types: (1) the clinical-side `Prescription` entity (com.orbix.api.domain.Prescription) created by the doctor during a Consultation/NonConsultation/Admission, and (2) the walk-in `PharmacySaleOrder` + `PharmacySaleOrderDetail` entities for over-the-counter pharmacy sales. PharmacyServiceImpl.java contains ONLY pharmacy CRUD + opening/update stock-card writes; ALL dispensing logic lives in PatientResource.java (clinical issue) and PatientBillResource.java (cashier pay-and-approve), with creation in PatientServiceImpl.java. The clinical Prescription state machine is exactly two states for the medicine flow: NOT-GIVEN -> GIVEN. The CASH pay gate for clinical prescriptions is a WORKLIST FILTER ONLY (the pharmacy worklist endpoints exclude patients whose PatientBill is not PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED); the terminal POST /patients/issue_medicine (PatientResource.java:3199-3293) does NOT re-check the bill status at all and will dispense any prescription whose status is NOT-GIVEN with sufficient stock. For walk-in PharmacySaleOrder the gate IS effectively hard: GET /patients/give_medicine (PatientResource.java:6212) rejects unless the order status == APPROVED, and an order only becomes APPROVED inside the cashier payment handler (PatientBillResource.java:369-387) when the CASH bill is paid.

findings.topic: No pharmacy Prescription aggregate / dispensing record split
findings.behaviour: Dispensing is NOT driven by any pharmacy-owned Prescription aggregate. Two separate record types carry the dispensing status: clinical-side `Prescription` (status field, default set in code) and walk-in `PharmacySaleOrderDetail` (status default 'PENDING', payStatus default 'UNPAID'). PharmacyServiceImpl only does pharmacy CRUD and opening/update stock cards; it contains no dispense/sell logic.

Only two states are used for medicines: created as 'NOT-GIVEN' (PatientServiceImpl.savePrescription) and transitioned to 'GIVEN' on dispense (PatientResource.issueMedicine). The accept/hold/reject/verify columns (acceptedby, heldby, rejectedby, verifiedby) exist on the entity but are DEAD for the medicine flow - no endpoint sets ACCEPTED/HELD/REJECTED/VERIFIED on a Prescription. The dispense reuses the approvedBy/approvedOn fields, not soldBy.

findings.topic: Clinical dispense terminal = issue_medicine (NO hard pay gate)
findings.behaviour: POST /patients/issue_medicine accepts a List<Prescription> + pharmacy_id. Guards in order: (1) prescription must exist; (2) status must equal 'NOT-GIVEN' else InvalidOperationException 'not a pending prescription'; (3) issued value validation (balance>issued OR issued<=0 rejected); (4) issued must equal qty else 'You can only issue the prescribed qty'. There is NO check of prescription.getPatientBill().getStatus() anywhere in this method - payment is NOT enforced at the terminal. After validation it sets status GIVEN, issuePharmacy, decrements PharmacyMedicine.stock, writes a PharmacyStockCard OUT row, and FEFO-deducts batches.

findings.topic: CASH pay gate for clinical prescriptions = WORKLIST FILTER
findings.behaviour: The pharmacy worklist endpoints filter patients/prescriptions by PatientBill.status, which is the ONLY place payment is enforced for clinical dispensing. OUTPATIENT and OUTSIDER require bill status PAID or COVERED; INPATIENT additionally accepts VERIFIED. get_prescriptions_by_patient_id returns only prescriptions whose bill is PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED. Because issue_medicine itself does not re-check the bill, the gate is bypassable if the endpoint is called directly.
findings.cite: PatientResource.java:4347 (OUTPATIENT PAID/COVERED); PatientResource.java:4364 (OUTSIDER PAID/COVERED); PatientResource.java:4381 (INPATIENT PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED); PatientResource.java:4410 (get_prescriptions_by_patient_id PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED)

findings.topic: How bill status (the gate input) is set at prescription creation
findings.behaviour: savePrescription creates PatientBill amount = medicine.price * qty, status 'UNPAID' (CASH default). If patient.paymentType=='INSURANCE' AND a covered MedicineInsurancePlan exists -> bill set COVERED (paid in full at plan price, paymentType INSURANCE, attached to a PENDING PatientInvoice). If admission present (INPATIENT) AND no covered plan -> bill set VERIFIED (post-pay/credit, balance retained, added to a non-insurance PENDING PatientInvoice). CASH outpatient/outsider remains UNPAID until cashier marks PAID. This is exactly why INPATIENT worklist also admits VERIFIED.

findings.topic: INPATIENT vs OUTPATIENT vs OUTSIDER distinction
findings.behaviour: The only behavioural difference is in the worklist filter: INPATIENT prescriptions are dispensable when the bill is VERIFIED (i.e. before cash is collected - inpatient credit), whereas OUTPATIENT and OUTSIDER require PAID or COVERED. There is no separate dispense method per patient type; all route through the same issue_medicine terminal.

Order statuses: PENDING (on create) -> APPROVED (on cashier payment) -> ARCHIVED (manual or auto after 24h when all details GIVEN); or PENDING -> CANCELED (manual, only PENDING; or auto after 24h). Detail statuses: created NOT-GIVEN/payStatus UNPAID -> payStatus PAID + soldBy set on payment -> status GIVEN on give_medicine. cancelPharmacySaleOrderById rejects unless status==PENDING. archivePharmacySaleOrderById rejects unless status==APPROVED and every detail GIVEN.
findings.cite: PatientServiceImpl.java:3341-3344 (PENDING+CASH on create); PatientServiceImpl.java:3075-3079 (cancel guard); PatientServiceImpl.java:3128-3138 (archive guard); PatientServiceImpl.java:3199-3224 (auto archive/cancel 24h)

findings.topic: Walk-in CASH pay gate = HARD (via order status)
findings.behaviour: GET /patients/give_medicine throws InvalidOperationException 'Order not approved' unless order.status=='APPROVED'. The order only reaches APPROVED inside the cashier payment endpoint (PatientBillResource) which, after marking the CASH PatientBill PAID, detects the PharmacySaleOrderDetail via findByPatientBill, sets the order APPROVED and each detail payStatus PAID + soldBy/soldOn/soldAt. So for walk-in sales, payment is a hard precondition for dispensing even though give_medicine never reads the bill directly.
findings.cite: PatientResource.java:6230-6232 (hard reject if not APPROVED); PatientBillResource.java:369-387 (order set APPROVED + detail payStatus PAID on payment)

findings.behaviour: Iterates order details; each must be status NOT-GIVEN else reject; issued forced to qty; validation balance>issued or issued<=0 rejected, issued!=qty rejected; sets detail GIVEN, sets issuePharmacy (NOT salesPharmacy), decrements PharmacyMedicine.stock, writes PharmacyStockCard OUT row 'Issued in sale: id <n>'. Note: batch FEFO deduction is COMMENTED OUT in this path (deductBatch call is disabled), so walk-in sales do NOT write PrescriptionBatch/decrement PharmacyMedicineBatch rows - unlike clinical issue_medicine which does.

issue_medicine calls deductBatch -> getEarlierBatch: among PharmacyMedicineBatch rows with qty>0, if any have an expiryDate it selects the EARLIEST expiryDate (FEFO); if none have expiry it falls back to LOWEST id (insertion order ~ FIFO). deductBatch recurses across batches if qty exceeds a single batch, writing a PrescriptionBatch row per consumed batch and decrementing PharmacyMedicineBatch.qty. The whole deductBatch body is wrapped in try/catch that swallows all exceptions (catch does nothing).
findings.cite: PatientResource.java:3296-3336 (deductBatch + swallowed catch); PatientResource.java:3338-3376 (getEarlierBatch FEFO then id fallback)

findings.behaviour: Both terminals check PharmacyMedicine.getStock() < prescription/detail.getQty() and throw 'Can not issue, insufficient stock in: <medicine>'. CRITICAL ORDERING BUG: in both methods the prescription/detail is already set to GIVEN and saved (and status persisted) BEFORE the stock check executes; the stock guard runs after the status mutation, so a stock-failure exception rolls back only via the surrounding @Transactional on the controller class. Stock deduction subtracts getQty() (not getIssued()) from PharmacyMedicine while the stock card OUT row records getIssued(); since issued is forced to equal qty these match, but the asymmetry is real in code.
findings.cite: PatientResource.java:3227-3247 (status GIVEN saved at 3237 before stock check at 3243); PatientResource.java:6256-6276 (detail GIVEN saved at 6266 before stock check at 6272)

findings.behaviour: PharmacyStockCard OUT rows are written inside the same terminal method/transaction as the status change: clinical at PatientResource.java:3252-3264 (reference 'Issued in prescription: id <n>'), walk-in at PatientResource.java:6281-6293 (reference 'Issued in sale: id <n>'). Opening-stock and stock-update IN rows are written in PharmacyServiceImpl.save (registration) and PharmacyResource.updateStock.

findings.behaviour: delete_prescription allows deletion only when status is 'PENDING' or 'NOT-GIVEN'. If a PatientPaymentDetail exists with status 'GIVEN' it creates a PatientCreditNote (amount = bill amount, status PENDING, no=patientCreditNoteService.requestPatientCreditNoteNo). It deletes any PatientInvoiceDetail (and the PatientInvoice if it becomes empty - via a no-op counter bug j=j++ that never increments), deletes the PatientPaymentDetail if present, then deletes the Prescription and its PatientBill.

save_prescription (2093), issue_medicine (3199) and give_medicine (6212) carry NO @PreAuthorize annotation - any authenticated user (valid JWT) can call them. Access control for dispensing is effectively absent at the method level; only the worklist UI restricts visibility. Contrast: pharmacies/save and pharmacists/save require 'ADMIN-ACCESS', update_stock requires 'MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE'.

findings.topic: Cashier payment handler is the single financial trigger
findings.behaviour: The pay-bills endpoint in PatientBillResource validates each PatientBill is UNPAID or VERIFIED (else reject), sets it PAID, writes PatientPaymentDetail + Collection (paymentChannel hardcoded 'Cash'), marks linked PatientInvoiceDetail PAID and increments PatientInvoice.amountPaid, and (if any bill maps to a PharmacySaleOrderDetail) flips the whole order to APPROVED. Final guard: if summed paid amount != totalAmount it throws 'amount mismatch' AFTER mutations (relies on @Transactional rollback).

verdicts.planClaim: Prescription status machine: PENDING → ACCEPTED → HELD → VERIFIED → APPROVED → SOLD; terminal REJECTED, CANCELLED (six-state lifecycle with two terminals, eight states total).
verdicts.evidence: The legacy clinical Prescription medicine flow has exactly TWO states: created 'NOT-GIVEN' (PatientServiceImpl.java:1532) and transitioned to 'GIVEN' on dispense (PatientResource.java:3230). The accept/hold/reject/verify columns (acceptedby/heldby/rejectedby/verifiedby at Prescription.java:114-137) EXIST on the entity but are DEAD for the medicine flow — a cross-module grep for setStatus(ACCEPTED|HELD|REJECTED|VERIFIED) and setAcceptedby/setHeldby/setRejectedby/setVerifiedby finds NO hit that touches a Prescription or PharmacySaleOrderDetail (only LabTest, Radiology, LPO, GRN, Payroll, RO/TO, PatientBill). No endpoint sets ACCEPTED/HELD/VERIFIED/APPROVED/SOLD/REJECTED/CANCELLED on a prescription. This is the exact inc-06 phantom pattern: dormant columns presented as a live state machine. There is no SOLD state; the dispense reuses approvedBy/approvedOn (PatientResource.java:3233-3235), not soldBy.
verdicts.correction: The legacy medicine lifecycle is two states: NOT-GIVEN → GIVEN. The six intermediate states (ACCEPTED/HELD/VERIFIED/APPROVED/SOLD) and the CANCELLED/REJECTED terminals are NOT reproduced legacy behaviour for prescriptions. If a richer pharmacist-worklist lifecycle is genuinely desired, it must be raised as NEW SCOPE via the change-request register and approved at the phase gate — it cannot be presented as 'exact process'. The accept/hold/verify endpoints in the doc (lines 38-44) have no legacy operation behind them for the medicine flow.

verdicts.planClaim: Per-line payStatus UNPAID → PAID gate for CASH patients.
verdicts.evidence: A two-state payStatus UNPAID → PAID exists, but ONLY on the walk-in PharmacySaleOrderDetail (default 'UNPAID' at PharmacySaleOrderDetail.java:41-42; flipped to PAID in the cashier handler PatientBillResource.java:369-387). The clinical Prescription has NO payStatus field — payment for clinical prescriptions is carried on the linked PatientBill.status (UNPAID/PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED), not a per-line payStatus. So the named field is a faithful modern unification of two different legacy mechanisms (PatientBill.status for clinical, detail.payStatus for walk-in) onto one per-line concept.
verdicts.correction: State explicitly that for clinical prescriptions the legacy pay signal is PatientBill.status (PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED), and only walk-in PharmacySaleOrderDetail carries a real UNPAID→PAID payStatus. Modelling both as a unified per-line payStatus is acceptable re-modelling ONLY if the COVERED (insurance) and VERIFIED (inpatient credit) bill states are preserved as non-blocking equivalents (see worklist claim) — a binary UNPAID/PAID alone would lose the COVERED/VERIFIED distinction the legacy depends on.

verdicts.planClaim: accept() callable on PENDING regardless of pay-status; sell() HARD-rejects unless every line payStatus=PAID; non-CASH treated as COVERED, sell() proceeds without pay check. 'This is a hard service-layer gate, not a worklist filter.'
verdicts.evidence: For the CLINICAL dispense path this directly CONTRADICTS the legacy. POST /patients/issue_medicine (PatientResource.java:3199-3293, read in full) NEVER references prescription.getPatientBill().getStatus(); its only guards are status=='NOT-GIVEN' (3217), issue-value validation (3221), issued==qty (3224), and a post-mutation stock check (3243). Payment is enforced EXCLUSIVELY in the worklist filters (4347 OUTPATIENT PAID/COVERED, 4364 OUTSIDER PAID/COVERED, 4381 INPATIENT PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED, 4410 get_prescriptions_by_patient_id) — i.e. a WORKLIST FILTER, the exact opposite of the doc's emphatic 'hard service-layer gate, not a worklist filter'. The legacy gate is bypassable by calling issue_medicine directly. There is also NO accept() operation in legacy to be 'callable on PENDING'. The walk-in path IS hard-gated, but via order.status=='APPROVED' (PatientResource.java:6230), not via a per-line payStatus=PAID check inside the dispense method, and APPROVED is set by the cashier handler (PatientBillResource.java:369-387).
verdicts.correction: The legacy reality is: (a) clinical dispense (issue_medicine) has NO terminal pay re-check — the only enforcement is the worklist filter on PatientBill.status; (b) walk-in dispense (give_medicine) is hard-gated on order.status=='APPROVED', not on a per-line payStatus. The doc's prescriptive design (hard sell() gate on per-line payStatus=PAID, with a bypassable-filter-is-a-defect framing) is a deliberate IMPROVEMENT over the legacy clinical behaviour (which is genuinely a security/integrity weakness — direct-call bypass). That is defensible as a hardening, but it is a DEVIATION from extracted legacy behaviour and must be logged in the change-request register with engagement-lead sign-off, NOT presented as exact-process fidelity. 'Non-CASH treated as COVERED' aligns with legacy COVERED/VERIFIED semantics and is fine.

verdicts.planClaim: worklist defaults settledOnly=true for OUTPATIENT/OUTSIDER CASH; INPATIENT CASH visible at VERIFIED (insurer verifies at discharge).
verdicts.evidence: The patient-type behavioural split is reproduced exactly: OUTPATIENT and OUTSIDER worklists admit only PatientBill.status PAID or COVERED (PatientResource.java:4347, 4364); INPATIENT additionally admits VERIFIED (4381), as does get_prescriptions_by_patient_id (4410). VERIFIED is set at prescription creation for inpatients with no covered plan (post-pay/credit) per PatientServiceImpl.java:1622-1628. The 'settledOnly' filter concept faithfully maps to the legacy PAID/COVERED requirement.
verdicts.correction: Two precision corrections: (1) The rationale 'insurer verifies at discharge' is imprecise — legacy VERIFIED is the INPATIENT CREDIT / post-pay state (balance retained, attached to a non-insurance PENDING PatientInvoice, PatientServiceImpl.java:1622-1628), it is not an insurer verification step. INSURANCE-covered patients get COVERED, not VERIFIED. (2) 'settledOnly' should treat COVERED as settled too, not just PAID — OUTPATIENT/OUTSIDER worklists pass on PAID OR COVERED. Keep the patient-type matrix exactly as cited; fix the explanatory note.

verdicts.planClaim: issuePharmacyUid vs salesPharmacyUid split per line; stock decrement hits salesPharmacyUid, dispense stamps issuePharmacyUid. (Exact-process target 7: fill at pharmacy A, pull from pharmacy B; B decrements, A unaffected.)
verdicts.evidence: salesPharmacy is NEVER populated in the legacy. A grep for setSalesPharmacy/salesPharmacy across com.orbix.api yields only the field DECLARATION at PharmacySaleOrderDetail.java:76 — it is never assigned anywhere. Both dispense terminals set issuePharmacy from the single pharmacy_id request param (PatientResource.java:3231 clinical, 6260 walk-in) and decrement that same PharmacyMedicine's stock (3239/3249, 6268/6278). There is one pharmacy per dispense, stamped as issuePharmacy; the stock decrement hits that same pharmacy. The 'split' and the 'fill at A, pull from B' cross-pharmacy parity test describe behaviour that does not exist in legacy.
verdicts.correction: Remove the salesPharmacy split and the A/B cross-pharmacy parity test from the exact-process targets. Legacy uses a single pharmacy per dispense (issuePharmacy = stock-source pharmacy = the pharmacy_id parameter); there is no legal cross-pharmacy pull without a transfer document. If split-source dispensing is a genuine product requirement it is NEW SCOPE for the change-request register, not a parity target — writing a golden-master 'parity' test for it would test invented behaviour. The Prescription.java schema has no salesPharmacy field at all; only PharmacySaleOrderDetail has the unused declaration.

verdicts.planClaim: PHARM-2/M3/M23: prior build allowed accept()/hold() on unpaid; only markSold had the gate. (Doc note: legacy method names may be markSold/issued not sell.)
verdicts.evidence: This is a claim about the PRIOR REBUILD ATTEMPT (HMSCLEAN/inc-06-era), not about the legacy ZANAHMIS-2 source — the rx-lifecycle lane was extracted from the legacy and does not carry prior-rebuild evidence, so the specific 'prior build allowed accept()/hold() on unpaid; only markSold had the gate' assertion cannot be confirmed from this lane. What CAN be confirmed against legacy: there are no accept()/hold() operations in legacy at all; the only effective terminal gate is the walk-in order.status=='APPROVED' check (PatientResource.java:6230), and the clinical terminal has no gate (worklist filter only). The parenthetical caveat that legacy method names are issue/markSold/give (issueMedicine PatientResource.java:3199, giveMedicine 6212) rather than 'sell' is CORRECT and is good adversarial hygiene.
verdicts.correction: Tag this bullet as referencing the prior-rebuild lessons register (PHARM-2/M3/M23), not legacy extraction, so reviewers do not treat it as a legacy parity claim. Verify the 'accept()/hold() allowed on unpaid in prior build' detail against the prior-rebuild lane before relying on it. Note for the team: in LEGACY there is no accept/hold to gate — the genuine legacy weakness is that the clinical terminal (issue_medicine) has NO pay gate at all (worklist-filter-only, bypassable), which is a stronger finding than 'only markSold had the gate' and should be the design driver.

newDriftFound: CRITICAL ORDERING BUG in both legacy dispense terminals: the prescription/detail is set to 'GIVEN' and SAVED (PatientResource.java:3237 clinical, 6266 walk-in) BEFORE the insufficient-stock check runs (3243, 6272). The status mutation is only undone by the controller-class @Transactional rollback. The planning doc's exact-process target 2 says 'Negative-stock guard runs after the lock is held, not before' — inc-08 must decide whether to preserve this after-mutation ordering (it is the legacy behaviour) or correct it to a check-before-mutate flow (an improvement requiring a change request). The doc does not mention this ordering at all.

Stock decrement uses getQty() but the stock-card OUT row records getIssued() (PatientResource.java:3247/3256, 6276/6285). They match only because issued is force-set equal to qty (3224, 6247-6253). inc-08 unifies these on remainingQty decrement; it should preserve the qty==issued invariant or the asymmetry could resurface. Not addressed in the doc.

FEFO is CLINICAL-PATH-ONLY in legacy. deductBatch is invoked for clinical issue_medicine (PatientResource.java:3288) via getEarlierBatch (earliest expiryDate, falling back to LOWEST id ~FIFO when no expiry), but the deductBatch call is COMMENTED OUT for walk-in give_medicine (line ~6317). So legacy walk-in OTC sales decrement PharmacyMedicine.stock + write a stock card but do NOT touch PharmacyMedicineBatch or write PrescriptionBatch rows. The inc-08 doc (line 18) says OTC sale orders have 'identical status lifecycle' and applies the same FEFO batch model to OTC — applying FEFO batch deduction to OTC is a DEVIATION (the legacy never did it for walk-in). Whether this is a deliberate fix or accidental over-application must be flagged; it changes batch/expiry accounting for retail sales.

deductBatch wraps its entire body in a try/catch that SWALLOWS all exceptions (PatientResource.java:3296+, empty catch). In legacy, a batch-deduction failure is silently ignored while the PharmacyMedicine.stock and stock-card still update — batch ledger can silently diverge from the stock total. inc-08's atomic/locked FEFO design (target 2) implicitly corrects this; it should be logged as a deliberate correction of a legacy defect, not silently assumed.

FEFO tie-break / null-expiry rule: legacy getEarlierBatch (PatientResource.java:3338-3376) selects EARLIEST expiryDate among qty>0 batches, and when NO batch has an expiry it falls back to LOWEST id (insertion order). The doc's lockFefoForDispense orders 'expiry_date ASC NULLS LAST' (target 2) — NULLS LAST matches the legacy behaviour of preferring dated batches first, but the legacy secondary sort among null-expiry batches is by id ASC, which the doc does not specify. Pin down the secondary sort to id ASC to preserve legacy consumption order.

Numbering-scheme divergence and double-save: legacy PharmacySaleOrder.no = 'PSO/'+id via a two-stage save (random placeholder then PSO/<id>), and PharmacyCustomer.no has TWO divergent legacy formats ('PCST'+id at PatientServiceImpl.java:3313 vs 'PCST/'+Year+'/'+id at 3249); PatientInvoice.no is Math.random() then overwritten to id.toString(). The doc's document-number section (target 5) covers GRN/LPO/RO/TO/RN but is SILENT on PSO, PCST, and PatientInvoice numbering — inc-08 must decide and CR-log how these three legacy schemes (including the inconsistent PCST format) are reproduced or normalized.

delete_prescription credit-note path (PatientResource.java:3540-3606): legacy allows delete only when status is 'PENDING' or 'NOT-GIVEN', and if a GIVEN PatientPaymentDetail exists it raises a PatientCreditNote (amount = bill amount, status PENDING). It also contains a no-op counter bug (j=j++ at 3587-3589) that prevents the parent PatientInvoice from being deleted when it becomes empty. The inc-08 doc has NO cancel/credit-note story for prescriptions at all — the cancel endpoint (line 44) has no legacy semantics defined behind it. This whole reversal/credit-note flow is missing from the plan.

Walk-in PharmacySaleOrder full lifecycle includes ARCHIVED and CANCELED with a 24-hour auto-transition (auto-archive when APPROVED + all details GIVEN, auto-cancel of stale PENDING) at PatientServiceImpl.java:3199-3224, plus cancel-guard (status==PENDING only) and archive-guard (status==APPROVED + all GIVEN). The doc's OTC lifecycle is described as 'identical to Prescription' (line 18) and omits ARCHIVED/CANCELED and the 24h auto-job entirely. The OTC terminal/archival states and the scheduled auto-transition are unaddressed.

No @PreAuthorize on ANY legacy dispense endpoint: save_prescription (PatientResource.java:2093), issue_medicine (3199-3203), give_medicine (6212-6216) carry NO method-level authorization — any authenticated JWT can dispense. The doc's DoD (line 173) and dependency note (line 100) assert a '177 privilege codes' set with @PreAuthorize on all pharmacy/procurement endpoints including PRESCRIPTION-ALL. This is the same 177-code RBAC over-claim flagged in inc-06: the 177-code figure and per-endpoint @PreAuthorize on dispensing have NO legacy basis (only pharmacies/save, pharmacists/save require ADMIN-ACCESS and update_stock requires MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE — PharmacyResource.java:122,200,217-229). Adding RBAC to dispense is a desirable NEW security control (net-new per the audit-trail standing finding), but must be labelled net-new, not legacy-reproduction, and the '177' count must be substantiated against I01 rather than asserted.

extract.summary: OTC walk-in pharmacy sales are modeled by PharmacySaleOrder (header) + PharmacySaleOrderDetail (lines) tied to a PharmacyCustomer, NOT to a clinical Patient/Visit. All OTC business logic lives in PatientServiceImpl (save/cancel/archive/getById) and in raw controller methods inside PharmacyResource and PatientBillResource — there is NO dedicated PharmacyServiceImpl OTC code. The header lifecycle is PENDING -> APPROVED -> ARCHIVED (plus PENDING -> CANCELED), where the PENDING->APPROVED transition is a SIDE EFFECT of paying the linked PatientBill rows via /bills/confirm_bills_payment, not an explicit approve endpoint. Each detail carries TWO independent status fields: a fulfilment status (entity default 'PENDING', but set to 'NOT-GIVEN' on save; -> 'GIVEN' on dispense) and a payStatus ('UNPAID' -> 'PAID'). Stock is decremented for OTC ONLY in the controller method PatientResource.giveMedicine (/patients/give_medicine), which decrements PharmacyMedicine.stock and writes a PharmacyStockCard row but — unlike prescription issuing — does NOT call deductBatch, so PharmacyMedicineBatch (FEFO batch) quantities are NOT reduced for OTC sales (the deductBatch call is commented out).

findings.behaviour: PharmacySaleOrder header fields: id, no (unique, @NotBlank), paymentType (@NotBlank; comment lists CASH,DEBIT CARD,CREDIT CARD,MOBILE,INSURANCE), status (@NotBlank), comments. Mandatory EAGER FK to PharmacyCustomer (nullable=false, updatable=false), Pharmacy (nullable=false, updatable=false) and Pharmacist (nullable=false, updatable=true). Has createdBy/On/At, approvedBy/On/At, canceledBy/On/At audit triplets. One-to-many to PharmacySaleOrderDetail (EAGER, orphanRemoval=true). NO Patient FK, NO Visit/NonConsultation FK on the header.

PharmacySaleOrderDetail fields: dosage, frequency, route, days, qty (@NotNull double), issued (default 0), balance, status (field default 'PENDING'), payStatus (field default 'UNPAID'), reference, instructions, paymentType (default 'CASH'). Mandatory FK to PharmacySaleOrder and Medicine; mandatory @OneToOne to PatientBill (nullable=false). Optional FKs issuePharmacy, salesPharmacy, pharmacist. Carries full audit triplets incl. soldBy/On/At and approvedBy/On/At. So each line item is billed via its OWN PatientBill row.

PharmacyCustomer fields: id, no (unique, @NotBlank), name (@NotBlank), gender, phoneNo, address, createdBy/On/At. It is a standalone walk-in customer record, completely separate from Patient. Validation only enforces @NotBlank on no and name; gender/phone/address are optional.

Observed status string values for PharmacySaleOrder: 'PENDING' (set on create), 'APPROVED' (set on bill payment), 'CANCELED' (manual or auto-expiry), 'ARCHIVED' (after all details GIVEN). Worklist getPharmacySaleOrders() returns only status IN ('PENDING','APPROVED').

findings.topic: OTC vs Prescription lifecycle — NOT identical
findings.behaviour: The OTC PharmacySaleOrder lifecycle is NOT identical to Prescription. Prescription detail uses statuses NOT-GIVEN -> GIVEN with no header-level PENDING/APPROVED/ARCHIVED and no separate payStatus on a sale-order header. PharmacySaleOrder adds a HEADER state machine (PENDING/APPROVED/CANCELED/ARCHIVED) plus per-detail payStatus, and the approve step is driven by payment, whereas prescriptions are issued (issue_medicine) directly once their PatientBill is PAID/COVERED with no header approval.

savePharmacySaleOrder: (1) ensures a dummy Patient with no='GENERAL' exists, creating it if absent (active=false, gender 'NONE', random searchKey/no). (2) Customer: if order.pharmacyCustomer.id != null it loads existing and validates no matches (else throws 'Invalid customer'); else creates a NEW PharmacyCustomer with only name+phoneNo copied (NOT gender/address), temp no=random, then renumbers no='PCST'+id. (3) For a NEW order: sets status='PENDING', paymentType='CASH' (HARDCODED, ignores incoming paymentType), temp no=random, then renumbers no='PSO/'+id. Pharmacy resolved by NAME, pharmacist by id.

PharmacySaleOrder.no = 'PSO/' + id (id from IDENTITY autoincrement; save-then-renumber, MAX-id is implicit via the generated id, no fiscal-year segment, no zero-padding). PharmacyCustomer.no created via savePharmacySaleOrder = 'PCST'+id (no slash, no year); but via the separate createPharmacyCustomer service method it is 'PCST/'+Year.now()+'/'+id (slash + year). TWO DIFFERENT customer numbering formats exist depending on entry path.

savePharmacySaleOrderDetail: requires parent order present and status=='PENDING' (else 'Only pending orders can be updated'); resolves medicine by NAME. Sets detail.status='NOT-GIVEN', payStatus='UNPAID', issued=0, balance=qty. Creates a PatientBill: amount = medicine.getPrice() * qty, paid=0, balance=amount, qty=qty, billItem='Medicine Sale', description='Medicine: '+name, status='UNPAID', patient=GENERAL dummy patient. Price is the flat Medicine.price; NO insurance plan pricing, NO co-pay, NO discount applied for OTC. Bill is linked 1:1 to the detail.

Selling is PER-DETAIL at billing/payment level (each detail has its own PatientBill, paid individually through the bills list), but DISPENSING (give_medicine) is WHOLE-ORDER: it takes a single order_id and loops over ALL details, requiring each to be NOT-GIVEN, and fails the whole call if any detail is not dispensable. There is no partial-quantity issue — code forces issued == qty.

There is NO explicit OTC approve endpoint. The header moves to APPROVED inside POST /bills/confirm_bills_payment: while paying bills it detects if any bill maps to a PharmacySaleOrderDetail (findByPatientBill); if so sets pharmacySaleOrder.status='APPROVED' + approved audit, and sets every detail's payStatus='PAID' and sold audit triplet. Note it only flips the order it found via the FIRST matching detail (isPharmacySaleOrder guard), and marks ALL details of that order PAID regardless of whether each detail's own bill was in the paid list.

GET /patients/give_medicine(pharmacy_id, order_id): HARD rejects if order.status != 'APPROVED' ('Order not approved'). Per detail: HARD reject if detail.status != 'NOT-GIVEN'; sets issued=qty; rejects if balance>issued or issued<=0; rejects if issued != qty ('You can only issue the specified qty'). Sets detail status='GIVEN', issuePharmacy, approved audit. Loads PharmacyMedicine by (pharmacy,medicine); HARD reject if stock < qty ('insufficient stock') — negative-stock prevention gate. Decrements PharmacyMedicine.stock by qty, writes a PharmacyStockCard (qtyIn=0, qtyOut=issued, balance=newStock, reference='Issued in sale: id '+detailId). NOTE: payStatus is never re-checked here; dispense relies solely on header==APPROVED.

give_medicine does NOT decrement PharmacyMedicineBatch quantities. It fetches pharmacyMedicineBatches via findAllByPharmacyAndMedicineAndQtyGreaterThan but the deductBatch(...) call is COMMENTED OUT (line 6317). Contrast: the prescription issue_medicine path DOES call deductBatch (line 3288) which performs FEFO (earliest expiry, else lowest id) batch reduction and writes PrescriptionBatch rows. Therefore OTC sales reduce only the aggregate PharmacyMedicine.stock and write a stock card, but leave batch-level stock and expiry tracking untouched.

GET /pharmacies/pharmacy_sale_orders/cancel: cancelPharmacySaleOrderById HARD rejects unless status=='PENDING' (throws NotFoundException 'Only pending orders can be canceled'); sets status='CANCELED' + canceled audit. Auto-cancel (cancelPharmacySaleOrders) flips any PENDING order older than 24h (ChronoUnit.HOURS >= 24) to CANCELED with comment 'Autocanceled after expiry'.

GET /pharmacies/pharmacy_sale_orders/archive: archivePharmacySaleOrderById HARD rejects unless status=='APPROVED' and every detail.status=='GIVEN' (else 'Only given orders can be archived'); sets status='ARCHIVED'. Auto-archive (archivePharmacySaleOrders) archives APPROVED orders older than 24h whose details are all GIVEN. archive_all endpoint runs both archive + cancel sweeps.

GET /patients/delete_pharmacy_sale_order_detail: allows delete only if detail.status IN ('PENDING','NOT-GIVEN') AND its PatientBill.status=='UNPAID'. If a PatientPaymentDetail exists with status 'GIVEN' it rejects 'Medicine already given'. Deletes associated PatientInvoiceDetail (and empty PatientInvoice), PatientPaymentDetail, the PharmacySaleOrderDetail and the PatientBill. (A PatientCreditNote branch exists but is gated behind the same already-rejected GIVEN condition, so it is effectively dead code.)

findings.topic: OTC worklist is a FILTER, not status gate
findings.behaviour: getPharmacySaleOrders() is a worklist FILTER returning status IN ('PENDING','APPROVED'). The OTC outsider list at /patients/get_pharmacy_outsider_list is unrelated to PharmacySaleOrder — it filters clinical Prescriptions for OUTSIDER patients with bill PAID/COVERED, confirming OTC sale orders flow through a different screen than OUTSIDER clinical prescriptions.

The pharmacy-sales-order component calls in sequence: POST /pharmacies/save_pharmacy_sale_order (header), POST /pharmacies/save_pharmacy_sale_order_detail (per line), POST /bills/confirm_bills_payment?total_amount=... (pay -> server-side APPROVED), then GET /patients/give_medicine?order_id=&pharmacy_id= (whole-order dispense). Detail delete via GET /patients/delete_pharmacy_sale_order_detail. Confirms payment precedes dispense and dispense is whole-order.

verdicts.planClaim: PharmacySaleOrder (OTC / OUTSIDER, no doctor Rx required) + PharmacySaleOrderDetail exist as the OTC/walk-in sale entities (doc line 18).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy confirms PharmacySaleOrder (header) + PharmacySaleOrderDetail (lines) are the OTC model (domain/PharmacySaleOrder.java:36-100; domain/PharmacySaleOrderDetail.java:29-126). The 'no doctor Rx required' framing is correct (no Visit/Prescription FK on header). CORRECTION 1: The header is tied to a standalone PharmacyCustomer (nullable=false), NOT a clinical Patient/Visit — the doc's 'OUTSIDER' label conflates OTC with the clinical OUTSIDER patient class. Legacy proves these are different screens: getPharmacySaleOrders() returns PharmacySaleOrder PENDING/APPROVED, while /patients/get_pharmacy_outsider_list filters clinical Prescriptions for OUTSIDER patients with PAID/COVERED bills (PatientServiceImpl.java:3019-3026; PatientResource.java:4356-4371). Calling the sale order 'OUTSIDER' risks re-modelling OTC onto the clinical OUTSIDER patient class, which the legacy keeps separate. CORRECTION 2: savePharmacySaleOrder also requires/creates a dummy Patient no='GENERAL' to hang the PatientBill off (PatientServiceImpl.java:3259-3355) — the OTC line bill is NOT customer-billed but billed to a GENERAL pseudo-patient; the design must reproduce or consciously replace this.
verdicts.correction: Describe PharmacySaleOrder as tied to a standalone PharmacyCustomer (walk-in record, separate from Patient), not the clinical OUTSIDER patient class. Note that each line is billed via its own PatientBill against a GENERAL dummy patient, and that OTC and clinical-OUTSIDER prescriptions are different flows in the legacy.

verdicts.planClaim: PharmacySaleOrder/PharmacySaleOrderDetail have an 'identical status lifecycle to Prescription' (doc line 18), i.e. PENDING → ACCEPTED → HELD → VERIFIED → APPROVED → SOLD with terminal REJECTED/CANCELLED (the Prescription lifecycle on line 17).
verdicts.evidence: Directly contradicted by legacy. The OTC header lifecycle is PENDING → APPROVED → ARCHIVED (plus PENDING → CANCELED) — there is NO ACCEPTED, HELD, VERIFIED, SOLD or REJECTED header state, and there IS an ARCHIVED state that Prescription does not have (PatientServiceImpl.java:3019-3026,3341,3079,3138,3199,3217). The legacy finding 'OTC vs Prescription lifecycle — NOT identical' is explicit: Prescription details go NOT-GIVEN → GIVEN with no header state machine, whereas PharmacySaleOrder adds a header state machine and a per-detail payStatus (PatientServiceImpl.java:3341,3079,3138; PatientResource.java:3217-3230,6230). Additionally the PENDING→APPROVED transition is a SIDE EFFECT of paying the linked PatientBill rows via /bills/confirm_bills_payment (PatientBillResource.java:269-393), NOT an explicit approve endpoint, and there is no standalone OTC approve transition (absence confirmed). The two detail status fields are ALSO distinct: a fulfilment status (default 'PENDING', set 'NOT-GIVEN' on save, → 'GIVEN' on dispense) and an independent payStatus ('UNPAID' → 'PAID') (PharmacySaleOrderDetail.java:29-126; PatientServiceImpl.java:3395-3442).
verdicts.correction: State the OTC PharmacySaleOrder lifecycle explicitly and separately: header PENDING → APPROVED → ARCHIVED with terminal CANCELED (PENDING→CANCELED only); per-detail fulfilment status NOT-GIVEN → GIVEN and independent payStatus UNPAID → PAID. PENDING→APPROVED is triggered as a side effect of bill payment (confirm_bills_payment), not by a dedicated approve endpoint. Remove the 'identical to Prescription' claim entirely.

verdicts.planClaim: OTC endpoints: POST /sale-orders, GET .../uid/{uid}, POST .../details, POST .../details/uid/{detailUid}/sell (doc lines 47-50).
verdicts.evidence: These are MODERN REST re-shapings of legacy raw controller methods, acceptable as design improvement IF behaviour is preserved — but the mapping has substantive gaps. Legacy reality: header create = POST /pharmacies/save_pharmacy_sale_order; line create = POST /pharmacies/save_pharmacy_sale_order_detail; dispense = GET /patients/give_medicine?order_id=&pharmacy_id= (Angular sequence at pharmacy-sales-order.component.ts:319,361,721,789,242; PatientResource.java:6212-6255; PharmacyResource.java:308-332). CORRECTION 1 — the 'sell' verb is misplaced: legacy DISPENSING is WHOLE-ORDER (give_medicine takes one order_id and loops ALL details, failing the whole call if any detail isn't NOT-GIVEN), so a per-detail .../details/uid/{detailUid}/sell endpoint does NOT match legacy granularity (PatientResource.java:6212-6255). CORRECTION 2 — the actual 'sell/pay' (per-detail payStatus→PAID and header→APPROVED) happens via billing's confirm_bills_payment, not a pharmacy sell endpoint (PatientBillResource.java:269-393); the doc's endpoint set has NO equivalent of the payment-driven approve step, and NO cancel/archive endpoints (legacy has /pharmacies/pharmacy_sale_orders/cancel and /archive — PatientServiceImpl.java:3068-3117,3121-3173; PharmacyResource.java:268-273). CORRECTION 3 — selling at billing level IS per-detail (each detail has its own PatientBill paid individually), so a per-detail concept is legacy-grounded, but the verb 'sell' on the pharmacy module misrepresents that as a dispense rather than a payment.
verdicts.correction: Either (a) keep dispense as a whole-order operation (POST .../sale-orders/uid/{uid}/dispense looping all details) to match legacy give_medicine, or (b) explicitly document a per-detail dispense as a NEW behaviour via change request. Add explicit cancel and archive transitions (legacy has both, with auto-expiry sweeps at 24h). Clarify that the payment/approve step flows through billing (confirm_bills_payment), and that per-detail granularity belongs to billing/payStatus, not to a pharmacy 'sell' verb.

verdicts.planClaim: 'Stock decremented via UI; OUTSIDER pharmacy sale order created/dispensed' (DoD line 172) — OTC sale dispense decrements stock.
verdicts.evidence: OTC dispense DOES decrement stock, but only at the aggregate level. Legacy give_medicine hard-rejects if order.status != 'APPROVED', sets detail status='GIVEN', hard-rejects if PharmacyMedicine.stock < qty ('insufficient stock' negative-stock gate), decrements PharmacyMedicine.stock by qty, and writes a PharmacyStockCard row (qtyIn=0, qtyOut=issued, balance=newStock) (PatientResource.java:6212-6322). CORRECTION (CRITICAL): OTC dispense does NOT consume FEFO batches — the deductBatch(...) call is COMMENTED OUT at PatientResource.java:6317, so PharmacyMedicineBatch quantities and expiry tracking are NOT reduced for OTC sales, unlike the prescription issue_medicine path which DOES call deductBatch (PatientResource.java:3286-3288). This directly conflicts with the doc's unified FEFO StockBatch/StockMovement model (doc lines 19-21) if it is applied to OTC. Also: dispense relies SOLELY on header==APPROVED; payStatus is never re-checked at dispense (absence confirmed) — the pay enforcement is indirect (header only reaches APPROVED via confirm_bills_payment). The 'via UI' claim matches the Angular flow (pharmacy-sales-order.component.ts:319,361,721,789).
verdicts.correction: State that OTC dispense decrements only the aggregate stock balance (legacy PharmacyMedicine.stock) and writes a stock-card row, and that the legacy DELIBERATELY does NOT reduce FEFO batches for OTC (deductBatch commented out). If the modern design intends to apply FEFO batch consumption to OTC (per the unified StockBatch/StockMovement model), that is a behaviour CHANGE from legacy and must be raised as a change request — it is not a faithful reproduction. Also note the OTC dispense gate is header==APPROVED only; payStatus is not re-checked at dispense.

verdicts.planClaim: Per-line payStatus: UNPAID → PAID gate applies to PharmacySaleOrderDetail the same as Prescription (implied by 'identical lifecycle', doc lines 17-18).
verdicts.evidence: PharmacySaleOrderDetail does carry an independent payStatus 'UNPAID' → 'PAID' (PharmacySaleOrderDetail.java:29-126; set UNPAID on save at PatientServiceImpl.java:3395-3442; flipped to PAID inside confirm_bills_payment at PatientBillResource.java:269-393). So a per-line pay status exists — partially supports the claim. CORRECTION: the legacy OTC gate is NOT a sell()-time payStatus check. Dispense (give_medicine) never reads detail.payStatus; it gates only on header status=='APPROVED' (PatientResource.java:6212-6322). And payStatus flips to PAID via billing, with a notable legacy quirk: confirm_bills_payment marks ALL details of the matched order PAID and the header APPROVED based on the FIRST matching detail, regardless of whether each detail's own bill was actually in the paid list (PatientBillResource.java:269-393). So the OTC pay gate is coarser and payment-driven at the header level, not a per-line sell-time hard gate as the Prescription model (doc §1, line 109) describes.
verdicts.correction: Document the OTC pay gate accurately and separately from the Prescription per-line sell() gate: OTC payStatus UNPAID→PAID is flipped in bulk by billing's confirm_bills_payment, the header→APPROVED is the effective gate for dispense, and dispense does not re-check payStatus. Flag the legacy 'mark all details PAID off first match' quirk for a decision (reproduce vs fix-via-change-request).

verdicts.planClaim: OTC sales emit StockMovement rows carrying (pharmacyUid, itemUid, patientUid, saleType=PRESCRIPTION/OTC, amount) for the pharmacy-sales report (doc line 147, BILL-5).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy OTC dispense writes a PharmacyStockCard row (the legacy equivalent of a StockMovement ledger row) with reference='Issued in sale: id '+detailId on stock decrement (PatientResource.java:6212-6322) — so a per-sale stock ledger entry IS written; modelling it as StockMovement is an acceptable modern re-naming. CORRECTION 1: the doc itself flags BILL-5 as an OPEN GAP ('prior build had no pharmacy-sales revenue breakdown by payment mode') — so the saleType/amount/payment-mode enrichment is NEW SCOPE, not legacy reproduction, and the doc correctly frames it as such; this is fine but should be tagged NEW SCOPE in the change-register. CORRECTION 2: a 'patientUid' on OTC stock movements has weak legacy basis — OTC has NO clinical Patient (only a PharmacyCustomer + a GENERAL dummy patient for billing); the legacy stock card reference is the sale-detail id, not a patient. CORRECTION 3: for OTC the legacy ledger amount/price is the flat Medicine.price * qty with NO insurance/co-pay/discount (PatientServiceImpl.java:3395-3442), so any payment-mode breakdown for OTC must reflect the hardcoded CASH paymentType (PatientServiceImpl.java:3342) — legacy ignores the incoming paymentType for OTC entirely.
verdicts.correction: Tag the saleType/amount/payment-mode breakdown explicitly as NEW SCOPE (BILL-5). For OTC use pharmacyCustomerUid (not patientUid); OTC has no clinical patient. Note OTC pricing is flat Medicine.price*qty with paymentType hardcoded to CASH (insurance/co-pay/discount never applied), so a 'by payment mode' breakdown for legacy OTC is effectively all-CASH unless the hardcode is changed via change request.

newDriftFound: OTC numbering scheme not mentioned: legacy PharmacySaleOrder.no = 'PSO/' + id (save-then-renumber off IDENTITY autoincrement, no fiscal-year segment, no zero-padding) (PatientServiceImpl.java:3352). The doc's §5 document-number-format section (GRN/LPO/RO/TO/RN with {PREFIX}{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} via DocumentNumberService) covers procurement/transfer docs but is SILENT on the sale-order number and customer number. inc-08 must decide: reproduce 'PSO/{id}' or modernize to a sequence (the latter is a change request, like the MAX(id)+1 finding C).

PharmacyCustomer entity and its DUAL numbering scheme not mentioned: walk-in customer record (no, name @NotBlank; gender/phone/address optional) separate from Patient (PharmacyCustomer.java:40-64). TWO different customer-number formats exist by entry path: 'PCST'+id via savePharmacySaleOrder vs 'PCST/'+Year+'/'+id via createPharmacyCustomer (PatientServiceImpl.java:3352,3313,3249). The doc never models PharmacyCustomer at all.

GENERAL dummy-patient dependency not mentioned: savePharmacySaleOrder ensures/creates a Patient no='GENERAL' (active=false, gender 'NONE') to hang each OTC line's PatientBill on (PatientServiceImpl.java:3259-3355). The doc's billing integration (recordClinicalCharge / ServicePrice) does not account for this OTC-to-GENERAL-patient billing path.

OTC line billing math + flat pricing not specified: each PharmacySaleOrderDetail creates its OWN PatientBill, amount = Medicine.price * qty, billItem='Medicine Sale', no insurance/co-pay/discount, paymentType hardcoded CASH ignoring incoming value (PatientServiceImpl.java:3395-3442,3342). Acceptance criteria are needed; the doc has none for OTC pricing.

OTC CANCEL transition + 24h auto-cancel sweep not mentioned: cancel allowed only when status=='PENDING' ('Only pending orders can be canceled'); auto-cancel flips PENDING orders older than 24h to CANCELED ('Autocanceled after expiry') (PatientServiceImpl.java:3068-3117,3207-3229). No endpoint or DoD item covers this.

OTC ARCHIVE transition + 24h auto-archive sweep not mentioned: archive allowed only when status=='APPROVED' and ALL details GIVEN; auto-archive sweeps such orders older than 24h; archive_all runs both archive+cancel sweeps (PatientServiceImpl.java:3121-3205; PharmacyResource.java:268-273). The doc omits ARCHIVED from the OTC lifecycle entirely.

OTC worklist FILTER semantics not mentioned: getPharmacySaleOrders() returns status IN ('PENDING','APPROVED') as a worklist filter (PatientServiceImpl.java:3019-3026). Given the inc-06 'hard gate vs filter' lesson, inc-08 should explicitly classify this as a FILTER (not a gate) for OTC and not conflate it with the prescription settledOnly gate.

OTC detail deletion gate not mentioned: delete allowed only if detail.status IN ('PENDING','NOT-GIVEN') AND its PatientBill.status=='UNPAID'; rejects 'Medicine already given' if a PatientPaymentDetail with status GIVEN exists; cascades delete of PatientInvoiceDetail/empty PatientInvoice/PatientPaymentDetail/PatientBill; a PatientCreditNote branch is effectively dead code (PatientResource.java:6330-6406). No endpoint or rule in the doc.

OTC dispense quantity rule not mentioned: give_medicine forces issued == qty and rejects partial issue ('You can only issue the specified qty') — no partial-quantity dispensing for OTC (PatientResource.java:6212-6322). Should be a stated acceptance criterion.

Architectural reality that ALL OTC logic lives in PatientServiceImpl + raw controller methods (no PharmacyServiceImpl OTC code) — confirmed by the absence search. Not a behaviour to port, but the modern 'pharmacy module owns sale-orders' placement is a deliberate re-modelling that moves OTC logic out of patient/billing into pharmacy; worth flagging as an intentional structural deviation since billing's confirm_bills_payment currently owns the APPROVE side effect (PatientBillResource.java:269-393) — splitting that across the pharmacy/billing module boundary is non-trivial.

extract.lane: FEFO stock model, batches, stock cards, conversion coefficients, negative-stock guard
extract.summary: Pharmacy stock is tracked TWO independent ways that are NOT kept in sync by all code paths: (1) an aggregate running balance in PharmacyMedicine.stock (a plain double field, default 0), and (2) per-lot PharmacyMedicineBatch rows (no/manufacturedDate/expiryDate/qty double). There is NO StockBalance entity and NO derived balance — PharmacyMedicine.stock is the authoritative aggregate. PharmacyStockCard is an append-only ledger (qtyIn/qtyOut/balance doubles, free-text reference) where the per-row balance is the snapshot of PharmacyMedicine.stock AFTER the movement; it is written by every stock-mutating path. FEFO is implemented in-memory via duplicated getEarlierBatch()/deductBatch() helpers (one copy in PatientResource for prescriptions, one in PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl for transfers): the repository query findAllByPharmacyAndMedicineAndQtyGreaterThan returns UNORDERED rows, then code picks the earliest non-null expiryDate; if NO batch in the set has an expiry it falls back to lowest id (insertion order). There is NO database locking of any kind (no @Lock / PESSIMISTIC / SELECT FOR UPDATE anywhere in com.orbix.api) and the negative-stock guard is a read-then-check-then-write on PharmacyMedicine.stock with no concurrency protection, so it is race-prone. Critically, batch decrement is INCONSISTENT: prescription issue and pharmacy-to-pharmacy transfer DO decrement PharmacyMedicineBatch.qty, but the pharmacy sale-order "give_medicine" path has its deductBatch call COMMENTED OUT, and the only active code that ever CREATES PharmacyMedicineBatch rows is StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.approveReceivingNote — pharmacy-to-pharmacy receiving never re-creates batches at the receiving pharmacy (also commented out).

findings.behaviour: Pharmacy stock balance is a single plain double field 'stock' on PharmacyMedicine (default 0), keyed by (pharmacy_id, medicine_id) which are non-updatable. There is no separate StockBalance entity and the balance is NOT derived from batches or stock cards — it is mutated directly.

PharmacyMedicineBatch holds: id, no (@NotBlank), manufacturedDate (LocalDate, nullable), expiryDate (LocalDate, nullable), qty (double, default 0), pharmacy, medicine. There are NO audit columns (no createdBy/createdOn/createdAt) and NO @Version optimistic-lock column.

PharmacyStockCard is append-only: qtyIn (double=0), qtyOut (double=0), balance (double=0), reference (free String), dateTime, medicine, pharmacy, createdBy/createdOn/createdAt. 'balance' is stored per row (a snapshot of PharmacyMedicine.stock after the movement) — it is NOT recomputed from prior rows. There is no movement-type enum; the movement type is conveyed only by the free-text 'reference' string.
Batches are fetched via derived query findAllByPharmacyAndMedicineAndQtyGreaterThan(pharmacy, medicine, 0) with NO OrderBy clause; FEFO ordering is done entirely in application memory afterward.

Iterates batches: if ANY batch has non-null expiryDate, it filters to only batches with non-null expiry and selects the one with the EARLIEST expiryDate (strict isAfter comparison; first-seen wins on ties). If NO batch in the list has an expiry date at all, it falls back to selecting the LOWEST id (insertion/FIFO order). Batches that have a null expiry are silently EXCLUDED from selection whenever at least one batch in the set has an expiry — meaning null-expiry stock can be stranded and never dispensed.

Recursive: pick earliest batch, remove it from the working list; if qty <= batch.qty set batch.qty = batch.qty - qty (save) and write a PrescriptionBatch trace row for qty; else set batch.qty=0 (save), remove, and recurse with the remainder. ALL of this is wrapped in a try/catch(Exception e){//do nothing} — so if batch stock is insufficient (getEarlierBatch returns null -> NPE) the exception is SWALLOWED silently and batch decrement just stops, leaving PharmacyMedicine.stock already decremented (the two stores diverge).
findings.cite: api/PatientResource.java:3296-3336; service/PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:285-304 (transfer variant: no PrescriptionBatch trace, otherwise identical, same empty catch)

findings.behaviour: There is no pessimistic or optimistic locking anywhere: grep for @Lock / LockModeType / PESSIMISTIC / SELECT FOR UPDATE across com.orbix.api returns zero matches. PharmacyMedicineBatch has no @Version. The decrement is a plain read-modify-write on PharmacyMedicine.stock and on batch.qty under the controller/service @Transactional only.
findings.cite: NOT FOUND — searched all of D:/My_Works/HMS/ZANAHMIS-2-feature/Zana-HMIS-API/api/api/src/main/java/com/orbix/api for @Lock|LockModeType|PESSIMISTIC|setLockMode|for update (0 matches); domain/PharmacyMedicineBatch.java + PharmacyMedicine.java have no @Version

findings.topic: Negative-stock guard (prescription issue) — position relative to mutation
findings.behaviour: In /patients/issue_medicine: guard reads pharmacyMedicine.getStock() and rejects (InvalidOperationException 'Can not issue, insufficient stock') if stock < prescription.getQty(). Guard is checked BEFORE computing newStock and saving. Guard compares against getQty() (the prescribed qty), NOT against getIssued(); and issue requires issued == qty (line 3224) and issued >= balance and >0 (3221). No lock around the read/check/write, so concurrent issues can both pass the guard and drive stock negative.

findings.topic: Negative-stock guard (sale order give) — and batch decrement DISABLED
findings.behaviour: In /patients/give_medicine: same hard guard pharmacyMedicine.getStock() < detail.getQty() throws InvalidOperationException before decrementing PharmacyMedicine.stock and writing a stock card. BUT the FEFO batch decrement is COMMENTED OUT: batches are fetched (line 6315) then 'deductBatch(...)' is commented at line 6317 with a '//review this section later' note (6313). Result: cash sales reduce aggregate PharmacyMedicine.stock and write a stock card, but NEVER reduce PharmacyMedicineBatch.qty.
findings.cite: api/PatientResource.java:6272-6273 (guard), 6276-6293 (stock + stock card), 6313-6317 (batch fetch then commented deductBatch)

findings.behaviour: On every outbound movement the PharmacyStockCard row is created and saved AFTER PharmacyMedicine.stock is updated and BEFORE batch deduction, with balance set to the post-movement newStock. Prescription issue: stock card 'Issued in prescription: id <id>' (qtyOut=issued). Sale: 'Issued in sale: id <id>'. Pharmacy-to-pharmacy issue: 'Goods transfered to Pharmacy PTPO# <no>'. All run inside the controller's class-level @Transactional (PatientResource) or the service @Transactional (transfer).
findings.cite: api/PatientResource.java:3252-3264 and 6281-6293; service/PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:256-268; class @Transactional at api/PharmacyResource.java:73 / PatientResource (class-level) / PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:54

findings.behaviour: The ONLY non-commented code that constructs and saves PharmacyMedicineBatch is StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.approveReceivingNote: for each StoreToPharmacyBatch it copies no, medicine, manufacturedDate, expiryDate, qty=batch.getPharmacySKUQty(), pharmacy=receiveNote.getPharmacy(). It also increments PharmacyMedicine.stock by detail.getReceivedPharmacySKUQty() and writes an inbound stock card 'Medicine received # <no>'. Three other 'new PharmacyMedicineBatch()' occurrences are inside commented-out blocks.

findings.topic: Pharmacy-to-pharmacy receiving does NOT create batches
findings.behaviour: PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.approveReceivingNote increments PharmacyMedicine.stock (originalStock + receivedQty) and writes an inbound stock card, but the loop that would create PharmacyMedicineBatch rows at the receiving pharmacy is entirely commented out. So inter-pharmacy transfers add to aggregate stock but add ZERO batches at the destination — destination batch table is permanently out of sync for transferred stock.

ItemMedicineCoefficient stores itemQty, medicineQty and coefficient (all double). On save coefficient = medicineQty / itemQty (plain double division, no rounding/scale). Zero or negative itemQty/medicineQty are rejected. Unique per (item, medicine).

findings.topic: Where coefficient is multiplied (store SKU -> pharmacy SKU)
findings.behaviour: Conversion is applied as pharmacySKUQty = storeSKUQty * coefficient with NO rounding (raw double). Applied (a) per batch when adding a store-to-pharmacy transfer batch: batch.setPharmacySKUQty(batch.getStoreSKUQty() * coe.getCoefficient()); (b) on detail save: transferedPharmacySKUQty = transferedStoreSKUQty * coefficient. The resulting pharmacySKUQty flows into PharmacyMedicineBatch.qty and PharmacyMedicine.stock at RN approval. If no coefficient exists the operation hard-fails ('Conversion coefficient ... not found').

Document numbers are application-level MAX(id)+1, NOT a DB sequence: requestTransferOrderNo/requestReceivingNoteNo do id = repo.getLastId()+1 (getLastId = JPQL SELECT MAX(s.id)), default 1 on exception, then Formater.formatWithCurrentDate(prefix,id) => prefix + yyyyMMdd + '-' + id. Prefixes: 'SPT' (pharmacy-to-pharmacy TO), 'PPRN' (pharmacy-to-pharmacy RN). The numeric suffix is the raw id with NO zero-padding and NO fiscal-year reset (id is a global max, only the embedded date changes daily). This is race-prone (two concurrent requests get the same MAX+1).

findings.topic: Manual stock update path (no batch effect)
findings.behaviour: POST /pharmacies/update_stock (auth MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE) sets PharmacyMedicine.stock = posted value absolutely (overwrite, not delta), rejects negative, requires medicine active, and writes a stock card 'Stock Update' with qtyIn = new stock and balance = new stock. It does NOT touch PharmacyMedicineBatch at all — manual stock edits desync batches from aggregate stock.

When a Pharmacy is first saved, a PharmacyMedicine row (stock=0) and an opening PharmacyStockCard ('Opening stock, pharmacy registration', qtyIn=0, balance=0) are created for EVERY Medicine in the catalog. No batches created.

verdicts.planClaim: StockBatch entity: FEFO batch per (pharmacyUid,itemUid) with batchNo, expiryDate nullable, receivedQty/remainingQty NUMERIC(19,6).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy DOES have a per-lot batch entity: PharmacyMedicineBatch (domain/PharmacyMedicineBatch.java:35-53) with fields no (@NotBlank — maps to batchNo), manufacturedDate (LocalDate nullable), expiryDate (LocalDate nullable), qty (double=0), keyed by pharmacy_id + medicine_id (i.e. per pharmacy+item). So StockBatch is a legitimate modern RE-MODELLING of PharmacyMedicineBatch and the (pharmacyUid,itemUid) key + nullable expiryDate are faithful. CORRECTIONS: (1) Legacy has a SINGLE qty field, not separate receivedQty + remainingQty — the legacy batch is decremented in place (deductBatch sets batch.qty = batch.qty - qty, PatientResource.java:3296-3336). Splitting into receivedQty/remainingQty is a new derived split, acceptable only if it preserves the in-place-decrement balance, but it is NOT a 1:1 legacy field and must be flagged. (2) qty is a plain double in legacy; double->BigDecimal NUMERIC(19,6) is the pre-approved data-type change (per engagement memory), so the type itself is fine but should be recorded as a data-type CR, not presented as legacy fact. (3) Legacy batch has NO audit columns and NO @Version — the modern entity adding AuditableEntity is net-new (tamper-evident audit is the approved net-new audit trail, not a ported behaviour).

verdicts.planClaim: StockBalance entity: running balance per (pharmacyUid,itemUid); PESSIMISTIC_WRITE lock on decrement.
verdicts.evidence: There is NO StockBalance entity in legacy. The aggregate balance is a single plain double field PharmacyMedicine.stock (domain/PharmacyMedicine.java:38) keyed by (pharmacy_id, medicine_id), mutated directly by read-modify-write — it is the authoritative aggregate, NOT derived from batches or stock cards. Promoting that field into a separate StockBalance entity is an acceptable modern re-modelling ONLY of the balance storage. But the claim bundles 'PESSIMISTIC_WRITE on decrement' as if reproducing legacy behaviour, and that CONTRADICTS the legacy: a grep for @Lock|LockModeType|PESSIMISTIC|setLockMode|'for update' across all of com.orbix.api returns 0 matches (confirmed independently), PharmacyMedicine has no @Version, and the decrement is an unlocked read-then-check-then-write under only the controller @Transactional (PatientResource.java:3243-3250). The legacy is explicitly RACE-PRONE with zero concurrency protection. Pessimistic locking is therefore NEW behaviour, not legacy reproduction.
verdicts.correction: Split into two statements: (a) StockBalance is a modern re-modelling of the legacy PharmacyMedicine.stock double field (ACCURATE re-model). (b) PESSIMISTIC_WRITE locking on decrement is a NET-NEW concurrency-correctness improvement with NO legacy basis (legacy has none and is race-prone). It must be logged in the change-request register as an intentional deviation (defensible safety improvement), not described as exact-process reproduction. Note also legacy keys on medicine_id, and stock rows are pre-created for EVERY medicine at pharmacy registration with stock=0 (PharmacyServiceImpl.java:67-91) — the modern model should preserve or consciously drop that eager pre-creation.

verdicts.planClaim: StockMovement append-only ledger (RECEIPT, DISPENSE, TRANSFER_OUT, TRANSFER_IN, ADJUSTMENT, WASTAGE) carrying runningBalance.
verdicts.evidence: Legacy DOES have an append-only ledger: PharmacyStockCard (domain/PharmacyStockCard.java:37-57) with qtyIn/qtyOut/balance (doubles) plus a free-text 'reference' string, written by every stock-mutating path with balance = snapshot of PharmacyMedicine.stock AFTER the movement (so 'runningBalance' is faithful — it is a stored post-movement snapshot, NOT recomputed). StockMovement is a legitimate re-model of PharmacyStockCard. CORRECTIONS: (1) The movement-TYPE ENUM is net-new — legacy has NO type column; movement type is conveyed only by free-text reference strings ('Issued in prescription: id <id>', 'Issued in sale: id <id>', 'Goods transfered to Pharmacy PTPO# <no>', 'Medicine received # <no>', 'Stock Update', 'Opening stock, pharmacy registration'). Introducing a typed enum is an improvement but must map exactly onto those legacy reference strings or it loses parity for the stock-card report. (2) Specific enum values need a legacy-fidelity check: there is no legacy WASTAGE path at all (no wastage/disposal code found in this lane); 'ADJUSTMENT' corresponds to the manual /pharmacies/update_stock overwrite (PharmacyResource.java:199-231); RECEIPT covers both StoreToPharmacy RN and PharmacyToPharmacy RN inbound. WASTAGE should be flagged as net-new scope unless a legacy disposal path is found. (3) Legacy DISPENSE and TRANSFER do NOT reliably move batches (see other findings) — if StockMovement is intended to drive batch remainingQty, that is stricter than legacy.

verdicts.planClaim: lockFefoForDispense acquires SELECT FOR UPDATE ordered expiry_date ASC NULLS LAST; negative-stock guard AFTER lock.
verdicts.evidence: This contradicts legacy on every clause. (1) NO SELECT FOR UPDATE / locking exists anywhere in com.orbix.api (0 grep matches, confirmed); there is no lockFefoForDispense or equivalent. (2) Ordering: the legacy repository query findAllByPharmacyAndMedicineAndQtyGreaterThan (PharmacyMedicineBatchRepository.java:26-27) has NO OrderBy — it returns UNORDERED rows and FEFO is computed in-memory in getEarlierBatch (PatientResource.java:3338-3376). (3) The legacy FEFO semantics are NOT 'expiry ASC NULLS LAST': getEarlierBatch picks the earliest NON-NULL expiry and, when ANY batch has an expiry, SILENTLY EXCLUDES null-expiry batches (they can be stranded forever); only when NO batch has an expiry does it fall back to lowest id (FIFO). 'NULLS LAST' would still allow a null-expiry batch to be dispensed once non-null ones are exhausted, which legacy never does within a single selection pass — so the ordering semantics DRIFT. (4) Guard ordering: legacy negative-stock guard is checked BEFORE mutation, on PharmacyMedicine.stock, with NO lock (PatientResource.java:3243-3250); 'guard AFTER lock is held' describes a locked critical section that does not exist in legacy.
verdicts.correction: State plainly: legacy has NO database locking and NO SQL-level FEFO ordering; FEFO is an in-memory selection over an unordered query that EXCLUDES null-expiry lots whenever any dated lot exists, falling back to lowest-id FIFO only when ALL lots are undated. The lock + 'expiry ASC NULLS LAST' SQL ordering + 'guard after lock' are all net-new design (acceptable as a correctness/concurrency improvement) and must be CR-logged. Crucially, decide and document whether the modern build preserves the legacy null-expiry-exclusion quirk or fixes it (NULLS LAST changes which lot dispenses) — this is a behaviour change, not a like-for-like port.

verdicts.planClaim: ItemMedicineCoefficient NUMERIC(19,6); transferQty.multiply(coefficient) full BigDecimal precision, no intermediate rounding; 1/3 * 9 = 3.000000.
verdicts.evidence: Legacy ItemMedicineCoefficient exists (domain/ItemMedicineCoefficient.java:35-53) with itemQty, medicineQty, coefficient — all plain DOUBLE (line 40), coefficient = medicineQty/itemQty plain double division with NO rounding/scale (ConversionCoefficientResource.java:87-101), unique per (item,medicine), zero/negative rejected. Conversion is applied as pharmacySKUQty = storeSKUQty * coefficient with NO rounding (raw double) at InternalOrderResource.java:595 and StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:417-424 (hard-fail if no coefficient). So 'no intermediate rounding' faithfully preserves the legacy NO-rounding behaviour, and double->BigDecimal NUMERIC(19,6) is the pre-approved data-type change. CORRECTIONS: (1) NUMERIC(19,6) is the new type, not legacy (legacy is double) — record as the pre-approved data-type CR. (2) The '1/3 * 9 = 3.000000' parity assertion is MISLEADING and likely a wrong test oracle: legacy stores coefficient as a finite double = medicineQty/itemQty (1.0/3.0 = 0.333333333...), and a NUMERIC(19,6) coefficient would store 0.333333, so 0.333333 * 9 = 2.999997, NOT 3.000000. Neither legacy double math nor a 6-dp stored coefficient yields exactly 3. To get 3.000000 you would have to store the coefficient as an unevaluated rational or carry >6 dp — which is NOT how legacy works (it evaluates and stores the quotient). The parity example must be corrected: either pick a coefficient that is exact at 6 dp, or define the expected value as the actual NUMERIC(19,6) round-trip result (2.999997), or it will fail/contradict legacy arithmetic. (3) Direction note: legacy multiplies storeSKU * coefficient where coefficient = medicineQty/itemQty; confirm transferQty in the doc is the store-side qty so the multiply direction matches legacy.

newDriftFound: Two independent, frequently-DESYNCED stock stores: aggregate PharmacyMedicine.stock (authoritative double) AND per-lot PharmacyMedicineBatch.qty are NOT kept in sync by all paths. The doc presents StockBalance + StockBatch as one coherent FEFO model and never acknowledges this divergence. inc-08 must consciously DECIDE to make balance := sum(batch.remainingQty) (a behaviour change / cleanup that needs a CR) rather than silently assuming legacy already enforced it.

Cash pharmacy sale-order ('give_medicine') deductBatch is COMMENTED OUT (PatientResource.java:6313-6317): OTC/cash sales decrement aggregate PharmacyMedicine.stock and write a stock card but NEVER decrement PharmacyMedicineBatch.qty. The doc's claim that sale-orders 'pull stock from a FEFO batch ledger' (Goal, line 5; OTC sell endpoints) is therefore NOT legacy behaviour for the dispense->batch link — it is net-new and must be flagged, not assumed.

Pharmacy-to-pharmacy RECEIVING does NOT create batches at the destination (PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:221-232 commented out): inter-pharmacy transfers add to aggregate stock + write a stock card but add ZERO batches at the destination, so the destination batch table is permanently out of sync. The doc's p2p RN 'triggers stock cards on both sides' + TRANSFER_IN to StockBatch implies destination batches ARE created — that is net-new (or a deliberate fix of a legacy bug) and must be CR-flagged.

deductBatch wraps its entire body in try/catch(Exception){ // do nothing } (PatientResource.java:3296-3336): on insufficient batch stock (getEarlierBatch returns null -> NPE) the exception is SWALLOWED silently, batch decrement stops, and PharmacyMedicine.stock is left already decremented. The doc's 422 INSUFFICIENT_STOCK hard-fail is therefore a behaviour CHANGE from the legacy silent-swallow; defensible but must be logged, and the parity harness must not expect an error where legacy emits none.

Manual stock-update path: POST /pharmacies/update_stock (auth MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE) OVERWRITES PharmacyMedicine.stock absolutely (not delta), rejects negative, writes a 'Stock Update' card with qtyIn = new stock and balance = new stock, and does NOT touch batches (PharmacyResource.java:199-231). The doc lists ADJUSTMENT as a StockMovement type but never specifies this overwrite (not delta) semantics nor the batch-desync — inc-08 must reproduce the overwrite semantics or CR the change.

Opening-stock behaviour: creating a Pharmacy auto-creates a PharmacyMedicine (stock=0) AND an opening PharmacyStockCard ('Opening stock, pharmacy registration', qtyIn=0, balance=0) for EVERY Medicine in the catalog (PharmacyServiceImpl.java:67-91). The doc's stock model is silent on this eager per-medicine row+card creation; inc-08 must decide to reproduce or drop it (lazy creation would be a behaviour change).
