FEFO logic is DUPLICATED, not centralized: getEarlierBatch/deductBatch exist verbatim in two places (api/PatientResource.java and service/PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java) with subtle divergence (prescription copy writes a PrescriptionBatch trace row; transfer copy does not). The doc's single StockBatchRepository.lockFefoForDispense is a consolidation — fine as design, but it erases the legacy per-path trace-row difference (PrescriptionBatch) which the doc never mentions.

PrescriptionBatch trace rows: the legacy prescription-issue deductBatch writes a PrescriptionBatch row recording which lot/qty satisfied each prescription line (PatientResource.java:3296-3336). The doc has no equivalent lot-traceability entity for dispensing; if regulatory lot-traceability is required this is a legacy capability at risk of being dropped silently.

Negative-stock guard compares against getQty() (prescribed qty), and issue additionally requires issued == qty and issued >= balance and > 0 (PatientResource.java:3217-3225, 3243-3250). The doc's pay-gate and FEFO sections do not capture these prescription-issue preconditions (issued must equal prescribed qty) — story-level acceptance criteria need them.

Document-number prefixes in legacy are 'SPT' for BOTH pharmacy-to-pharmacy TO and (per other lanes) store-to-pharmacy, generated via app-level MAX(id)+1 with Formater.formatWithCurrentDate(prefix,id) => prefix+yyyyMMdd+'-'+id, NO zero-padding, NO fiscal reset (PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:421-430). The doc correctly flags the SPT collision and the MAX(id)+1 race as fixes — these are acknowledged CRs, so NOT drift, but the doc should note the suffix is the raw global id (no zero-pad) so the new {seq} format is itself a cosmetic change reviewers may flag against legacy output.

extract.summary: The pharmacy-to-pharmacy transfer is a three-document chain: a Requisition Order (RO, PharmacyToPharmacyRO) raised by the REQUESTING pharmacy, a Transfer Order (TO, PharmacyToPharmacyTO) created by the DELIVERING pharmacy from a SUBMITTED RO, and a Receiving Note (RN, PharmacyToPharmacyRN) created back at the requesting pharmacy once goods are issued. Stock movement is split across two documents and NOT on RN-complete alone: the SOURCE/delivering pharmacy stock deduction + source stock card row + source FEFO batch deduction happen on TO.issue() at the APPROVED->GOODS-ISSUED transition (PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.issue, lines 215-283). The DESTINATION/requesting pharmacy stock increment + destination stock card row happen on RN approve_receipt, via PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.approveReceivingNote (lines 192-219), invoked by InternalOrderResource.approveReceiptPharmacyToPharmacyRN (lines 1360-1401). CRITICAL ABSENCE: the destination pharmacy NEVER gets PharmacyMedicineBatch rows created — the batch-creation block in approveReceivingNote is fully commented out (lines 221-232), so received stock has no batch/expiry record at the destination. Document number prefixes are PPR (RO), SPT (TO — legacy prefix, NOT PPTO), and PPRN (RN); all use MAX(id)+1 with format PREFIX+yyyyMMdd+"-"+rawId and NO zero-padding.

findings.behaviour: PharmacyToPharmacyRO statuses observed: PENDING (set on save of new RO), VERIFIED, APPROVED, SUBMITTED, RETURNED, REJECTED, IN-PROCESS (set when TO is created), GOODS-ISSUED (set when TO goods issued), COMPLETED (set when RN receipt approved). Each is a String literal on the entity's 'status' field.

save: new RO forced to PENDING; edit allowed only if existing status==PENDING else InvalidOperationException (only validUntil editable). verify: requires status==PENDING and non-empty details -> VERIFIED. approve: requires status==VERIFIED and non-empty details -> APPROVED. submit: requires status==APPROVED and non-empty details -> SUBMITTED. _return: requires status==SUBMITTED -> RETURNED. reject: requires status==SUBMITTED -> REJECTED. All guards are HARD service-layer rejects (throw InvalidOperationException), not worklist filters. verify/approve/submit additionally guard that the RO belongs to the requesting pharmacy (id equality).

TO is created by PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.createOrder from an RO. Guard: RO.status must == 'SUBMITTED' else InvalidOperationException 'Can only create transfer order for submitted requests'. On creation the RO is flipped to 'IN-PROCESS'. TO header copies requesting+delivering pharmacy from RO; TO details copy medicine+orderedQty from each RO detail with transferedQty unset (0) and status PENDING. If a TO already exists for the RO (findByPharmacyToPharmacyRO non-empty) it is returned as-is (idempotent, no new creation).
findings.cite: PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:72-119 (esp. 80-86 SUBMITTED guard + IN-PROCESS flip, 101-114 detail copy, 117-118 idempotent return)

findings.behaviour: RO._return and RO.reject both hard-require status=='SUBMITTED'. Once a TO has been created the RO is already 'IN-PROCESS', so the RO can no longer be returned or rejected. No transition exists out of IN-PROCESS except via TO.issue (->GOODS-ISSUED) or RN approve (->COMPLETED). No code path restores SUBMITTED.

PharmacyToPharmacyTO statuses: PENDING (at createOrder), VERIFIED, APPROVED, GOODS-ISSUED, COMPLETED. verify: requires PENDING -> VERIFIED. approve: requires VERIFIED -> APPROVED. issue: requires APPROVED -> GOODS-ISSUED (and also flips parent RO to GOODS-ISSUED). COMPLETED set later by RN approve controller. All guards HARD service-layer rejects. NOTE: TO verify/approve do NOT check that the TO belongs to the delivering pharmacy (no ownership guard, unlike RO).

findings.topic: SOURCE goods-issue stock write — EXACT location
findings.behaviour: Source/delivering-pharmacy stock is deducted in TO.issue() AT THE GOODS-ISSUED TRANSITION (status set to GOODS-ISSUED at line 224, then stock loop). For each TODetail: loads PharmacyMedicine for (deliveringPharmacy, medicine); HARD reject if stock < transferedQty ('Can not issue goods. Stock qty is less than transfer qty'); newStock = stock - transferedQty; saves PharmacyMedicine; then writes ONE PharmacyStockCard row (qtyIn=0, qtyOut=transferedQty, balance=newStock, reference='Goods transfered to Pharmacy PTPO# '+order.getNo()); then calls deductBatch on source batches.
findings.cite: PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:242-280 (negative-stock gate 247-249, PharmacyMedicine save 253, PharmacyStockCard save 268, batch deduct 277)

findings.behaviour: deductBatch consumes PharmacyMedicineBatch rows of the delivering pharmacy. getEarlierBatch selects earliest expiryDate among batches that HAVE an expiryDate (FEFO). If NO batch has an expiry, it falls back to lowest id (FIFO-by-id). Source batch query is findAllByPharmacyAndMedicineAndQtyGreaterThan(deliveringPharmacy, medicine, 0) so only positive-qty batches considered. deductBatch recurses across batches; on any exception it silently swallows ('//do nothing') — so a shortfall beyond available batch qty does NOT throw here (the only hard stock gate is the PharmacyMedicine.stock check at 247).
findings.cite: PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:275 (query),285-304 (deductBatch incl. silent catch 300-302),306-344 (getEarlierBatch FEFO then id fallback)

findings.topic: transferedQty origin (NOT in TO detail save)
findings.behaviour: TODetail.transferedQty is accumulated when batches are added to a PENDING TO via controller endpoint /pharmacy_to_pharmacy_t_os/add_batch: tod.transferedQty += batch.qty, with HARD guards: TODetail.status must be PENDING; batch.qty>0; medicine SKU must match the detail's medicine; cumulative transferedQty must not exceed orderedQty ('Can not transfer more than ordered qty'). The PharmacyToPharmacyBatch row (with no/manufacturedDate/expiryDate/qty) is persisted HERE, linked to the TODetail. delete_batch reverses the qty and removes the medicine if transferedQty hits 0.
findings.cite: InternalOrderResource.java:1219-1268 (add_batch: PENDING guard 1229-1231, qty>0 1248-1250, accumulate 1255, over-order guard 1258-1260, batch save 1266); 1271-1304 (delete_batch)

findings.behaviour: PharmacyToPharmacyBatch is created ONLY at TO-PENDING via add_batch (linked to PharmacyToPharmacyTODetail, table pharmacy_to_pharmacy_batches). It is RE-LINKED to the RN detail (pharmacyToPharmacyRNDetail FK) during RN.createReceivingNote (the only place the RN-detail FK is set). These batches are display/traceability records on the transfer; they are NOT promoted into destination PharmacyMedicineBatch rows anywhere.
findings.cite: InternalOrderResource.java:1262-1266 (TO-detail link at create); PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:126-129 (re-link to RN detail); PharmacyToPharmacyBatch.java:44-52 (dual FK)

findings.behaviour: RN created by PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.createReceivingNote from an RO. Looks up the TO via findByPharmacyToPharmacyRO; HARD reject if no TO ('Corresponding transfer order not found'); HARD reject if TO.status != 'GOODS-ISSUED' ('Could not create/process GRN. Goods not issued'). Idempotent: if RN already exists for the TO it is returned as-is. New RN header status=PENDING; details copy medicine, orderedQty, and receivedQty = TODetail.transferedQty (auto-filled from transfer, NOT re-entered). Each RN detail status=PENDING. Controller also guards requesting-pharmacy ownership before calling service.
findings.cite: PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:83-136 (TO lookup 85-88, GOODS-ISSUED guard 90-92, idempotent 134-135, receivedQty=transferedQty 117); InternalOrderResource.java:1334-1357 (ownership guard 1352-1354)

findings.topic: RN state machine - statuses (incomplete vs entity)
findings.behaviour: RN entity declares verified/approved audit fields, but the RN service has NO verify() or approve() method. RN status is only ever set to PENDING (createReceivingNote, line 104) and COMPLETED (controller approve_receipt, line 1392). VERIFIED/APPROVED are never set for RN. So RN is a TWO-state machine: PENDING -> COMPLETED.
findings.cite: PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:104 (PENDING), grep confirmed no VERIFIED/APPROVED set in service; InternalOrderResource.java:1392 (COMPLETED); PharmacyToPharmacyRN.java:77-87 (unused verified/approved fields)

findings.topic: DESTINATION stock write — EXACT location (RN approve)
findings.behaviour: Destination/requesting-pharmacy stock is incremented in PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.approveReceivingNote. For each RN detail: loads PharmacyMedicine for (requestingPharmacy, medicine); newStock = stock + receivedQty; saves PharmacyMedicine; if receivedQty>0 writes ONE PharmacyStockCard row (qtyIn=receivedQty, qtyOut=0, balance=newStock, reference='Medicine received # '+receiveNote.getNo()). This method is called by controller approve_receipt AFTER the controller has already set RN/TO/RO to COMPLETED. The method itself does NOT change any status.
findings.cite: PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:192-219 (PharmacyMedicine save 202, PharmacyStockCard save 218); InternalOrderResource.java:1392-1400 (status flips then service call)

findings.behaviour: approve_receipt (controller) HARD-requires RN.status=='PENDING' else 'Could not receive. Not a pending GRN'. Then sets parent RO -> COMPLETED, parent TO -> COMPLETED, RN -> COMPLETED (all saved), THEN calls approveReceivingNote for the stock posting. No re-fetch guard prevents double-posting beyond the PENDING check (idempotency relies solely on RN no longer being PENDING after first call).
findings.cite: InternalOrderResource.java:1360-1401 (PENDING guard 1372-1374, RO COMPLETED 1378-1381, TO COMPLETED 1386-1389, RN COMPLETED 1392-1395)

findings.behaviour: SOURCE (delivering pharmacy): stock-- + stock-card-out + batch FEFO deduction happen on TO.issue() (APPROVED->GOODS-ISSUED). DESTINATION (requesting pharmacy): stock++ + stock-card-in happen on RN approve_receipt (PENDING->COMPLETED). They are on DIFFERENT documents and DIFFERENT transactions. There is a window where goods are deducted from source but not yet added to destination (TO GOODS-ISSUED but RN not yet approved).

RO no = Formater.formatWithCurrentDate('PPR', MAX(id)+1). TO no = Formater.formatWithCurrentDate('SPT', MAX(id)+1) — legacy prefix is SPT, NOT PPTO. RN no = Formater.formatWithCurrentDate('PPRN', MAX(id)+1). All use repository getLastId()=SELECT MAX(id), wrapped in try/catch defaulting id=1L on any exception (e.g. empty table -> null). Format = prefix + yyyyMMdd + '-' + rawId with NO zero-padding. Numbers are application-generated (MAX+1), not DB sequences; race-prone (no locking).

On new RO save: requestingPharmacy and deliveringPharmacy must exist (NotFoundException); requestingPharmacy.code must match the loaded record ('Invalid Pharmacy'); requesting and delivering pharmacy must differ ('Order can not be placed in the same pharmacy' — uses == on boxed Long ids). RO.no is unique (DB constraint) and validUntil is @NotNull on entity. RODetail save rejects duplicate medicine per RO ('Duplicates are not allowed'), sets receivedQty=0, status=PENDING.
findings.cite: PharmacyToPharmacyROServiceImpl.java:60-87 (existence 62-69, code match 70-72, same-pharmacy 74-76),299-313 (duplicate guard 300-304); PharmacyToPharmacyRO.java:48-53 (unique no, NotNull validUntil)

findings.behaviour: Every RO/TO/RN/batch endpoint in InternalOrderResource for this lane is gated by @PreAuthorize("hasAnyAuthority('PHARMACY_ORDER-ALL')") — a single coarse privilege. There is NO separation of duty between verify/approve/issue/receive; one privilege authorizes all transitions.
findings.cite: InternalOrderResource.java:1220 (add_batch),1272 (delete_batch),1335 (RN create),1361 (RN approve_receipt) and the RO/TO mapping block 780-1151

absences.expected: Destination pharmacy PharmacyMedicineBatch rows created on receipt (so received medicine carries batch no / manufactured / expiry at the requesting pharmacy)
absences.searched: PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.approveReceivingNote — the batch-creation loop that would build new PharmacyMedicineBatch from PharmacyToPharmacyBatch is FULLY COMMENTED OUT (PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:221-232). Grep for 'new PharmacyMedicineBatch' across the three services returned none. Therefore received stock increments PharmacyMedicine.stock but creates NO destination batch/expiry record — destination batch tracking is lost.

absences.expected: RN verify and approve transitions (entity has verified/approved audit fields)
absences.searched: PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl has no verify()/approve() method; grep for setStatus/VERIFIED/APPROVED in the RN service shows only PENDING set. RN goes PENDING->COMPLETED only. The verified*/approved* columns on PharmacyToPharmacyRN are never populated.

absences.expected: TO prefix 'PPTO'
absences.searched: PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.requestTransferOrderNo uses Formater.formatWithCurrentDate('SPT', id) (line 428). The TO prefix is the legacy 'SPT', not 'PPTO'. (RO=PPR, RN=PPRN as expected; only TO diverges.)

absences.expected: Zero-padded sequence numbers in document numbers
absences.searched: Formater.formatWithCurrentDate (Formater.java:14-17) concatenates the raw id.toString() with no padding; the formatNine/Six/Three/Five padding helpers are NOT used by any of the three requestXxxNo methods. So numbers are e.g. PPR20260605-7, not PPR20260605-000000007.

absences.expected: Ownership / delivering-pharmacy guard on TO verify/approve/issue
absences.searched: PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.verify/approve/issue (lines 171-283) check only status, never that the TO belongs to the acting (delivering) pharmacy — unlike the RO service which checks requestingPharmacy id equality. No pharmacy-scoping guard found.

absences.expected: Negative-stock / over-issue hard gate on the batch level at source
absences.searched: The only hard stock gate at issue is PharmacyMedicine.stock < transferedQty (PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:247-249). deductBatch (285-304) swallows all exceptions silently ('//do nothing'), so if batch quantities are inconsistent with PharmacyMedicine.stock the batch deduction can under-deduct without error.

absences.expected: Idempotency lock preventing double stock posting on RN approve_receipt
absences.searched: InternalOrderResource.approveReceiptPharmacyToPharmacyRN relies solely on the RN.status=='PENDING' check (line 1372). No DB lock / optimistic version. Concurrent calls before commit could both pass the PENDING check (race-prone).

absences.expected: Envers @Audited on these entities
absences.searched: PharmacyToPharmacyRO/TO/RN/Batch and their *Detail entities (domain/*.java) carry @Entity/@Data/@Table only; no @Audited annotation. Consistent with the engagement-wide finding that no entity is @Audited; envers is a phantom dependency.

verdicts.planClaim: PharmacyToPharmacyRO/TO/RN three-document dance: RO raised by requesting pharmacy, TO created by delivering pharmacy from a SUBMITTED RO, RN created back at requesting pharmacy.
verdicts.evidence: Ground-truth lane summary confirms the exact three-document chain with the same entity names. RO is PharmacyToPharmacyRO (PharmacyToPharmacyROServiceImpl); TO is created by PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.createOrder, hard-gated on RO.status=='SUBMITTED' (PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:80-86) and flips RO to IN-PROCESS; RN created by PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.createReceivingNote (lines 83-136). The named aggregates and the RO->TO->RN flow direction match legacy exactly.

verdicts.planClaim: StockMovement rows written ONLY on RN COMPLETED, inside the same transaction as PharmacyToPharmacyRN.complete(); zero movement rows on the source pharmacy until RN COMPLETED (DoD line 165: 'zero StockMovement rows on source pharmacy until complete()').
verdicts.evidence: Directly contradicts legacy. The SOURCE/delivering-pharmacy stock deduction, the source PharmacyStockCard 'out' row (qtyOut=transferedQty, reference 'Goods transfered to Pharmacy PTPO# ...'), AND the FEFO batch deduction all happen on TO.issue() at the APPROVED->GOODS-ISSUED transition (PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:242-280; stock-card save at 268). Only the DESTINATION increment + destination stock-card 'in' row happen on RN approve_receipt (PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.approveReceivingNote:192-219). Source and destination writes are on DIFFERENT documents and DIFFERENT transactions, with a real window where goods are deducted from source but not yet added to destination. The legacy writes a source stock-card row at goods-issue, so 'zero movement rows on source until RN COMPLETED' is false; the RN.complete()-only-tx framing collapses two distinct legacy posting points into one.
verdicts.correction: Split the stock posting to match legacy: write the source-side stock decrement + source stock-card OUT row + FEFO source-batch deduction inside the TO goods-issue transaction (APPROVED->GOODS-ISSUED), and write only the destination increment + destination stock-card IN row inside the RN complete/approve_receipt transaction. The DoD test asserting zero source movement until RN COMPLETED must be rewritten to assert the source movement appears at TO goods-issue and the destination movement appears at RN complete. Note the legacy also has NO ownership guard on TO verify/approve/issue (only status guards), unlike the RO service which checks requesting-pharmacy id equality.

verdicts.planClaim: Document number: Pharmacy-to-Pharmacy RO = PPR{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} via seq_ppr_no.
verdicts.evidence: Prefix PPR matches legacy: RO no = Formater.formatWithCurrentDate('PPR', MAX(id)+1) (PharmacyToPharmacyROServiceImpl.java:372-381). The PREFIX+yyyyMMdd+'-'+id pattern is preserved. However legacy uses application-side MAX(id)+1 (race-prone), NOT a PostgreSQL sequence, and applies NO zero-padding (e.g. PPR20260605-7). The seq_ppr_no sequence is a modern re-modelling of the numbering MECHANISM under ADR-0009 §5; behaviour (format string, prefix) is preserved, so this is acceptable as a labelled deviation, not legacy reproduction.
verdicts.correction: Note explicitly that seq_ppr_no replaces the legacy MAX(id)+1 mechanism per ADR-0009 §5 (an approved change to remove the race), and that the legacy {seq} segment is the raw id with NO zero-padding — if the fresh build zero-pads or formats {seq} differently, that is a further (currently undocumented) format deviation from legacy output.

verdicts.planClaim: Document number: Pharmacy-to-Pharmacy TO = PPTO{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} via seq_ptp_no (replaces legacy SPT, ADR-0009 §6).
verdicts.evidence: Correctly identifies that the legacy TO prefix is SPT, NOT PPTO: PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.requestTransferOrderNo calls Formater.formatWithCurrentDate('SPT', id) (line 428/421-430). The doc labels PPTO as 'replaces legacy SPT' and cites ADR-0009 §6, which I verified (docs/adr/0009-money-and-numbering.md:39-47, 104-105, 133-134) documents the SPT collision (StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl:438 and PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl:428 both using 'SPT') and assigns PPTO via seq_ptp_no as an approved change. This is a correctly-flagged deviation, not a false legacy claim. Correction needed only on the numbering MECHANISM: legacy is MAX(id)+1 with no padding, the sequence is the modern replacement.
verdicts.correction: Keep the PPTO assignment (it is a properly documented change request via ADR-0009 §6), but ensure the change-request register records BOTH deviations bundled here: (1) prefix SPT->PPTO, and (2) MAX(id)+1 -> seq_ptp_no. Both are intentional deviations from legacy behaviour and need explicit sign-off.

verdicts.planClaim: Document number: Pharmacy-to-Pharmacy RN = PPRN{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} via seq_pprn_no.
verdicts.evidence: Prefix PPRN matches legacy: RN no = Formater.formatWithCurrentDate('PPRN', MAX(id)+1) (PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:321-330). Format pattern preserved. Same MECHANISM caveat as RO/TO: legacy uses MAX(id)+1 with no zero-padding, not seq_pprn_no.
verdicts.correction: Note that seq_pprn_no replaces legacy MAX(id)+1 (ADR-0009 §5 approved change); legacy {seq} is the raw unpadded id.

verdicts.planClaim: Coefficient applied on cross-unit movement for the P2P transfer (DoD line 165: TRANSFER_OUT/TRANSFER_IN rows written 'with correct coefficients applied'; entity ItemMedicineCoefficient 'applied on every movement crossing unit boundaries').
verdicts.evidence: No legacy basis in the P2P transfer lane. The three P2P services move quantities 1:1 with no unit conversion: add_batch accumulates tod.transferedQty += batch.qty directly (InternalOrderResource.java:1255); TO.issue deducts exactly transferedQty from source stock and writes qtyOut=transferedQty (PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:242-280); RN sets receivedQty = TODetail.transferedQty verbatim (PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:117) and the destination adds exactly receivedQty (lines 192-219). Nothing in the ground-truth findings or absences mentions any coefficient, multiply, or unit boundary in the P2P path. Applying a coefficient to TRANSFER_OUT/TRANSFER_IN would CHANGE the transferred quantity relative to legacy.
verdicts.correction: Remove the coefficient from the P2P transfer movement path (or prove it from a separate legacy lane and flag as NEW SCOPE via change-request register). Source and destination must move the same quantity 1:1 (transferedQty == receivedQty) exactly as legacy. The DoD coefficient assertion on P2P TRANSFER_OUT/TRANSFER_IN rows has no legacy support for this lane; if ItemMedicineCoefficient applies elsewhere (e.g. dispensing/sale unit conversion), scope it there, not on the inter-pharmacy transfer.

verdicts.planClaim: ADR-0009 §6: PPTO must NOT use legacy SPT prefix.
verdicts.evidence: Confirmed on both sides. Legacy P2P TO prefix is SPT (PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:428). ADR-0009 §6 (docs/adr/0009-money-and-numbering.md:39-47) names the exact legacy collision (StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl:438 + PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl:428 both 'SPT') and resolves PharmacyToPharmacy TO to PPTO and StoreToPharmacy TO to SPTO, with product-owner sign-off as a release gate. The 'SPT is a defect' pitfall (planning doc line 144) is a correct, approved change-control statement, not invented legacy behaviour.

newDriftFound: RN is only a two-state machine in legacy (PENDING -> COMPLETED). PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl has NO verify()/approve() method; the entity's verified*/approved* audit fields are NEVER populated (PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:104 PENDING, InternalOrderResource.java:1392 COMPLETED). The planning doc never states the RN lifecycle; the inc-08 endpoint list shows only /p2p-rns/{uid}/complete which is consistent, but the build must NOT add RN verify/approve transitions (would be PHANTOM, inc-06-style). Decide and document.

CRITICAL ABSENCE the doc is silent on: the destination pharmacy NEVER gets PharmacyMedicineBatch (StockBatch) rows on receipt - the batch-creation block in approveReceivingNote is fully commented out (PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:221-232). So legacy received stock increments balance but carries NO batch/expiry record at the destination (batch traceability is lost). The doc's StockBatch model implies destination batches would be created on TRANSFER_IN. Inc-08 must consciously decide: faithfully reproduce the legacy gap (no destination batch) OR treat destination-batch creation as a NEW SCOPE / bug-fix change request (likely the right clinical call, but it must go through change-request control, not be silently 'improved').

RO 'IN-PROCESS' state is a dead-end for return/reject: RO._return and RO.reject hard-require status=='SUBMITTED', but creating a TO flips RO to IN-PROCESS, after which no path restores SUBMITTED (PharmacyToPharmacyROServiceImpl.java:254-256,275-277; TO createOrder IN-PROCESS flip). The planning doc RO/TO/RN section and endpoint list omit return/reject entirely and never mention the IN-PROCESS state. Build must decide whether to reproduce this irreversibility.

RO full legacy state set is richer than implied: PENDING, VERIFIED, APPROVED, SUBMITTED, RETURNED, REJECTED, IN-PROCESS, GOODS-ISSUED, COMPLETED (9 states). The doc shows only verify/approve endpoints for the RO and no submit/return/reject and no IN-PROCESS/GOODS-ISSUED visibility. Submit (APPROVED->SUBMITTED) is REQUIRED before a TO can be created (TO createOrder gates on RO=='SUBMITTED'), so the missing /p2p-ros/{uid}/submit endpoint is a functional gap in the doc.

TO has its own VERIFIED/APPROVED transitions before goods-issue (PENDING->VERIFIED->APPROVED->GOODS-ISSUED; PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:177-181,199-203,221-236), but the inc-08 endpoint list only shows /p2p-tos and /p2p-tos/{uid}/goods-issue - the TO verify/approve transitions are missing from the doc.

add_batch / delete_batch on the TO and the PharmacyToPharmacyBatch lifecycle are entirely absent from the doc. transferedQty is NOT entered on the TO header; it is accumulated via the /pharmacy_to_pharmacy_t_os/add_batch endpoint (InternalOrderResource.java:1219-1268) with hard guards (PENDING-only, qty>0, SKU match, cumulative transferedQty <= orderedQty 'Can not transfer more than ordered qty'). PharmacyToPharmacyBatch rows are display/traceability only and are re-linked to the RN detail in createReceivingNote (PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:126-129). The doc omits this whole sub-flow and its over-order guard.

Legacy idempotency is structural, not lock-based: TO createOrder and RN createReceivingNote return existing docs as-is (idempotent), and approve_receipt relies SOLELY on RN.status=='PENDING' (InternalOrderResource.java:1372) with NO DB/optimistic lock - race-prone double-post. The planning doc instead mandates @Version optimistic lock on status transitions (ADR-0017 §2) and PESSIMISTIC_WRITE only on stock decrement; that is a deliberate modern hardening (acceptable) but it is a change from legacy's no-lock behaviour and should be in the change-request register, plus the legacy idempotent 'return existing doc' behaviour on TO/RN creation must be preserved or explicitly changed.

177-code RBAC claim is contradicted for this lane: every RO/TO/RN/batch endpoint in legacy is gated by a SINGLE coarse privilege @PreAuthorize("hasAnyAuthority('PHARMACY_ORDER-ALL')") with NO separation of duty between verify/approve/issue/receive (InternalOrderResource.java:1220,1272,1335,1361 + 780-1151). The doc's DoD line 173 ('correct @PreAuthorize codes from the 177-code set') and the architecture note assume fine-grained per-transition codes. If inc-08 invents distinct verify/approve/issue/receive privilege codes for P2P, that is PHANTOM granularity vs legacy; reproducing exact RBAC means one PHARMACY_ORDER-ALL-equivalent code covers all P2P transitions. (Same inc-06 177-code trap.)

FEFO + silent-swallow batch deduction at source: legacy deductBatch consumes source batches FEFO (earliest expiryDate, fallback lowest id) and SILENTLY swallows all exceptions ('//do nothing', PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:300-302); the ONLY hard stock gate is PharmacyMedicine.stock < transferedQty at line 247-249. The doc's FEFO/negative-stock guard (target #2, ADR-0017) describes a hard INSUFFICIENT_STOCK gate at the dispense path but does not address that the legacy P2P source batch deduction can UNDER-deduct without error when batch qty is inconsistent with the aggregate stock. Reproducing 'exact process' vs fixing the silent-swallow is a change-request decision.

No Envers/@Audited on any P2P entity (PharmacyToPharmacyRO/TO/RN/Batch + details carry only @Entity/@Data/@Table). Consistent with the engagement-wide phantom-Envers finding. The doc's DoD line 175 ('Audit events emitted for every state transition') is correctly NET-NEW (tamper-evident trail is approved new scope), not a legacy reproduction - ensure it stays labelled as such and is not mistaken for porting legacy audit behaviour.

extract.summary: The lane is a 3-document chain: (1) PharmacyToStoreRO = pharmacy's requisition/request (prefix PSR); (2) StoreToPharmacyTO = store's transfer/issue order (prefix SPT, NOT SPTO/SPTO); (3) StoreToPharmacyRN = pharmacy's goods-received note (prefix PGRN, NOT PGRN-vs-SPT confusion — RN is PGRN). The RO is request-only and moves NO stock. STORE stock decrements ONLY in StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.issue() (status APPROVED→GOODS-ISSUED): it hard-rejects on negative stock, decrements StoreItem.stock, writes ONE StoreStockCard qtyOut row per detail, and FEFO-deducts StoreItemBatch rows. PHARMACY stock increments ONLY in StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.approveReceivingNote() (called from controller after RN PENDING→COMPLETED): it adds to PharmacyMedicine.stock, writes a PharmacyStockCard qtyIn row (only when receivedPharmacySKUQty>0), and creates PharmacyMedicineBatch rows. Store uses the SAME StoreStockCard ledger discipline as pharmacy's PharmacyStockCard (qtyIn/qtyOut/balance/reference). All three doc numbers are built as PREFIX + yyyyMMdd + "-" + (MAX(id)+1) with NO zero-padding and NO fiscal/daily reset — the suffix is a global monotonic table-id counter; the embedded date is cosmetic.

findings.behaviour: PharmacyToStoreRO.no = Formater.formatWithCurrentDate("PSR", id) where id = pharmacyToStoreRORepository.getLastId()+1 (getLastId = SELECT MAX(p.id)). Final format: PSR + yyyyMMdd + "-" + id. No zero-padding on suffix; suffix is global MAX(id)+1, never resets by day/year. On any exception id defaults to 1L.

findings.topic: TO document number scheme (SPT — NOT SPTO)
findings.behaviour: StoreToPharmacyTO.no = Formater.formatWithCurrentDate("SPT", MAX(StoreToPharmacyTO.id)+1). Prefix literal is "SPT" (three letters), NOT "SPTO". Same yyyyMMdd-suffix scheme, no padding, no reset.
StoreToPharmacyRN.no = Formater.formatWithCurrentDate("PGRN", MAX(StoreToPharmacyRN.id)+1). Prefix literal is "PGRN". Same scheme.

Statuses: PENDING (set on save, line 81) → VERIFIED (verify, 170) → APPROVED (approve, 201) → SUBMITTED (submit, 232) → then store side flips it to IN-PROCESS (in TO.createOrder, 87) → GOODS-ISSUED (in TO.issue, 240) → COMPLETED (in RN approve_receipt controller, 731). Side branches from SUBMITTED: RETURNED (_return, 253, guard: status must be SUBMITTED) and REJECTED (reject, 274, guard: status must be SUBMITTED). Each forward transition HARD-rejects if current status != required prior status (verify requires PENDING:163; approve requires VERIFIED:194; submit requires APPROVED:225). Edit/save only allowed while PENDING (91-93).

Statuses: PENDING (createOrder, 98) → VERIFIED (verify, 186; guard status==PENDING:183) → APPROVED (approve, 208; guard status==VERIFIED:205) → GOODS-ISSUED (issue, 230; guard status==APPROVED:227) → COMPLETED (set in RN approve_receipt controller, 739). No RETURNED/REJECTED on the TO. createOrder only proceeds if the source RO is SUBMITTED, else InvalidOperationException (86-92).

findings.topic: RN state machine + where transitions actually live
findings.behaviour: RN statuses: PENDING (createReceivingNote, 110) → COMPLETED. The PENDING→COMPLETED transition AND the guard (status must be PENDING) live in the CONTROLLER approve_receipt (InternalOrderResource.java:725-748), NOT in the service. The controller also cascades RO→COMPLETED (731) and TO→COMPLETED (739) before invoking the service. RN entity has verifiedBy/On/At and approvedBy/On/At columns but they are NEVER populated anywhere in this lane (no setVerified*/setApproved* calls in createReceivingNote or approveReceivingNote).

Exclusively in StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.issue() (class @Transactional). Per TO detail: loads StoreItem by (store,item); HARD reject if StoreItem.stock < transferedStoreSKUQty ("Can not issue goods. Stock qty is less than transfer qty..."); newStock = stock - transferedStoreSKUQty; saves StoreItem; writes StoreStockCard{qtyIn=0, qtyOut=transferedStoreSKUQty, balance=newStock, reference="Goods transfered to Pharmacy STPO# "+order.getNo()}; then FEFO-deducts StoreItemBatch.

deductBatch()/getEarlierBatch() over StoreItemBatch with qty>0. If ANY batch in the list has a non-null expiryDate, only those with expiry are considered and the EARLIEST expiryDate is chosen (FEFO). If NO batch has an expiry, it falls back to the LOWEST id (effectively FIFO by insert order). Deduction recurses across batches when qty exceeds a batch; on exception it silently does nothing (empty catch).

Exclusively in StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.approveReceivingNote() (class @Transactional). Per RN detail: loads PharmacyMedicine by (pharmacy,medicine); newStock = stock + receivedPharmacySKUQty; saves PharmacyMedicine; if receivedPharmacySKUQty>0 writes PharmacyStockCard{qtyIn=receivedPharmacySKUQty, qtyOut=0, balance=newStock, reference="Medicine received # "+receiveNote.getNo()}; creates one PharmacyMedicineBatch per StoreToPharmacyBatch (no, mfg/expiry dates, qty=pharmacySKUQty).

findings.topic: Store uses same StoreStockCard ledger discipline as pharmacy
findings.behaviour: YES. StoreStockCard mirrors PharmacyStockCard fields (qtyIn, qtyOut, balance, reference, dateTime, createdBy/On/At). Store ledger rows are appended (never updated) on: transfer issue (TO.issue), supplier GRN receipt, item creation initial stock, manual stock update, and store-register update. The 'balance' written is the computed running stock at time of write.

findings.topic: TO batch entry, qty caps and SKU conversion
findings.behaviour: Batches are entered on the TO while status==PENDING via controller add_batch. Guards: detail must be PENDING (573-575); item must exist; if detail already has an item the new batch item must match same SKU (581-585); storeSKUQty>0 (592-594); pharmacySKUQty = storeSKUQty * ItemMedicineCoefficient.coefficient (595); running transferedStoreSKUQty/transferedPharmacySKUQty are incremented (599-600); HARD reject if transferedPharmacySKUQty > orderedPharmacySKUQty (602-604). delete_batch reverses the sums and clears item when transferedStoreSKUQty hits 0.

saveDetail also sets item + transferedStoreSKUQty and computes transferedPharmacySKUQty = transferedStoreSKUQty * coefficient, requiring an ItemMedicineCoefficient for (item,medicine) else NotFoundException. This is a second write path coexisting with add_batch; both mutate the same detail qty fields.

findings.topic: RN snapshots TO quantities and re-parents batches
findings.behaviour: createReceivingNote copies each TO detail into an RN detail: orderedPharmacySKUQty=TO.orderedPharmacySKUQty, receivedPharmacySKUQty=TO.transferedPharmacySKUQty, receivedStoreSKUQty=TO.transferedStoreSKUQty, item=TO.item. It then RE-PARENTS the existing StoreToPharmacyBatch rows from the TO detail onto the RN detail (b.setStoreToPharmacyRNDetail(...)). It only runs if TO status==GOODS-ISSUED (96-98); otherwise InvalidOperationException.

findings.topic: Decoupling: pharmacy-bound batch identity vs FEFO-consumed store batch
findings.behaviour: The StoreToPharmacyBatch rows (no/mfg/expiry/qty) that ultimately become PharmacyMedicineBatch are entered MANUALLY at TO add_batch time and carried through unchanged. They are NOT reconciled against the StoreItemBatch rows that issue() actually FEFO-deducts. The store-side decrement (FEFO over StoreItemBatch) and the pharmacy-side batch identity (StoreToPharmacyBatch→PharmacyMedicineBatch) are independent — a manually-entered transfer batch no/expiry need not match the FEFO-consumed store batch.

findings.topic: Worklist FILTER vs HARD reject (store/pharmacy queues)
findings.behaviour: Worklist endpoints are FILTERS, not gates: store sees ROs only in {SUBMITTED, IN-PROCESS, GOODS-ISSUED} (load_pharmacy_orders_by_store); pharmacy sees its ROs in the full status set (load_pharmacy_orders_by_pharmacy). The actual state guards are HARD service/controller rejects (InvalidOperationException), independent of these filters.

All three numbers use MAX(id)+1 read at request_no time, NOT a DB sequence and NOT computed atomically at save. The unique constraint on 'no' (entity @Column(unique=true)) is the only collision guard; concurrent requests can compute the same suffix. Suffix is the raw Long with no padding.

All quantities/stock/coefficient math is Java primitive double (StoreItem.stock, StoreItemBatch.qty, StoreToPharmacyTODetail.transfered*SKUQty, StoreToPharmacyBatch.*SKUQty, StoreStockCard.qtyIn/qtyOut/balance, coefficient multiply). No BigDecimal, no rounding applied in this lane.

findings.topic: RO detail duplicate guard and receivedQty
findings.behaviour: RO saveDetail rejects duplicate (RO,medicine) pairs on insert (295-298), forces receivedQty=0 and status=PENDING. RO detail receivedQty is never updated back in this lane (the RN does not write back to RODetail.receivedQty).

RO endpoints require PHARMACY_ORDER-ALL (state changes) or PHARMACY_ORDER-CREATE/UPDATE (save). TO state-change + batch endpoints require STORE_ORDER-ALL. RN create + approve_receipt require PHARMACY_ORDER-ALL. Several GET worklist/no endpoints have NO @PreAuthorize.

absences.expected: Hibernate Envers @Audited annotation on any transfer-lane entity (PharmacyToStoreRO/RODetail, StoreToPharmacyTO/TODetail, StoreToPharmacyRN/RNDetail, StoreToPharmacyBatch, StoreItem, StoreItemBatch, StoreStockCard)
absences.searched: Read all 9 listed domain classes in com.orbix.api.domain; none carry @Audited. Consistent with memory note that Envers is a bundled dependency but never activated. No Envers audit baseline exists for this lane.

absences.expected: Device-fingerprint / device-binding logic on the transfer endpoints
absences.searched: Read InternalOrderResource and StoreResource; only standard @PreAuthorize authority checks and HttpServletRequest→userService.getUser(request). No device-fingerprint/device-binding mechanism in this lane.

absences.expected: RN-level negative-stock or over-receive guard at pharmacy receive
absences.searched: StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.approveReceivingNote (201-242): increments PharmacyMedicine.stock unconditionally by receivedPharmacySKUQty; no upper-bound/over-receipt check; relies solely on PharmacyMedicine existing (Optional.get() would throw if absent).

absences.expected: Service-layer status guard inside StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.approveReceivingNote
absences.searched: The method (201-298) has NO status check; the PENDING guard and all status transitions are in the controller approve_receipt (InternalOrderResource.java:725-748). Calling the service directly would re-increment pharmacy stock with no guard — a latent double-post risk.

absences.expected: Population of RN verifiedBy/On/At and approvedBy/On/At, and any RN verify/approve step distinct from receipt
absences.searched: StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl (full file) and InternalOrderResource RN endpoints (687-754): no setVerified*/setApproved* calls; RN has a single PENDING→COMPLETED transition via approve_receipt; verify/approve audit columns remain null.

absences.expected: Reconciliation between FEFO-consumed StoreItemBatch and the StoreToPharmacyBatch carried to pharmacy
absences.searched: StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.issue (248-286) FEFO-deducts StoreItemBatch independently; StoreToPharmacyBatch (entered at add_batch, InternalOrderResource:563-610) is not linked to the deducted StoreItemBatch. No code reconciles batch no/expiry between the two.

absences.expected: Store-side stock decrement at any point other than TO.issue (e.g. at RN time)
absences.searched: Grep of storeItemRepository.save / new StoreStockCard across com.orbix.api: store decrement for transfers occurs ONLY in StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.issue:259-274; RN service never touches StoreItem/StoreStockCard. No double-decrement on receive.

verdicts.planClaim: Pharmacy<->store side is modelled as PharmacyToStoreRO + StoreToPharmacyRN; StoreToPharmacyTO (store prepares shipment) lives in the inventory module (doc lines 23, 28).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy ground truth confirms the lane is a 3-document chain: PharmacyToStoreRO (pharmacy requisition, PSR), StoreToPharmacyTO (store transfer/issue order, SPT), StoreToPharmacyRN (pharmacy goods-received note, PGRN). All three entities exist (PharmacyToStoreROServiceImpl, StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl, StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl). The doc DOES name all three: RO+RN appear on the pharmacy-side line 23, TO on the inventory-side line 28, and the REST endpoints at lines 68-70 wire ps-ros (pharmacy), sp-tos (inventory), sp-rns (inventory). Correction: the entity set is right but the doc's module split (RO on pharmacy side, TO+RN endpoints under /inventory) does not match the legacy reality that the RN is the PHARMACY's own goods-received note where PHARMACY stock increments (StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.approveReceivingNote, 201-242). Placing the sp-rn complete endpoint under /api/v1/inventory (line 70) is a defensible modern module choice, not legacy drift, but the planning doc must record that the RN's stock effect is a PHARMACY-side increment, not an inventory/store action.
verdicts.correction: State explicitly that StoreToPharmacyRN is the pharmacy's own goods-received note and its only stock effect is incrementing PharmacyMedicine/pharmacy stock; the store-side decrement is a separate event on the TO.

verdicts.planClaim: Doc number PSR{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} via seq_psr_no for Pharmacy-to-Store RO (doc lines 123, ADR-0009 §5).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy: PharmacyToStoreRO.no = Formater.formatWithCurrentDate("PSR", MAX(id)+1) => PSR + yyyyMMdd + "-" + id, no zero-padding, no daily/fiscal reset, suffix is a global monotonic table-id counter (PharmacyToStoreROServiceImpl.java:367-376; Formater.java:14-17). Prefix PSR and the PREFIX{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} shape match. CORRECTION: the legacy {seq} is NOT a per-document-type day-reset sequence value -- it is MAX(table id)+1, a global monotonic counter, and the embedded date is cosmetic. The fresh build replaces this with PostgreSQL seq_psr_no starting at 1 (ADR-0009 §5, lines 102/131), which is a deliberate, pre-approved mechanism change (CR-09) that preserves the format string but NOT the legacy semantic that the suffix equals the row id. The doc should not imply seq mirrors the legacy id value; it is a clean-break atomic sequence.
verdicts.correction: Note that legacy suffix = MAX(id)+1 (global, equals row id, racy), whereas seq_psr_no is a fresh atomic per-type sequence starting at 1; format preserved, suffix-equals-id invariant intentionally dropped (CR-09).

verdicts.planClaim: Doc number SPTO{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} via seq_sto_no for StoreToPharmacy TO, replacing legacy SPT (doc lines 124, 144, 167; ADR-0009 §6).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy literal prefix is "SPT" (three letters), NOT SPTO: StoreToPharmacyTO.no = Formater.formatWithCurrentDate("SPT", MAX(id)+1) (StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:431-439, confirmed at line 438 by legacy-findings.md §D and ADR-0009 §6 line 40). The SPT prefix is SHARED with PharmacyToPharmacyTO (collision defect). The SPTO rename is a deliberate, change-request-controlled deviation: CR-10 (02-masterdata-discovery/00-build-spec.md:284), ratified in ADR-0009 §6 (SPT->SPTO), pending product-owner sign-off (overview.md:188; 02-master-data.md:139). This is NOT silent drift -- it is a documented, approved fix to a real legacy collision. The doc correctly labels it "replaces legacy SPT". CORRECTION/CAUTION: the deviation is still gated on product-owner sign-off (engagement-lead is approver, not the BA), so any inc-08 acceptance criterion must reference the change request, not assert SPTO as reproduced legacy behaviour. The doc's line 144 ("any test or seed script using legacy SPT prefix is a defect") is correct given CR-10.
verdicts.correction: Tie the SPTO assertion to CR-10 / ADR-0009 §6 and the outstanding product-owner sign-off; the legacy emits SPT, SPTO is the approved replacement, not legacy behaviour.

verdicts.planClaim: Doc number PGRN{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} via seq_pgrn_no for StoreToPharmacy RN (doc lines 125, 135).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy: StoreToPharmacyRN.no = Formater.formatWithCurrentDate("PGRN", MAX(id)+1) (StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:332-340). Prefix PGRN and format shape are correct. Same MAX(id)+1 vs atomic-sequence (seq_pgrn_no, ADR-0009 line 106/135) caveat as PSR: format preserved, suffix-equals-id invariant intentionally dropped via CR-09. No prefix collision on PGRN, so no SPTO-style rename needed; this one is a straight format reproduction.
verdicts.correction: Same as PSR: the legacy suffix is MAX(id)+1 (= row id); seq_pgrn_no is the modernized atomic mechanism. No prefix change.

verdicts.planClaim: Inventory module: StoreItem / StoreItemBatch -- store-side stock, same FEFO discipline; StoreStockBalance + StoreStockMovement ledger (doc line 27).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy has StoreItem (StoreItem.java:44, holds .stock as a double scalar) and StoreItemBatch (StoreItemBatch.java:43, FEFO over qty>0 with earliest non-null expiry then lowest-id fallback -- StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:291-350). The FEFO discipline claim is ACCURATE. BUT the legacy ledger is a SINGLE entity, StoreStockCard, that mirrors PharmacyStockCard (qtyIn, qtyOut, balance, reference, dateTime -- StoreStockCard.java:34-58); the running balance is a FIELD ON THE LEDGER ROW (the computed running stock at time of write), and the authoritative current stock lives on StoreItem.stock. There is NO separate balance table and NO separate movement table -- it is one append-only StoreStockCard plus the scalar StoreItem.stock. The doc splits this into TWO modern entities, StoreStockBalance (running balance) + StoreStockMovement (movement ledger). This is a re-modelling that goes BEYOND faithful field renaming: it invents a dedicated balance table the legacy never had and renames the single StoreStockCard ledger to StoreStockMovement. The behaviour (append-only ledger row carrying running balance + a scalar current stock) can be preserved, but the doc presents a two-table model with no legacy basis and must be flagged. Treat the StoreStockBalance/StoreStockMovement split as an inventory-design decision requiring explicit data-architect sign-off, not as reproduced legacy structure.
verdicts.correction: Legacy is ONE StoreStockCard ledger (qtyIn/qtyOut/balance/reference) plus a scalar StoreItem.stock -- there is no separate balance table. If the modern model splits into StoreStockBalance + StoreStockMovement, label it a deliberate re-modelling (data-architect sign-off) and a parity test must prove the StoreStockCard running-balance + StoreItem.stock semantics are preserved.

verdicts.planClaim: ADR-0009 §6: SPTO must NOT use legacy SPT (doc lines 124, 144).
verdicts.evidence: ADR-0009 §6 (0009-money-and-numbering.md:39-47) exists and explicitly states the legacy root cause (StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl:438 and PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl:428 both call formatWithCurrentDate("SPT", id)) and assigns StoreToPharmacyTO -> SPTO, PharmacyToPharmacyTO -> PPTO, declaring itself the single source of truth pending product-owner sign-off. The doc's citation of ADR-0009 §6 and the "must not use SPT" rule is faithful to the ADR. The underlying legacy fact (literal SPT prefix, shared collision) is confirmed by the ground-truth findings (StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:431-439).

verdicts.planClaim: StoreToPharmacy RN /complete endpoint (POST /api/v1/inventory/sp-rns/uid/{uid}/complete, doc line 70) drives the pharmacy-side stock credit at RN COMPLETED.
verdicts.evidence: Legacy: pharmacy stock increments EXCLUSIVELY in StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.approveReceivingNote() (class @Transactional; StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:201-242): adds receivedPharmacySKUQty to PharmacyMedicine.stock, writes a PharmacyStockCard qtyIn row ONLY when receivedPharmacySKUQty>0, and creates one PharmacyMedicineBatch per StoreToPharmacyBatch. So crediting pharmacy stock at RN-complete is ACCURATE. CRITICAL CORRECTIONS the inc-08 doc must capture: (1) the PENDING->COMPLETED transition and its guard (status must be PENDING) live in the CONTROLLER approve_receipt (InternalOrderResource.java:725-748), NOT in the service -- the service has NO status guard, so calling it directly double-posts pharmacy stock (latent double-post risk, absences[3]). The modern build must move the guard into the transactional service to be safe. (2) The controller ALSO cascades RO->COMPLETED (731) and TO->COMPLETED (739). (3) There is NO over-receipt / upper-bound guard at pharmacy receive -- stock is incremented unconditionally (absences[2]). (4) RN verifiedBy/On/At and approvedBy/On/At columns are NEVER populated -- there is a single PENDING->COMPLETED step, no distinct RN verify/approve.
verdicts.correction: Document that the RN status guard currently lives in the controller (latent double-post if service called directly) and must be moved into the transactional service; record there is NO over-receipt guard and NO RN verify/approve step (verified/approved audit columns stay null in legacy).

verdicts.planClaim: ADR-0017 §2 pessimistic-write lock on stock decrement; FEFO dispatch acquires SELECT FOR UPDATE with negative-stock guard after the lock (doc lines 20, 111, 145).
verdicts.evidence: This claim is in the pharmacy-dispensing section, but it is the same locking model the doc applies across the stock-decrement path including the store/transfer side, so it is in-scope to flag. The legacy ps-sp lane has NO pessimistic locking anywhere: store decrement in TO.issue() is a plain read-modify-write on StoreItem.stock inside a class-level @Transactional with a HARD negative-stock reject (StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:221-289), and the ONLY collision guard on document numbers is the @Column(unique=true) on no (numbering precision/race finding; PharmacyToStoreRO.java:48-50). There is no SELECT FOR UPDATE, no @Version, no row lock in this lane. SELECT FOR UPDATE / PESSIMISTIC_WRITE / OptimisticLock-as-409 is therefore a NET-NEW concurrency-correctness feature with NO legacy basis -- exactly the inc-06-class drift to flag. It is a legitimate modernization (the legacy MAX(id)+1 and unprotected read-modify-write are genuinely racy), but it must be labelled NEW SCOPE / design decision (ADR-0017), not reproduced legacy behaviour. The legacy negative-stock guard itself (hard reject when stock < transfer qty) IS real and is preserved.
verdicts.correction: Pessimistic locking and FEFO SELECT-FOR-UPDATE are net-new (ADR-0017), not legacy behaviour; the legacy has only a hard negative-stock reject and a unique(no) constraint. Label the locking model as a deliberate concurrency-hardening design decision; preserve and cite the real legacy hard negative-stock reject in TO.issue() separately.

verdicts.planClaim: ItemMedicineCoefficient.coefficient NUMERIC(19,6); transferQty.multiply(coefficient) carries full BigDecimal precision (doc lines 24, 128).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy ItemMedicineCoefficient.coefficient exists and is applied: TO add_batch computes pharmacySKUQty = storeSKUQty * coefficient (InternalOrderResource.java:595) and saveDetail computes transferedPharmacySKUQty = transferedStoreSKUQty * coefficient requiring an ItemMedicineCoefficient(item,medicine) else NotFoundException (StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:405-429). So the coefficient and the cross-unit multiplication are REAL legacy behaviour. CORRECTION: legacy does ALL of this in primitive double (StoreToPharmacyTODetail.transfered*SKUQty, coefficient multiply -- arithmetic-precision finding, lines 424/595); there is NO BigDecimal and NO rounding in this lane. The NUMERIC(19,6) / full-BigDecimal-precision claim is the pre-approved double->BigDecimal migration (ADR-0009 §3, client pre-approved per memory note), a deliberate accepted deviation, not reproduced legacy behaviour. Acceptable, but must be tagged as the approved precision change, and the parity definition (ADR-0009 §4) governs equivalence.
verdicts.correction: Coefficient and cross-unit multiply are real legacy (double); the NUMERIC(19,6)/BigDecimal precision is the pre-approved double->BigDecimal change (ADR-0009 §3), not a legacy attribute -- tag it as such.

newDriftFound: TWO write paths mutate TO detail quantities: legacy has BOTH the controller add_batch path (InternalOrderResource.java:563-648) AND StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.saveDetail (405-429), both setting item + transfered*SKUQty on the same detail. The inc-08 doc models a single TO with no mention of these coexisting write paths. The fresh build must consciously decide whether to keep both or consolidate to one -- and if consolidating, that is a deliberate behaviour change to log.

Batch-identity decoupling: the StoreToPharmacyBatch rows (no/mfg/expiry/qty) that become PharmacyMedicineBatch are entered MANUALLY at TO add_batch time and are NOT reconciled against the StoreItemBatch rows that issue() actually FEFO-deducts (InternalOrderResource.java:595-610; StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:277-283; StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:230-241). The store-side FEFO consumption and the pharmacy-bound batch identity are independent in legacy -- the manually-entered transfer batch no/expiry need NOT match the FEFO-consumed store batch. The doc's clean StockBatch/FEFO model implies reconciled batch identity; this decoupling (and whether to preserve or fix it) is unmentioned and must be decided via change request.

RO state machine forward-transition hard guards: legacy RO requires exact prior status for each forward step (verify requires PENDING:163, approve requires VERIFIED:194, submit requires APPROVED:225) and edit/save only while PENDING (91-93), with RETURNED/REJECTED side-branches only from SUBMITTED (PharmacyToStoreROServiceImpl.java:81-279). The doc's ps-sp endpoint list (lines 68-70) is thin (ps-ros POST, sp-tos POST, sp-rns complete) and does not enumerate the RO verify/approve/submit/return/reject transitions or their hard guards. The full RO/TO state machines and their hard-reject guards must be authored into stories with explicit prior-status preconditions.

RO detail receivedQty is never written back in the legacy lane -- saveDetail forces receivedQty=0 and the RN does NOT update RODetail.receivedQty (PharmacyToStoreROServiceImpl.java:282-317). If the modern design expects the RO to reflect received quantities, that is net-new and must be flagged; legacy leaves RODetail.receivedQty permanently 0.

Worklist endpoints are FILTERS, not gates: store sees ROs only in {SUBMITTED, IN-PROCESS, GOODS-ISSUED}, pharmacy sees the full status set (InternalOrderResource.java:184-220); the real state enforcement is the hard service/controller InvalidOperationException rejects, independent of the filters. The doc's general 'hard gate not filter' principle (line 109, PHARM-2) is asserted for the pay gate but the inc-08 doc does not explicitly carry this filter-vs-gate distinction into the transfer worklists -- it should, to avoid re-implementing a filter as the enforcement mechanism.

RN snapshots TO quantities and RE-PARENTS batch rows: createReceivingNote copies TO detail qty fields into RN detail (orderedPharmacySKUQty, receivedPharmacySKUQty=TO.transferedPharmacySKUQty, etc.) and re-parents existing StoreToPharmacyBatch rows from the TO detail onto the RN detail, only if TO status==GOODS-ISSUED (StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:88-141). This snapshot+re-parent behaviour is unmentioned in the doc and is load-bearing for how PharmacyMedicineBatch rows are produced at RN-complete.

Envers/@Audited absence: NONE of the 9 transfer-lane domain classes carry @Audited (confirmed absence). The doc's DoD line 175 requires audit events for every state transition via AuditableEntity + TxAuditContext -- this is correct as NET-NEW (per memory note Envers is a bundled-but-unactivated dependency), but the inc-08 doc does not explicitly state that the transfer-lane audit trail is net-new with no legacy baseline. Should be noted so QA does not look for a legacy audit baseline to match.

double-precision is universal in this lane: StoreItem.stock, StoreItemBatch.qty, StoreStockCard.qtyIn/qtyOut/balance, StoreToPharmacyBatch.*SKUQty are all primitive double (arithmetic-precision finding). The doc's NUMERIC(19,6) is correct as the pre-approved migration but applies to MORE fields than the doc enumerates (it lists StockBatch receivedQty/remainingQty and coefficient); the store-side ledger balance fields also move to NUMERIC(19,6) and that should be explicit.

extract.lane: GRN + LPO + three-way match + Supplier procurement
extract.summary: Legacy procurement is a two-document linear flow: LocalPurchaseOrder (LPO) -> GoodsReceivedNote (GRN). LPO state machine is PENDING -> VERIFIED -> APPROVED -> SUBMITTED (-> RECEIVED, flipped by GRN approval), with branch transitions REJECTED and RETURNED (both reachable only from PENDING or VERIFIED). All LPO transitions are hard service-layer guards in LocalPurchaseOrderServiceImpl that throw InvalidOperationException on wrong source status. A GRN can only be created from a SUBMITTED LPO (guard is in the resource, not the service). GRN has only TWO real lifecycle states at the header level: PENDING -> APPROVED; GRN line details carry their own status NOT VERIFIED -> VERIFIED. GRN approval is one @Transactional method (GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.approve) that, per detail: increments StoreItem.stock, writes a StoreStockCard row (only if receivedQty>0), copies every GoodsReceivedNoteDetailBatch into a brand-new StoreItemBatch, writes a Purchase row (only if receivedQty>0), then flips the linked LPO to RECEIVED and the GRN to APPROVED. There is NO three-way match (no supplier-invoice entity/quantity exists anywhere); the only quantity reconciliation is GRN line: receivedQty<=orderedQty and sum(batch.qty)==receivedQty. Doc numbers (LPO/GRN) are generated as PREFIX + yyyyMMdd + "-" + (MAX(id)+1) at create time. Supplier price list is a flat per-(supplier,item) price table (SupplierItemPrice); SupplierItemPriceList is a non-persistent wrapper DTO.

findings.topic: LPO state machine - statuses + initial state
findings.behaviour: On create (id==null) LPO is set status="PENDING", statusDescription="Order pending for verification". Real status values observed across the service: PENDING, VERIFIED, APPROVED, SUBMITTED, RETURNED, REJECTED, RECEIVED. There is NO DRAFT state.

verify(): hard guard - rejects unless current status equals "PENDING" (throws "Could not verify. Only pending orders can be verified") AND order has at least one detail (else "Could not verify. Order has no items"). Sets status="VERIFIED", statusDescription="Order awaiting for approval", stamps verifiedBy/On/At.

approve(): hard guard - rejects unless status=="VERIFIED" (throws "Could not approve. Only verified orders can be approved") and details non-empty. Sets status="APPROVED", statusDescription="Order awaiting for submission", stamps approvedBy/On/At.

submit(): hard guard - rejects unless status=="APPROVED" (throws "Could not submit. Only approved orders can be submitted") and details non-empty. Sets status="SUBMITTED", statusDescription="Submited to supplier. Order awaiting for delivery". NOTE: submit does NOT stamp any submittedBy/On/At fields (no such columns exist on the entity).

_return(): hard guard - allowed ONLY when status is PENDING or VERIFIED (throws "Could not returned. Only PENDING or VERIFIED orders can be returned"). Sets status="RETURNED", statusDescription="Order returned for ammendment". No re-open transition back to PENDING exists in code; RETURNED is effectively terminal in the service (no method accepts RETURNED as source).

reject(): hard guard - allowed ONLY when status is PENDING or VERIFIED (throws "Could not reject. Only PENDING or VERIFIED orders can be rejected"). Sets status="REJECTED". REJECTED is terminal (no method accepts it as source).

LPO is flipped to RECEIVED NOT by any LPO service method but by GRN approval. GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.approve sets localPurchaseOrder.setStatus("RECEIVED") and stamps receivedBy/On/At, then saves. There is no guard here re-checking the LPO was SUBMITTED (the SUBMITTED check happens earlier at GRN-create time in the resource).

save() on existing LPO (id!=null): only allowed if status=="PENDING" (else "Can not edit. Only pending orders can be edited"); editing no, or changing supplier id, is forbidden. ONLY validUntil is actually updated on edit - order date, store, details are NOT updatable via save().

findings.topic: LPO detail add guard + supplier-item validity + duplicate block + price source
findings.behaviour: saveDetail(): requires LPO status=="PENDING" (else "Could not edit. only PENDING LPO can be edited"). Item must exist. HARD reject if the (supplier,item) pair has no SupplierItemPrice row ("Item not valid for this supplier"). For new detail, duplicate item on same LPO is rejected ("Duplicates items are not allowed"). The line price is NOT taken from request - it is copied from supplierItemPrice.getPrice(). The SupplierItemPrice.active flag is NOT checked.

requestRequestOrderNo(): id = localPurchaseOrderRepository.getLastId()+1 where getLastId is @Query SELECT MAX(lpo.id). On empty table MAX returns null -> NPE caught by empty catch -> id stays 1. Number = Formater.formatWithCurrentDate("LPO", id) = "LPO" + yyyyMMdd + "-" + id (NO zero-padding on the id). The number is requested by a SEPARATE GET endpoint (request_no) and supplied by the client in the save body; it is NOT generated server-side inside save().

POST /goods_received_notes/create: looks up LPO by no; requires request store id == LPO store id ("Order not designated to the selected store") and LPO status=="SUBMITTED" ("Could not create GRN. Local Purchase Order not submitted"). Only then calls service.create.

findings.topic: GRN create - one-GRN-per-LPO + initial state + line seeding
findings.behaviour: create(): double-checks no existing GRN for the LPO (findByLocalPurchaseOrder AND existsByLocalPurchaseOrder, both throw same message). New GRN: no="NA" placeholder, store=LPO.store, status="PENDING", statusDescription="GRN Pending for verification". Saved once to get id, then no is set to generated GRN no and saved again. For each LPO detail it creates a GRN detail: orderedQty=lpoDetail.qty, receivedQty=0, price=lpoDetail.price, status="NOT VERIFIED". OneToOne LPO link is updatable=false.

findings.topic: GRN header state machine - only PENDING and APPROVED exist
findings.behaviour: GRN header is only ever set to PENDING (create) or APPROVED (approve). The GoodsReceivedNoteService interface declares ONLY create() and approve() - there is NO verify/reject/return at the GRN header level. The worklist endpoint filters on statuses PENDING/VERIFIED/APPROVED/REJECTED/SUBMITTED/RETURNED, but VERIFIED/REJECTED/SUBMITTED/RETURNED are never assigned to a GRN header anywhere - that status list is a copy-paste FILTER, not evidence of reachable GRN states.

POST save_detail_qty: requires GRN header status=="PENDING"; detail not already "VERIFIED"; detail belongs to the GRN; submitted orderedQty must equal stored orderedQty ("Ordered qty does not match..."); receivedQty>=0 ("must not be less than zero"); receivedQty<=orderedQty ("must not exceed ordered qty"). Persists receivedQty.

findings.topic: GRN detail VERIFIED transition + batch-quantity reconciliation
findings.behaviour: POST verify_detail_qty: same guards as save_detail_qty PLUS requires submitted receivedQty == stored receivedQty ("Qty Mismatch... Please save Qty before verifying") AND sum of all GoodsReceivedNoteDetailBatch.qty == detail.receivedQty ("Batch quantities are not equal to total received quantities"). Then sets detail.status="VERIFIED". This batch-sum==receivedQty check is the ONLY quantity reconciliation in the whole lane.

add_batch: detail must exist, qty>0 ("Qty must be more than zero"); creates GoodsReceivedNoteDetailBatch (no, manufacturedDate, expiryDate, qty). No check that detail is still PENDING/NOT VERIFIED here. delete_batch: deletes by id if found. No status guard on delete.

findings.topic: GRN approval is one @Transactional method doing all stock effects
findings.behaviour: approve(): guard GRN status=="PENDING" ("Not a pending GRN"); guard EVERY detail status=="VERIFIED" ("All the items in the GRN must be verified before approving"). Per detail: reads StoreItem via findByStoreAndItem and calls .get() WITHOUT empty-check (NPE/NoSuchElement if the item has never existed in that store); newStock=originalStock+receivedQty; saves StoreItem. The whole method is @Transactional (class-level javax.transaction.Transactional).

findings.topic: GRN approval - stock card write timing/condition
findings.behaviour: StoreStockCard row is written inside the same approve loop ONLY when detail.receivedQty>0: qtyIn=receivedQty, qtyOut=0, balance=newStock, reference="Goods received GRN# "+grn.no. If receivedQty==0 NO stock card row is written (but stock and batch copy still run for that path; stock add of 0 is a no-op).

findings.topic: GRN approval - StoreItemBatch creation (no merge, raw copy)
findings.behaviour: For each GoodsReceivedNoteDetailBatch on the detail, a NEW StoreItemBatch is created and saved (no, manufacturedDate, expiryDate, qty, store, item). It does NOT merge into an existing batch with same no/expiry - every GRN batch becomes a distinct StoreItemBatch row. This is the row that downstream FEFO consumption reads (expiryDate is carried here).

Inside the `if(localPurchaseOrder != null)` block, per detail with receivedQty>0 a Purchase row is written: qty=receivedQty, amount=receivedQty*price (double multiplication), linked to the GRN. If GRN has no LPO, NO Purchase rows are written and LPO is not touched. Purchase amount precision is raw double, not BigDecimal.

approve() saves the APPROVED GRN but RETURNS null (explicit `return null;`). The resource wraps this in ResponseEntity.created(...).body(null), so the approve endpoint returns an empty body despite the method signature promising a GoodsReceivedNote.

requestRequestGrnNo(): id = goodsReceivedNoteRepository.getLastId()+1, getLastId = @Query SELECT MAX(grn.id). Empty-table null caught by empty catch -> id=1. no = Formater.formatWithCurrentDate("GRN", id) = "GRN"+yyyyMMdd+"-"+id, NO zero-padding. Unlike LPO, GRN no is assigned server-side inside create() (line 86) after the first save.

SupplierItemPrice is a flat persistent table keyed by (supplier,item) with fields price(double), terms(String), active(boolean default true). Uniqueness enforced at app layer (existsBySupplierAndItem -> "Duplicate values are not allowed"). SupplierItemPriceList is a NON-entity DTO (plain @Data class) bundling one Supplier + its List<SupplierItemPrice>. There is no notion of price effective dates, tiers, or quantity breaks.

findings.topic: Supplier item price save vs save_or_update + price update path
findings.behaviour: save: validates supplier+item by id, blocks duplicate, stamps createdBy/On/At in service. save_or_update: looks up item by CODE; if (supplier,item) price exists it updates ONLY price and terms (active flag and createdAt untouched), else creates new. update() does not re-validate or re-stamp anything.

Supplier.code is user-supplied, sanitized via Sanitizer.sanitizeString and has spaces stripped; NOT auto-generated. code and name are unique columns. On create, active is forced true. There is NO supplier code sequence/prefix scheme.

findings.topic: LPO line price arithmetic / data type
findings.behaviour: LocalPurchaseOrderDetail.qty and price are primitive double; GRN detail orderedQty/receivedQty/price are primitive double; Purchase.amount computed as double receivedQty*price. No BigDecimal anywhere in this lane; all monetary/quantity math is binary floating point.

absences.expected: Three-way match (LPO qty vs GRN qty vs supplier invoice qty)
absences.searched: Grep for SupplierInvoice/supplierInvoice/invoiceQty/threeWay/matchQty/invoiceQuantity across com.orbix.api (0 hits); read GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl, GoodsReceivedNoteResource, LocalPurchaseOrderServiceImpl in full. NOT FOUND - there is no supplier-invoice entity and no invoice-quantity comparison. The only reconciliation is GRN line receivedQty<=orderedQty (GoodsReceivedNoteResource.java:208-210) and sum(batch.qty)==receivedQty (GoodsReceivedNoteResource.java:258-264). A true three-way match would be a NEW feature, not legacy behaviour.

absences.expected: GRN header VERIFIED / REJECTED / RETURNED / SUBMITTED states
absences.searched: Grep setStatus across all GoodsReceivedNote* files: header only ever set PENDING (line 77) or APPROVED (line 194). GoodsReceivedNoteService interface declares only create+approve. NOT FOUND as reachable header states - they appear only in the worklist status filter list (GoodsReceivedNoteResource.java:81-89), which is a copy-paste filter.

absences.expected: GRN-driven partial / multiple receipts against one LPO
absences.searched: GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.create lines 66-72 enforce ONE GRN per LPO (findByLocalPurchaseOrder + existsByLocalPurchaseOrder). NOT FOUND - a single GRN per LPO; partial receipt is modeled only via receivedQty<orderedQty on that single GRN, with no second-receipt mechanism and no back-order/remaining-qty tracking.

absences.expected: Negative-stock prevention gate on GRN approval
absences.searched: GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.approve lines 126-132 only ADD to stock (newStock=originalStock+receivedQty); no negative-stock branch needed/found here. Note StoreItem lookup uses .get() without isEmpty() check (line 127-128) - NPE risk if item never existed in the store, which is an implicit precondition, not an explicit guard.

absences.expected: LPO total / grand-total amount field
absences.searched: Read LocalPurchaseOrder.java and LocalPurchaseOrderDetail.java in full - no total/grandTotal/amount field on the order; only per-line qty*price implied, never summed/persisted. NOT FOUND - any LPO total must be computed in UI/report.

absences.expected: Enforcement of SupplierItemPrice.active flag and Supplier.active flag in procurement
absences.searched: LocalPurchaseOrderServiceImpl.saveDetail (lines 248-251) checks only existence of the (supplier,item) price, not its active flag; supplier active flag never re-checked in save/verify/approve. NOT FOUND - active flags exist as data but are not used as gates in the LPO/GRN flow.

absences.expected: Zero-padding / fiscal-year reset in LPO and GRN numbers
absences.searched: Formater.formatWithCurrentDate (Formater.java:14-17) only concatenates prefix+yyyyMMdd+"-"+suffix with no padding; the suffix is MAX(id)+1 which is a global monotonic id, never reset per year. NOT FOUND - no zero-padding and no fiscal-year sequence reset for LPO/GRN.

absences.expected: Hibernate Envers @Audited on procurement entities
absences.searched: Reviewed annotations on GoodsReceivedNote, GoodsReceivedNoteDetail, GoodsReceivedNoteDetailBatch, LocalPurchaseOrder, LocalPurchaseOrderDetail, Supplier, SupplierItemPrice, Purchase - none carry @Audited. Consistent with project memory that Envers is a bundled-but-unused dependency. (Lane-scoped confirmation; the engagement-wide @Audited scan is the early-discovery obligation.)

verdicts.planClaim: LPO lifecycle: DRAFT -> PENDING -> VERIFIED -> APPROVED -> SUBMITTED -> RECEIVED; terminal REJECTED, RETURNED (doc line 29).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy LocalPurchaseOrderServiceImpl.save sets the initial status to 'PENDING' on create (id==null), line 84 ('PENDING' / 'Order pending for verification'). There is NO DRAFT state anywhere in the service. The rest of the chain is exact: verify() guards status=='PENDING' -> VERIFIED (lines 117-131); approve() guards 'VERIFIED' -> APPROVED (145-159); submit() guards 'APPROVED' -> SUBMITTED (165-183); RECEIVED is set by GRN approval not by LPO (GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.approve line 184). _return() (189-204) and reject() set RETURNED/REJECTED but ONLY from source PENDING or VERIFIED. So the chain is right minus the phantom DRAFT head, and the doc omits the constraint that REJECTED/RETURNED are reachable only from PENDING/VERIFIED (not from APPROVED/SUBMITTED).
verdicts.correction: Remove DRAFT. Legacy initial state is PENDING. State machine is PENDING -> VERIFIED -> APPROVED -> SUBMITTED -> (RECEIVED, set by GRN approval). Add the source-state constraint: REJECTED and RETURNED are only reachable from PENDING or VERIFIED; APPROVED and SUBMITTED LPOs cannot be rejected or returned. Also note RETURNED has no re-open transition in legacy (no method accepts RETURNED as source) so it is effectively terminal as-built; if the rebuild adds a re-open that is NEW SCOPE and needs a change request.

verdicts.planClaim: GRN lifecycle: PENDING -> VERIFIED -> APPROVED; terminal REJECTED, RETURNED (doc line 30).
verdicts.evidence: At the GRN HEADER level the legacy only ever sets PENDING (GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.java:77) and APPROVED (line 194) - confirmed by grep of setStatus( in that file (only 77, 98, 184, 194; 184 is the LPO and 98 is the detail). The GoodsReceivedNoteService interface declares ONLY create() and approve() - there is no header verify/reject/return. VERIFIED in legacy belongs to the GRN DETAIL line (grnDetail.setStatus('NOT VERIFIED') line 98, flipped to 'VERIFIED' via the verify_detail_qty resource endpoint), not to the GRN header/aggregate the doc is describing. REJECTED and RETURNED are NEVER assigned to a GRN header anywhere - they appear only in the worklist status-filter list in GoodsReceivedNoteResource (a copy-paste filter, not reachable states).
verdicts.correction: The GRN HEADER state machine is PENDING -> APPROVED only. There is no header-level VERIFIED, REJECTED or RETURNED state. Verification happens per LINE (GoodsReceivedNoteDetail.status: NOT VERIFIED -> VERIFIED), and approve() hard-guards that EVERY detail line is VERIFIED before the header can go APPROVED. If the rebuild wants header REJECTED/RETURNED states they are NEW SCOPE requiring a change request, not legacy reproduction.

verdicts.planClaim: Stock credited to StoreItemBatch + StoreStockBalance + StoreStockMovement(RECEIPT) + LPO->RECEIVED, all in ONE @Transactional on GRN.approve(); no async event boundary (doc lines 30, 115, exact-process target 4).
verdicts.evidence: GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl is @Transactional at class level (line 47) and approve() (110-204) performs ALL effects synchronously in one method: per detail increments StoreItem.stock (128-132), writes a StoreStockCard row when receivedQty>0 (135-149), copies each GoodsReceivedNoteDetailBatch into a new StoreItemBatch (152-163), writes a Purchase ledger row when receivedQty>0 (167-183), then flips the LPO to RECEIVED (184-190) and the GRN header to APPROVED (194-201). No event/async boundary exists. The one-transaction, no-async claim is ACCURATE. Corrections: (a) 'StoreStockBalance' and 'StoreStockMovement(RECEIPT)' are MODERN re-modellings of legacy StoreItem.stock (running balance field) and StoreStockCard (ledger row) - behaviour preserved, names changed, so this is fine but must be documented as a rename not a new concept; (b) legacy writes the StockCard/Movement row ONLY when receivedQty>0 (line 135) - a zero-received line writes NO ledger row - the rebuild must preserve that conditional; (c) the doc OMITS the Purchase ledger row, which is a real legacy side-effect written inside the same approve transaction (amount = receivedQty*price) and feeds purchase reports.
verdicts.correction: Note StoreStockBalance == legacy StoreItem.stock and StoreStockMovement(RECEIPT) == legacy StoreStockCard (rename, behaviour preserved). Add the conditional: a stock-card/movement row is written only when a detail line's receivedQty > 0. Add the missing fourth/fifth write: a Purchase ledger row per detail with receivedQty>0 (qty=receivedQty, amount=receivedQty*price), written inside the same @Transactional approve() and only when the GRN is linked to an LPO. The doc's 'four writes' (StoreItemBatch + StoreStockBalance + StoreStockMovement + LPO->RECEIVED) understates the legacy effect set.

verdicts.planClaim: Document numbers: GRN{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} via seq_grn_no; LPO{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} via seq_lpo_no (doc lines 117-119, exact-process target 5).
verdicts.evidence: Formater.formatWithCurrentDate (Formater.java:14-17) returns prefix + yyyyMMdd + '-' + suffix. Legacy uses prefix 'LPO' (LocalPurchaseOrderServiceImpl.requestRequestOrderNo) and 'GRN' (GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.requestRequestGrnNo lines 207-214), so the OUTPUT FORMAT GRN{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} and LPO{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} is reproduced exactly. BUT the legacy suffix is MAX(id)+1 (a global monotonic JPA id, getLastId() = SELECT MAX(id)), NOT a dedicated sequence; the value is NOT zero-padded; and there is NO fiscal-year reset. Replacing the racy MAX(id)+1 with a PostgreSQL sequence (seq_grn_no/seq_lpo_no via DocumentNumberService) is a DELIBERATE remediation already captured by the lessons brief (Legacy finding C / ADR-0009 §5) - that is acceptable as an approved change, not drift, because the observable output format is preserved.
verdicts.correction: Confirm the {seq} component is NOT zero-padded (legacy emits the raw integer, e.g. LPO20260605-7 not LPO20260605-0000007) and does NOT reset per fiscal year, so output parity with 3 years of legacy numbers holds. Document the MAX(id)+1 -> PostgreSQL-sequence swap explicitly in the change-request register as an intentional concurrency-correctness deviation (the output is identical; the generation mechanism changes). Also note seq value in legacy is a GLOBAL id-derived counter, so historical legacy numbers are not contiguous per-prefix - the migrated/new sequence need only avoid collisions, not reproduce the exact historical integers.

verdicts.planClaim: ThreeWayMatch: LPO orderedQty == GRN receivedQty == SupplierInvoice qty per line; payment release gated on PASS; supplier invoice qty input at GRN detail level (doc lines 32, 89, 132, exact-process target 8, DoD line 171).
