verdicts.evidence: Grep for SupplierInvoice|invoiceQty|threeWay|matchQty|invoiceQuantity|ThreeWayMatch across the entire legacy com.orbix.api returned ZERO hits. There is no supplier-invoice entity, no invoice-quantity field, and no payment-release gate anywhere in the procurement lane. The ONLY quantity reconciliation in legacy is: (1) GRN line receivedQty <= orderedQty (GoodsReceivedNoteResource save_detail_qty / verify_detail_qty), and (2) sum(GoodsReceivedNoteDetailBatch.qty) == detail.receivedQty before a line can be VERIFIED. That is a two-way (ordered vs received) line check plus a batch-sum integrity check - NOT a three-way match. There is no third document (supplier invoice) and no payment workflow in this lane at all.
verdicts.correction: Three-way match has NO legacy basis and must be labelled NEW SCOPE in the change-request register, exactly like the M22 claims-workflow precedent. Do NOT write its acceptance criteria as legacy reproduction. The legacy-reproduction requirement is the two-way line check (receivedQty <= orderedQty) plus the batch-sum integrity rule (sum of GRN detail batch qty == receivedQty before a line goes VERIFIED). If a SupplierInvoice entity, invoice-qty capture at GRN detail level, and a payment-release gate are genuinely wanted, raise a change request, get engagement-lead approval, and tag the stories safety-critical (financial). Until then, remove ThreeWayMatch from the scope/aggregate list (line 32), the /three-way-match/validate endpoint (line 89), exact-process target 8, PROCESS.md §16.11, and the DoD line that claims a three-way match endpoint reproduces legacy.

newDriftFound: Purchase ledger row write OMITTED from the plan: legacy GRN.approve() writes a Purchase row per detail with receivedQty>0 (qty=receivedQty, amount=receivedQty*price, linked to the GRN) inside the same approve transaction, only when the GRN is linked to an LPO (GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.java:167-183). The doc's exact-process target 4 lists only StoreItemBatch + StoreStockBalance + StoreStockMovement + LPO->RECEIVED and never mentions the Purchase ledger - this feeds the legacy purchase/daily-purchase reports and must be reproduced or consciously deferred via change request.

Stock-card / StoreStockMovement is written ONLY when receivedQty>0 (line 135). A zero-received GRN line still copies batches and runs the stock add (a no-op) but writes NO ledger row. The plan's atomic-write description implies an unconditional RECEIPT movement per line; the rebuild must reproduce the receivedQty>0 conditional or it will emit phantom zero-qty movement rows.

GRN detail line state machine (NOT VERIFIED -> VERIFIED) is not modelled in the plan. Legacy verification is per-LINE: each GoodsReceivedNoteDetail starts 'NOT VERIFIED' (line 98) and must reach 'VERIFIED' (resource verify_detail_qty) before approve() will let the header go APPROVED (approve guards every detail status=='VERIFIED', lines 120-124). The plan collapses this to a header-level VERIFIED state that does not exist. The line-level NOT VERIFIED/VERIFIED status and its guards must be a story.

GRN detail batch-sum integrity rule not in plan: a GRN line cannot be VERIFIED unless sum(GoodsReceivedNoteDetailBatch.qty) == detail.receivedQty (GoodsReceivedNoteResource verify_detail_qty). This is the real legacy quantity-reconciliation gate (the plan instead describes a phantom three-way match). Needs its own acceptance criteria.

One-GRN-per-LPO constraint not in plan: legacy create() rejects a second GRN for the same LPO (findByLocalPurchaseOrder + existsByLocalPurchaseOrder, lines 66-72). There is NO partial/multiple-receipt mechanism and NO back-order/remaining-qty tracking; partial receipt is modelled only via receivedQty<orderedQty on the single GRN. The doc's 'close-out an LPO' language must not imply multi-receipt - if multi-receipt is wanted it is NEW SCOPE.

GRN-create precondition lives at the resource layer, not the service: legacy requires request store id == LPO store id and LPO status=='SUBMITTED' before a GRN can be created (GoodsReceivedNoteResource.java:97-117). The plan's POST /grns has no documented store-match or SUBMITTED-LPO guard - both are load-bearing legacy preconditions that must be acceptance criteria.

LPO edit is near-frozen: legacy save() on an existing LPO only allows editing when status=='PENDING' and then updates ONLY validUntil - order date, store, supplier, no, and details are NOT editable (LocalPurchaseOrderServiceImpl.java:88-103). The plan does not capture this edit-immutability rule. Detail add/edit is also PENDING-only (saveDetail guard).

LPO detail price is copied from SupplierItemPrice, not from the request, and a (supplier,item) without a SupplierItemPrice row is HARD-rejected ('Item not valid for this supplier'); duplicate item on the same LPO is rejected (LocalPurchaseOrderServiceImpl saveDetail, ~231-273). These pricing-source and validity rules are not in the plan and are safety-critical (financial).

SupplierItemPrice.active flag and Supplier.active flag are NOT enforced as gates anywhere in the legacy LPO/GRN flow (they exist as data only). If the rebuild starts gating on active flags that is a behaviour change requiring a change request; the plan is silent on this and should not silently add active-flag gating.

Floating-point money in legacy: every qty/price/amount in this lane is primitive double (LocalPurchaseOrderDetail, GoodsReceivedNoteDetail, Purchase.amount = receivedQty*price). The double->BigDecimal change is pre-approved per project memory, but the plan's NUMERIC(19,6) choice should be cross-referenced to that standing change-request so QA parity tolerances are set correctly against legacy floating-point outputs.

LPO has no persisted total/grand-total field (confirmed by reading LocalPurchaseOrder + LocalPurchaseOrderDetail in full per ground truth). Any LPO total is computed in UI/report. If the rebuild persists an order total that is a (minor) new field; ensure it is not treated as legacy-sourced.

Legacy GRN approve() returns null (explicit return null at line 203) despite the signature, so the legacy approve endpoint responds with an empty body. This is a known legacy bug, NOT behaviour to reproduce - the rebuild should return the approved GRN; flag so QA does not write a parity test asserting an empty approve response.

RBAC / 177-privilege-code claim (doc line 100, 173, DoD): this lane's ground truth includes a confirmation that NONE of the procurement entities carry @Audited (Envers bundled-but-unused) and the lane evidence does not include any @PreAuthorize privilege-code inventory. The '177 privilege codes' / 'all endpoints @PreAuthorize' claim is UNVERIFIABLE from this lane and is exactly the inc-06 phantom-RBAC pattern (177-code claim was phantom there); it must be independently re-derived from the legacy authority/privilege tables before being accepted, not carried over on faith.

extract.lane: Document numbering across all pharmacy/inventory/procurement document types
extract.summary: All server-assigned procurement/inventory document numbers are built by the shared helper Formater.formatWithCurrentDate(prefix, suffix) = prefix + LocalDateTime.now() formatted "yyyyMMdd" + "-" + suffix, where suffix is the RAW id.toString() with NO zero-padding (accessories/Formater.java:14-17). The sequence source for every server-assigned type is the legacy MAX(id)+1 race pattern: each service calls its own repository's getLastId() (a @Query "SELECT MAX(x.id) FROM Entity") and adds 1, wrapped in try/catch defaulting to 1L on empty table. There is NO DB sequence and NO dedicated counter table anywhere. Two distinct numbering ownership models exist: (a) TRANSFER ORDERS (TO), RECEIVING NOTES (RN) and GRN are assigned server-side inside the create method; (b) REQUEST ORDERS (RO: PharmacyToPharmacyRO/PPR, PharmacyToStoreRO/PSR) are NOT numbered by the service at all — the no field is supplied by the client (frontend calls requestRequestOrderNo() to preview a number, then posts it back); the only setNo on the RO model is inside commented-out code. The documented SPT collision is CONFIRMED: PharmacyToPharmacyTO and StoreToPharmacyTO both emit literal prefix "SPT" but draw their sequence from two different MAX(id) repositories, so the two tables can independently produce identical document numbers. PharmacySaleOrder does not use Formater at all — it uses a Math.random() placeholder then "PSO/"+id.

findings.topic: Formater helper (date format + NO zero padding)
findings.behaviour: formatWithCurrentDate(prefix, suffix) returns prefix + DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyyMMdd").format(LocalDateTime.now()) + "-" + suffix. The suffix is the raw sequence id.toString() — it is NOT passed through formatThree/formatSix/formatNine, so there is NO zero-padding on any document number using this helper. Resulting shape: PREFIX + yyyyMMdd + '-' + <unpadded id>. Example shape: SPT20260605-42.

findings.topic: Sequence source = MAX(id)+1 race pattern (NOT a sequence/counter table)
findings.behaviour: Every server-assigned document number is derived from <entityRepository>.getLastId()+1, where getLastId() is a JPA @Query 'SELECT MAX(x.id) FROM Entity'. There is no DB sequence and no dedicated counter table. Concurrent creates can read the same MAX and collide. Confirmed getLastId queries: GoodsReceivedNoteRepository.java:23-24 (SELECT MAX(grn.id)), LocalPurchaseOrderRepository.java:21-22 (MAX(lpo.id)), PharmacyToPharmacyRORepository.java:20-21 (MAX(p.id)), PharmacyToPharmacyRNRepository.java:20-21 (MAX(p.id)), PharmacyToPharmacyTORepository.java:20-21 (MAX(s.id)), PharmacyToStoreRORepository.java:22-23 (MAX(p.id)), StoreToPharmacyTORepository.java:20-21 (MAX(s.id)), StoreToPharmacyRNRepository.java:19-20 (MAX(s.id)).

Prefix literal "GRN". Built via Formater.formatWithCurrentDate("GRN", id.toString()) where id = goodsReceivedNoteRepository.getLastId()+1 (requestRequestGrnNo()). Server-assigned. NOTE OFF-BY-ONE QUIRK: in createGRN the entity is first saved with placeholder no="NA" (line 74), saved (line 84), and only THEN is requestRequestGrnNo() called (line 86) — so getLastId() already includes this row's own id, making the number's sequence = own_id+1 (one higher than its own primary key). Entity then re-saved (line 88).
Prefix literal "LPO". Built via Formater.formatWithCurrentDate("LPO", id.toString()) where id = localPurchaseOrderRepository.getLastId()+1 (method requestRequestOrderNo()).

Prefix literal "PPR". requestRequestOrderNo() builds Formater.formatWithCurrentDate("PPR", (pharmacyToPharmacyRORepository.getLastId()+1).toString()). CLIENT-SUPPLIED: the save() method NEVER sets no server-side; the only setNo(ro.getNo()) at lines 114 and 329 are inside commented-out model-building blocks. The persisted no comes from the client payload (frontend previews it via requestRequestOrderNo()).

Prefix literal "SPT". requestTransferOrderNo() builds Formater.formatWithCurrentDate("SPT", (pharmacyToPharmacyTORepository.getLastId()+1).toString()). Server-assigned: order.setNo(this.requestTransferOrderNo().getNo()) is called at line 87 BEFORE the first save (line 99), so getLastId()+1 reflects the correct next id at assignment time.

Prefix literal "PPRN". requestReceivingNoteNo() builds Formater.formatWithCurrentDate("PPRN", (pharmacyToPharmacyRNRepository.getLastId()+1).toString()). Server-assigned: note.setNo(this.requestReceivingNoteNo().getNo()) at line 99.

Prefix literal "PSR". requestRequestOrderNo() builds Formater.formatWithCurrentDate("PSR", (pharmacyToStoreRORepository.getLastId()+1).toString()). CLIENT-SUPPLIED like PPR: only setNo at lines 109 and 324 which are inside commented-out model code; service does not assign no server-side.

findings.topic: StoreToPharmacy TO (transfer order) number — SPT COLLISION
findings.behaviour: Prefix literal "SPT" — SAME literal as PharmacyToPharmacyTO. requestTransferOrderNo() builds Formater.formatWithCurrentDate("SPT", (storeToPharmacyTORepository.getLastId()+1).toString()). Sequence drawn from storeToPharmacyTORepository.getLastId() (StoreToPharmacyTO.id), which is a DIFFERENT table from PharmacyToPharmacyTO. Server-assigned via order.setNo(...) at line 93 before save (line ~?). CONFIRMS legacy finding D: both TO types share prefix SPT but use independent MAX(id) counters, so SPT<date>-<n> can be produced identically by both tables.

Prefix literal "PGRN" (note: NOT 'GRN' and NOT 'STRN'). requestReceivingNoteNo() builds Formater.formatWithCurrentDate("PGRN", (storeToPharmacyRNRepository.getLastId()+1).toString()). Server-assigned: note.setNo(this.requestReceivingNoteNo().getNo()) at line 105.

Does NOT use Formater and has NO date component. On create, no is first set to String.valueOf(Math.random()) placeholder (line 3344), the entity is saved to obtain its id, then no is set to literal "PSO/"+pharmacySaleOrder.getId() (line 3352) and re-saved. So the final format is 'PSO/<id>' (forward slash, raw primary key, no date, no padding). On edit (order.getId()!=null) the no is left unchanged.
Not a procurement doc but generated in the same savePharmacySaleOrder flow: placeholder no=Math.random(), save, then no="PCST"+pharmacyCustomer.getId(), re-save. Format 'PCST<id>'.

absences.expected: A separate StoreToPharmacyRO service/number type (RO prefix for the store->pharmacy request side)
absences.searched: service/ directory glob and grep for formatWithCurrentDate; the store->pharmacy request order is owned by PharmacyToStoreROServiceImpl (prefix PSR). There is no StoreToPharmacyROServiceImpl. The store->pharmacy cycle is: PharmacyToStoreRO (PSR, request) -> StoreToPharmacyTO (SPT, transfer) -> StoreToPharmacyRN (PGRN, receive).

absences.expected: A separate PharmacyToStoreRN service/number type
absences.searched: service/ glob; only PharmacyToStoreROServiceImpl exists on that axis. The receiving leg is StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl (PGRN). No PharmacyToStoreRN class found.

absences.expected: Zero-padding of the numeric suffix in any procurement document number
absences.searched: accessories/Formater.java:14-17 — formatWithCurrentDate concatenates the raw suffix; formatThree/formatFive/formatSix/formatNine padding helpers exist (lines 18-102) but are NOT invoked by any of the GRN/LPO/PPR/PPRN/SPT/PSR/PGRN number builders. Suffix is raw id.toString().

absences.expected: A DB sequence or dedicated counter/numbering table for document numbers
absences.searched: grep getLastId across repositories/ — every match is a JPA @Query 'SELECT MAX(<alias>.id)'; no @SequenceGenerator-backed number, no NumberSeries/Counter entity used in these flows. Confirmed MAX(id)+1 race pattern only.

absences.expected: Fiscal-year reset logic in document numbers
absences.searched: accessories/Formater.java (full file) and all requestXxxNo() methods — the only date element is the literal yyyyMMdd of now(); the sequence (MAX(id)+1) is global/monotonic and never resets by year. No fiscal-year reset exists.

absences.expected: Server-side assignment of the RO (PPR/PSR) document number
absences.searched: PharmacyToPharmacyROServiceImpl.java save() (lines 56-108) and PharmacyToStoreROServiceImpl.java — no server-side setNo on the persisted entity; the only setNo calls (114/329 and 109/324) are within commented-out model blocks. RO numbers are therefore client-supplied (trust-the-client), unlike TO/RN/GRN.

verdicts.planClaim: Eight doc types each get {PREFIX}{yyyyMMdd EAT}-{seq} (doc lines 117-126, 167; ADR-0009 §5).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy Formater.formatWithCurrentDate(prefix, suffix) = prefix + LocalDateTime.now() 'yyyyMMdd' + '-' + suffix (accessories/Formater.java:14-17). The {PREFIX}{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} SHAPE is reproduced faithfully. TWO corrections: (1) the legacy suffix is the RAW id.toString() with NO zero-padding (formatThree/Six/Nine padding helpers exist at Formater.java:18-102 but are never invoked by the number builders); the doc's '{seq}' must likewise be unpadded to preserve the legacy string shape. (2) Legacy uses bare LocalDateTime.now() (JVM-local), not an explicit EAT zone; ADR-0009 §7 correctly documents the move to LocalDate.now(Africa/Dar_es_Salaam) as a deliberate timezone-correctness change, not a legacy reproduction. The shape is right; the 'EAT' qualifier is a documented modern fix, not legacy behaviour.
verdicts.correction: State explicitly that {seq} is rendered UNPADDED (raw id.toString() in legacy; legacy example shape SPT20260605-42). Note that 'EAT timezone' is a deliberate modern correction over legacy's bare now(), per ADR-0009 §7 — not a reproduced legacy behaviour.

verdicts.planClaim: Each number is produced through DocumentNumberService.next(DocType) backed by a dedicated PostgreSQL sequence (ADR-0009 §5, doc line 143).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy has NO DB sequence and NO counter table anywhere: every server-assigned number is <repo>.getLastId()+1 where getLastId() is a JPA @Query 'SELECT MAX(x.id) FROM Entity', wrapped try/catch defaulting to 1L (GoodsReceivedNoteRepository.java:23-24, LocalPurchaseOrderRepository.java:21-22, PharmacyToPharmacyTORepository.java:20-21, StoreToPharmacyTORepository.java:20-21, etc.). The DocumentNumberService + PostgreSQL SEQUENCE design is therefore a CLEAN-BREAK MODERN FIX, not a reproduction. This is legitimate and correctly framed in ADR-0009 §5 / 'Exact-process impact' as replacing an implementation defect (the MAX(id)+1 race) while preserving the output format. Marked WITH_CORRECTIONS only to be explicit: do not describe this as 'how the legacy numbers' — the legacy mechanism is MAX(id)+1.
verdicts.correction: Keep the sequence design, but ensure the doc never implies legacy used a sequence. Legacy mechanism = repository.getLastId()+1 (MAX(id)+1). The sequence is the remediation.

verdicts.planClaim: ...inside the insert tx, with no double-save (ADR-0009 §5(c), doc line 143).
verdicts.evidence: Confirmed as a deliberate improvement over a legacy defect, correctly labelled. Legacy GRN DOES double-save: createGRN saves the row with placeholder no='NA' (GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.java:74,84), THEN calls requestRequestGrnNo() (line 86) and re-saves (line 88) — an off-by-one quirk where getLastId() already counts the row's own id. ADR-0009 §5 and 'Not reproduced (implementation defects)' explicitly reject the save-then-assign double-save. The single-insert design is sound and honestly scoped as not-reproduced legacy behaviour.

verdicts.planClaim: Prefix set: GRN, LPO, PPR, PPTO, PPRN, PSR, SPTO, PGRN (doc lines 118-125, 144, 167).
verdicts.evidence: Verified prefix-by-prefix against legacy: GRN literal 'GRN' (GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.java:207-214); LPO literal 'LPO' (LocalPurchaseOrderServiceImpl.java:325-334); P2P RO literal 'PPR' (PharmacyToPharmacyROServiceImpl.java:372-381); P2P RN literal 'PPRN' (PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:322-329); P2S RO literal 'PSR' (PharmacyToStoreROServiceImpl.java:368-375); StoreToPharmacy RN literal 'PGRN' (StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:332-341) — NOT 'GRN' and NOT 'STRN'. Six of the eight prefixes (GRN, LPO, PPR, PPRN, PSR, PGRN) are reproduced VERBATIM from legacy — accurate. The two TO prefixes (PPTO, SPTO) are NEW: legacy emits literal 'SPT' for BOTH (see SPT-collision verdict). The doc honestly labels PPTO/SPTO as 'replaces legacy SPT' (lines 121,124,144) and ADR-0009 §6 gates them on product-owner sign-off. Correction is only to be explicit that PPTO/SPTO are deviations, while the other six are verbatim.
verdicts.correction: Annotate that GRN/LPO/PPR/PPRN/PSR/PGRN are verbatim-legacy prefixes, whereas PPTO and SPTO are change-controlled replacements for the single legacy literal 'SPT'. The set is otherwise correct.

verdicts.planClaim: Pharmacy-to-Pharmacy TO uses seq_ptp_no; StoreToPharmacy TO uses seq_sto_no (doc lines 121,124).
verdicts.evidence: Internally consistent with ADR-0009 prefix table (lines 133-134: StoreToPharmacy TO=SPTO=seq_sto_no, PharmacyToPharmacy TO=PPTO=seq_ptp_no) and the Flyway sequence block (lines 104-105). No legacy sequence names exist to contradict (legacy has no sequences), so the only verifiable property is internal consistency of the new scheme — which holds. The earlier inc-06-style risk of swapped sequence names does not occur here.

verdicts.planClaim: Legacy finding C: legacy used MAX(id)+1 race for document numbers (doc line 143).
verdicts.evidence: Directly confirmed by ground truth. Every server-assigned number is repository.getLastId()+1 with getLastId() = @Query 'SELECT MAX(x.id) FROM Entity' — GoodsReceivedNoteRepository.java:23-24, LocalPurchaseOrderRepository.java:21-22, PharmacyToPharmacyTORepository.java:20-21, StoreToPharmacyTORepository.java:20-21, and the rest. No DB sequence, no counter table (absence confirmed by grep across repositories/). Concurrent creates can read the same MAX and collide. Finding C is correct.

verdicts.planClaim: Legacy finding D: legacy SPT collides for both TO types; fresh build must use SPTO (StoreToPharmacy) and PPTO (PharmacyToPharmacy), and any SPT in tests/seed is a defect (doc line 144; ADR-0009 §6).
verdicts.evidence: SPT collision CONFIRMED. PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:87,428 builds Formater.formatWithCurrentDate('SPT', pharmacyToPharmacyTORepository.getLastId()+1). StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:93,431-440 builds Formater.formatWithCurrentDate('SPT', storeToPharmacyTORepository.getLastId()+1). Both emit literal prefix 'SPT' but draw the sequence from two different MAX(id) tables, so e.g. SPT20260605-5 can be produced independently by both. The doc's resolution (PPTO for P2P, SPTO for StoreToPharmacy) and the 'SPT in tests/seed = defect' rule correctly remediate the confirmed collision.

verdicts.planClaim: All eight document types must use server-side DocumentNumberService.next() inside the insert tx (doc line 143 — applied uniformly, incl. PPR and PSR request orders).
verdicts.evidence: For the two REQUEST ORDER types the legacy does NOT assign the number server-side at all. PharmacyToPharmacyROServiceImpl.save() never calls setNo on the persisted entity — the only setNo(ro.getNo()) at lines 114/329 are inside COMMENTED-OUT model-building blocks (cite: PharmacyToPharmacyROServiceImpl.java:372-381 builder + commented 114,329). Same for PharmacyToStoreROServiceImpl (commented setNo at 109/324; PharmacyToStoreROServiceImpl.java:368-375). In legacy the RO 'no' is CLIENT-SUPPLIED: the frontend calls requestRequestOrderNo() to PREVIEW a number, then posts it back in the payload, which is persisted as-is. Routing PPR and PSR through server-side next() inside the insert tx is therefore a behavioural change (trust-the-client → server-authoritative), not a reproduction. The doc presents it as a uniform race-fix for all eight without disclosing that two of the eight were never server-numbered. This is undisclosed scope/behaviour drift.
verdicts.correction: Either (a) document that PPR and PSR (request orders) were legacy client-supplied 'preview-and-post-back' numbers, and that moving them to server-authoritative assignment is a deliberate, change-controlled improvement; or (b) preserve the legacy preview-then-supply contract. The uniform 'all eight assigned server-side, no double-save' statement is correct for GRN/LPO/PPTO/SPTO/PPRN/PGRN (six TO/RN/GRN types) but is NOT a faithful description of legacy PPR/PSR behaviour.

verdicts.planClaim: Implied completeness: these are the relevant pharmacy/procurement document-number types (doc treats the eight as the full numbering surface for this increment).
verdicts.evidence: The eight Formater-based procurement/transfer types match the legacy set (GRN, LPO, PPR, SPT(=PPTO), PPRN, PSR, SPT(=SPTO), PGRN). However the pharmacy SALE document numbers use a DIFFERENT scheme entirely and are NOT covered: PharmacySaleOrder.no = 'PSO/'+id (forward slash, raw PK, NO date, NO Formater — set via Math.random() placeholder then 'PSO/'+id; PatientServiceImpl.java:3344,3350,3352-3354), and PharmacyCustomer.no = 'PCST'+id (PatientServiceImpl.java:3304,3312-3314). Since inc-08 scope (doc line 18) explicitly includes PharmacySaleOrder, its 'PSO/{id}' numbering must be specified and is currently absent from the numbering section.
verdicts.correction: Add the PharmacySaleOrder ('PSO/{id}') and, if in scope, PharmacyCustomer ('PCST{id}') numbering schemes. These do NOT use Formater, have no date component and no '-' separator — they must not be forced into the {PREFIX}{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} pattern. Decide and document whether the fresh build keeps 'PSO/{id}' verbatim or converts it (a change request if converted).

verdicts.planClaim: There is no fiscal-year reset / the sequence is global-monotonic (implicit in {PREFIX}{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} with one sequence per type).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy has NO fiscal-year reset: the only date element is the literal yyyyMMdd of now(), and the MAX(id)+1 sequence is global/monotonic, never resetting by year (absence confirmed across Formater.java and all requestXxxNo() methods). ADR-0009 §5 SEQUENCE ... NO CYCLE starting at 1 reproduces this global-monotonic-with-date-stamp behaviour. The date in the number reflects the calendar day, not a fiscal counter reset, in both legacy and fresh build.

newDriftFound: PPR and PSR request-order numbers are legacy CLIENT-SUPPLIED (preview via requestRequestOrderNo(), posted back in payload; server never setNo on the persisted entity — only commented-out setNo at PharmacyToPharmacyROServiceImpl.java:114,329 and PharmacyToStoreROServiceImpl.java:109,324). The planning doc routes all eight types through server-side DocumentNumberService.next() inside the insert tx without disclosing that two of them were never server-numbered in legacy. inc-08 must either reproduce the preview-and-post-back contract or document the server-authoritative change as a change request.

Zero-padding: legacy suffix is the RAW id.toString() (Formater.java:14-17); the padding helpers formatThree/formatFive/formatSix/formatNine exist (lines 18-102) but are NEVER invoked for any procurement number. The doc's '{seq}' must be unpadded to preserve the exact legacy string shape (e.g. SPT20260605-42, not -000042). inc-08 should assert unpadded sequence rendering in the parity tests.

PharmacySaleOrder numbering ('PSO/'+id; PatientServiceImpl.java:3344,3350,3352-3354) is in inc-08 scope (doc line 18) but absent from the numbering section. It is a wholly different scheme — forward slash, raw PK, no date, no Formater — and must not be coerced into {PREFIX}{yyyyMMdd}-{seq}. Decide keep-verbatim vs convert (the latter being a change request).
PharmacyCustomer numbering ('PCST'+id; PatientServiceImpl.java:3304,3312-3314) rides the same savePharmacySaleOrder flow and is likewise unaddressed; flag for scope inclusion/exclusion.

GRN legacy off-by-one quirk: createGRN saves with no='NA' first (GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.java:74,84), calls requestRequestGrnNo() AFTER the row exists (line 86) so getLastId() already counts the row's own id, then re-saves (line 88) — making the embedded number own_id+1. ADR-0009 §5 correctly DROPS this defect (single insert, sequence-driven), so the fresh-build number will NOT equal legacy-style own_id+1. This is an intentional non-reproduction; ensure no parity test asserts the legacy off-by-one correlation.

There is no StoreToPharmacyRO and no PharmacyToStoreRN class in legacy; the store axis cycle is PharmacyToStoreRO (PSR, request) → StoreToPharmacyTO (SPTO, transfer) → StoreToPharmacyRN (PGRN, receive). The planning doc's entity list (lines 22-23,28) names 'PharmacyToStoreRO' and 'StoreToPharmacyRN' correctly but should ensure no phantom StoreToPharmacyRO/PharmacyToStoreRN number type is invented in the seed/enum.

OUT-OF-LANE (numbering evidence cannot adjudicate): the doc's three-way-match (ThreeWayMatchService, doc lines 32,132), the 177-privilege RBAC claim (line 154), pessimistic-vs-optimistic lock split (ADR-0017 §2, lines 20,145), and the StockBalance/StockMovement entity re-modelling (lines 19-21,27) are NOT covered by the numbering ground truth and were not judged here. Given the inc-06 phantom pattern, these should be sent to their own lanes (locking, rbac, stock-ledger, procurement-match) for adversarial verification — do not assume they are accurate merely because the numbering claims largely hold up.

extract.lane: RBAC / privilege gating + pharmacy session scoping
extract.summary: Legacy authorization is method-level only: Spring `@EnableGlobalMethodSecurity(prePostEnabled=true)` plus per-endpoint `@PreAuthorize("hasAnyAuthority(...)")`. There is NO `.anyRequest().authenticated()` rule (it is commented out in SecurityConfig.java:89), so any endpoint WITHOUT a `@PreAuthorize` is reachable without any privilege — and, because the authorization filter falls through (`filterChain.doFilter`) when no Bearer token is present (CustomAuthorizationFilter.java:105-106), such endpoints are effectively unauthenticated. Privilege coverage is highly uneven across the pharmacy/procurement controllers: LocalPurchaseOrder and GoodsReceivedNote endpoints are gated, but the entire PharmacySaleOrder flow is ungated (the `@PreAuthorize` on save_pharmacy_sale_order is COMMENTED OUT, PharmacyResource.java:298), and many list/get endpoints carry no annotation at all. There is NO server-side pharmacy session scoping: the JWT carries only `subject`(username) + `privileges` claim (CustomAuthenticationFilter.java:78-81), no pharmacy/store. The operating pharmacy is supplied by the CLIENT on every call as a request param (`pharmacy_name`/`pharmacy_id`) or inside the posted entity body, and `Pharmacist` has NO `Pharmacy` affiliation field at all. This confirms PHARM-1: no server-side session scoping for pharmacy exists. (Stores differ: `StorePerson` does have a `@ManyToMany Collection<Store>`, but it is used only as a UI worklist filter, not a write-time hard gate.)

findings.topic: Global security model — method-level only, no blanket authenticated() rule
findings.behaviour: SecurityConfig uses @EnableGlobalMethodSecurity(prePostEnabled=true) and STATELESS sessions. In configure(HttpSecurity), only login/token-refresh/swagger/wms are permitAll; the catch-all `.anyRequest().authenticated()` is COMMENTED OUT. Therefore endpoints are protected ONLY if they carry a method-level @PreAuthorize; any endpoint without one has no authority requirement.

findings.topic: Authorization filter falls through when no token present
findings.behaviour: CustomAuthorizationFilter only sets the SecurityContext when an `Authorization: Bearer` header is present and verifies; if the header is absent it simply calls filterChain.doFilter with NO authentication set (else-branch). Combined with the missing anyRequest().authenticated(), ungated endpoints are reachable anonymously. Token integrity re-check against stored token is commented out; HMAC secret is the literal string 'secret'.
findings.cite: filter/CustomAuthorizationFilter.java:70-107 (else at 105-106; secret at 73; commented integrity check 82-90)

findings.behaviour: successfulAuthentication mints a JWT whose only claims are subject=username and a 'privileges' list (the user's GrantedAuthorities). No pharmacy, store, branch, or device claim is added. Access token TTL 8h, refresh 24h. Therefore the server cannot derive 'which pharmacy' from the token.

findings.topic: Privileges are decoded straight into authorities
findings.behaviour: CustomAuthorizationFilter reads decodedJWT.getClaim('privileges').asArray and maps each string to a SimpleGrantedAuthority. So the @PreAuthorize hasAnyAuthority('X') strings are matched directly against privilege codes carried in the token.

Of all PharmacyResource endpoints, only two carry an active @PreAuthorize: POST /pharmacies/save -> 'ADMIN-ACCESS' (line 122) and POST /pharmacies/update_stock -> 'MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE' (line 200). All sale-order endpoints are ungated: save_pharmacy_sale_order has its @PreAuthorize COMMENTED OUT (line 298); save_pharmacy_sale_order_detail, pharmacy_sale_orders, .../get, .../cancel, .../archive, .../archive_all, pharmacy_customers, load_pharmacy_sale_order_details have NO annotation. All read endpoints (get_pharmacy_medicine_list, get_pharmacy_medicine_batches, get_prescription_batches, load_pharmacies_like, get/get_by_name/get_names) have NO annotation.

All LPO endpoints are gated. List/get/search/save_detail/delete_detail use 'LOCAL_PURCHASE_ORDER-ALL'. save uses ANY of 'LOCAL_PURCHASE_ORDER-ALL','-CREATE','-UPDATE'. Crucially the four state-transition endpoints verify/approve/submit/return/reject ALL require the SAME single privilege 'LOCAL_PURCHASE_ORDER-ALL' — there are NO distinct -VERIFY/-APPROVE/-SUBMIT/-REJECT privileges; segregation of duties is not enforced at the privilege layer. /local_purchase_orders/request_no has NO annotation.
findings.cite: api/LocalPurchaseOrderResource.java:59-365 (verify 92-93, approve 102-103, submit 112-113, return 122-123, reject 132-133, request_no 173 ungated)

findings.behaviour: create -> 'GOODS_RECEIVED_NOTE-ALL' OR '-CREATE' (98). save_detail_qty, verify_detail_qty, add_batch, delete_batch -> '-ALL' OR '-UPDATE' (177,219,286,312). approve -> '-ALL' OR '-APPROVE' (274). The list endpoint GET /goods_received_notes has its @PreAuthorize COMMENTED OUT ('GOO-ALL', line 71) and the GET /goods_received_notes/search has its @PreAuthorize COMMENTED OUT (line 121) — both effectively ungated.

StoreResource: only /stores/save and /stores/update_store_item_register gated 'ADMIN-ACCESS' (108,119); /stores/update_stock gated 'ITEM_STOCK-UPDATE' (173); all read + load_stores_by_store_person UNGATED. StorePersonResource: only /store_persons/save gated 'ADMIN-ACCESS' (78); everything else (incl. assign_user_profile) UNGATED. SupplierResource: /suppliers/save has @PreAuthorize COMMENTED OUT (106) so ALL supplier endpoints are ungated.
Write endpoints delete, save, save_or_update all gated with single privilege 'SUPPLIER_PRICE_LIST-ALL' (100,117,144). Read endpoints (list, get_item_price_list_by_supplier, get) UNGATED.
Only /pharmacists/save gated 'ADMIN-ACCESS' (79). load_pharmacist_by_username and assign_user_profile UNGATED (both tagged '// to do later'). Reads UNGATED.

findings.topic: No Pharmacist->Pharmacy affiliation in data model
findings.behaviour: The Pharmacist entity has @OneToOne to User (user_id) but NO field linking to Pharmacy (no pharmacy_id, no Collection<Pharmacy>). The Pharmacy entity has NO field linking to any user/pharmacist. The User entity has no pharmacy field. A codebase-wide search for Collection/List/Set<Pharmacy> on any domain entity returns nothing. There is therefore no way to determine a user's pharmacy from server state.

findings.topic: Operating pharmacy comes from the CLIENT on every call
findings.behaviour: Pharmacy is identified per-request by client input, never from session/token. get_pharmacy_medicine_list takes @RequestParam pharmacy_name (line 143); get_pharmacy_medicine_batches takes @RequestParam pharmacy_id (line 165); update_stock derives pharmacy from the posted PharmacyMedicine record's own pharmacy (line 206). In savePharmacySaleOrder the pharmacy is resolved purely from the request body via pharmacyRepository.findByName(order.getPharmacy().getName()) and the pharmacist from order.getPharmacist().getId() — with NO check that the authenticated user is associated with that pharmacy/pharmacist.

findings.topic: getUser(request) resolves identity only from JWT subject
findings.behaviour: userService.getUser(request)/getUserId(request) simply look up the user by request.getUserPrincipal().getName() (the JWT subject/username). It returns the User; it does NOT and cannot resolve a pharmacy. createdBy/orderedBy stamps use this user id, but no pharmacy scoping is applied.

findings.topic: Store-person scoping exists but is a worklist FILTER, not a write gate
findings.behaviour: StorePerson DOES have @ManyToMany Collection<Store> stores. GET /stores/load_stores_by_store_person -> getStoresByStorePerson() looks up StorePerson by the logged-in user and returns only that person's stores. This is used to populate the UI store list; it is a soft filter. It throws only if the user is not in the store_persons register. Write/stock/transfer/GRN endpoints still take store from request param/body and do NOT re-verify the user's store affiliation. No equivalent exists for pharmacy at all.

findings.topic: GRN/transfer worklists are store/pharmacy-scoped by request param, not by user
findings.behaviour: GET /goods_received_notes takes @RequestParam store_id and returns findAllByStoreAndStatusIn for the client-supplied store; no verification the user belongs to that store. Same pattern holds for transfer-order entities (e.g. StoreToPharmacyTO carries pharmacy_id and store_id set from the request, not from session).

absences.expected: Server-side pharmacy session scoping (a Pharmacist->Pharmacy or User->Pharmacy affiliation enforced on writes)
absences.searched: domain/Pharmacist.java (no pharmacy field), domain/Pharmacy.java (no user field), domain/User.java (no pharmacy field); grep across com.orbix.api for Collection/List/Set<Pharmacy> on entities (none); grep for findByPharmacist/getPharmacistByUser/getPharmaciesByPharmacist (none). Confirms PHARM-1: no server-side session scoping for pharmacy.

absences.expected: A pharmacyUid/pharmacy_uid (or storeUid) passed as a stable session/context token
absences.searched: grep com.orbix.api for pharmacyUid|pharmacy_uid|storeUid|store_uid — no matches. Pharmacy/store is passed as pharmacy_name/pharmacy_id/store_id request params or embedded in the request entity body on each call.

absences.expected: Pharmacy claim inside the JWT (so the server could trust which pharmacy)
absences.searched: filter/CustomAuthenticationFilter.java:76-88 — JWT withClaim only adds 'privileges'; subject is username. No pharmacy/store/branch claim. filter/CustomAuthorizationFilter.java:76-77 reads only subject + 'privileges'.

absences.expected: A blanket .anyRequest().authenticated() so unannotated endpoints still require login
absences.searched: security/SecurityConfig.java:80-89 — the catch-all is commented out (line 89). Only listed permitAll matchers exist; protection relies entirely on per-method @PreAuthorize.

absences.expected: Granular segregation-of-duties privileges for LPO state transitions (separate VERIFY/APPROVE/SUBMIT/REJECT codes)
absences.searched: api/LocalPurchaseOrderResource.java:92-133 — verify/approve/submit/return/reject all require the single 'LOCAL_PURCHASE_ORDER-ALL' authority; no distinct transition-level privilege codes exist.

absences.expected: Privilege gating on the PharmacySaleOrder sale flow
absences.searched: api/PharmacyResource.java:297-345 — save_pharmacy_sale_order @PreAuthorize is commented out (line 298); save_pharmacy_sale_order_detail, cancel, archive, archive_all, list/get all carry NO @PreAuthorize. The pharmacy sale flow is effectively ungated.

verdicts.planClaim: All pharmacy and procurement endpoints are annotated with @PreAuthorize using codes from the 177-code set (DoD line 173; Dependencies line 100).
verdicts.evidence: Two independent contradictions. (1) Coverage: the legacy does NOT annotate all pharmacy/procurement endpoints. PharmacyResource gates only POST /pharmacies/save ('ADMIN-ACCESS', :122) and POST /pharmacies/update_stock ('MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE', :200); the ENTIRE PharmacySaleOrder sale flow is ungated (save_pharmacy_sale_order @PreAuthorize is COMMENTED OUT, PharmacyResource.java:298; detail/cancel/archive/list/get have no annotation), all read endpoints ungated. GoodsReceivedNoteResource list (:71) and search (:121) have @PreAuthorize COMMENTED OUT. SupplierResource /suppliers/save is COMMENTED OUT (:106) so ALL supplier endpoints are ungated. StoreResource reads + load_stores_by_store_person ungated; StorePersonResource assign_user_profile ungated; PharmacistResource assign_user_profile ungated ('// to do later'). There is no .anyRequest().authenticated() (SecurityConfig.java:89 commented), so these ungated endpoints are reachable anonymously. (2) The '177-code set' framing is itself false: the ratified as-built IAM record (docs/delivery/increments/01-identity-access.md:7-8) states privilege codes are 35 DISTINCT; '177' counts @PreAuthorize SITES not codes, of which only 26 are live gates and 9 are commented-dead. This is the exact inc-06 trap (177 phantom codes) recurring.
verdicts.correction: State that legacy authorization is method-level only with NO blanket authenticated() rule, that privilege coverage across pharmacy/procurement is deliberately uneven, and that several flows (entire PharmacySaleOrder sale path, GRN list/search, all Supplier endpoints) are UNGATED in legacy. If inc-08 intends to gate these flows uniformly, that is a deliberate security HARDENING beyond legacy behaviour and must be logged in the change-request register as new scope (with security-architect sign-off), not presented as legacy reproduction. Replace '177-code set' with the ratified '35 distinct privilege codes' per the I01 as-built record.

verdicts.planClaim: Privilege codes used include PRESCRIPTION-ALL, GOODS_RECEIVED_NOTE-CREATE, LOCAL_PURCHASE_ORDER-ALL (Dependencies line 100).
verdicts.evidence: Mixed. GOODS_RECEIVED_NOTE-ALL/-CREATE/-UPDATE/-APPROVE do exist on GRN endpoints (GoodsReceivedNoteResource.java:98,177,219,274,286,312) and LOCAL_PURCHASE_ORDER-ALL/-CREATE/-UPDATE exist on LPO endpoints (LocalPurchaseOrderResource.java:59-365). So GOODS_RECEIVED_NOTE-CREATE and LOCAL_PURCHASE_ORDER-ALL are real legacy codes. However PRESCRIPTION-ALL is NOT cited anywhere in the rbac-scoping ground truth — the actual prescription/sale flow in PharmacyResource is ungated (commented-out @PreAuthorize at :298), and the only active pharmacy gates are 'ADMIN-ACCESS' and 'MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE'. PRESCRIPTION-ALL should be treated as unverified/likely-invented until legacy-analyst confirms it against the @PreAuthorize grep fixture (expected-privilege-codes.txt).
verdicts.correction: Keep GOODS_RECEIVED_NOTE-CREATE and LOCAL_PURCHASE_ORDER-ALL (legacy-verified). Remove or flag PRESCRIPTION-ALL as UNVERIFIED — the legacy pharmacy sale/dispense flow carries no @PreAuthorize at all; the live pharmacy codes are 'ADMIN-ACCESS' (pharmacies/save) and 'MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE' (update_stock). Any prescription-gating privilege must be confirmed against the legacy fixture before authoring acceptance criteria.

verdicts.planClaim: Security integration test asserts 403 for wrong roles on all pharmacy and procurement endpoints (DoD line 173); requests from a user not in pharmacy_staff return 403 Forbidden (DoD line 169).
verdicts.evidence: Behaviourally this is a desirable modern hardening, but it does NOT reproduce legacy behaviour and the doc presents it as straightforward parity. In legacy, ungated endpoints (PharmacySaleOrder flow, GRN list/search, all Supplier endpoints) return NO 403 — they are reachable without any privilege and, absent a Bearer token, anonymously (CustomAuthorizationFilter.java:105-106 else-branch + SecurityConfig.java:89 commented anyRequest). A test asserting 403 on those endpoints would assert NON-legacy behaviour. The pharmacy_staff 403 path is entirely net-new (no legacy affiliation exists — see PHARM-1 verdict).
verdicts.correction: Frame uniform 403-on-wrong-role coverage and pharmacy_staff 403 as a deliberate, change-request-approved security improvement (audit-trail/RBAC hardening is pre-approved net-new per the engagement principle), NOT as legacy reproduction. For the endpoints legacy left ungated, the acceptance criteria must explicitly state this is new gating, and security-architect must own the privilege-to-endpoint mapping.

verdicts.planClaim: PHARM-1: the front-end passes pharmacyUid on every dispensing/stock/transfer call; NO server-side session state (Exact-process target 9, line 134; pitfall line 141; DoD line 169).
verdicts.evidence: Directly confirmed. Legacy identifies the operating pharmacy per-request from CLIENT input, never from session/token: get_pharmacy_medicine_list takes @RequestParam pharmacy_name (PharmacyResource.java:143); get_pharmacy_medicine_batches takes @RequestParam pharmacy_id (:165); update_stock derives pharmacy from the posted PharmacyMedicine body (:206); savePharmacySaleOrder resolves pharmacy purely from request body via pharmacyRepository.findByName(order.getPharmacy().getName()) (PatientServiceImpl.java:3334-3339). The JWT carries only subject(username) + 'privileges' (CustomAuthenticationFilter.java:76-88) — no pharmacy/store/branch claim. getUser(request) resolves identity from JWT subject only (UserServiceImpl.java:649-656). 'No server-side session state / pharmacy comes from the client per call' is exactly correct.

verdicts.planClaim: PHARM-1: backend validates the authenticated user is affiliated with the named pharmacy via an M:N pharmacy_staff table (target 9, line 134; pitfall line 141; DoD line 169).
verdicts.evidence: No legacy basis for the affiliation/validation. The Pharmacist entity has @OneToOne to User but NO link to Pharmacy (no pharmacy_id, no Collection<Pharmacy>) (Pharmacist.java:44-72); Pharmacy has no user/pharmacist field (Pharmacy.java:34-54); User has no pharmacy field. A codebase-wide grep for Collection/List/Set<Pharmacy> and for findByPharmacist/getPharmaciesByPharmacist returns nothing (absences §1). savePharmacySaleOrder does NOT check that the authenticated user is associated with the named pharmacy/pharmacist (PatientServiceImpl.java:3334-3339). There is no 'pharmacy_staff' table in legacy. The closest analogue — StorePerson.@ManyToMany Collection<Store> — exists ONLY for STORES and is used as a UI worklist FILTER, not a write-time hard gate (StoreResource.java:72-75; StoreServiceImpl.java:147-163); no pharmacy equivalent exists. So the entire 'validates affiliation before any state transition / 403 if not in pharmacy_staff' mechanism is net-new design with no legacy behaviour to reproduce.
verdicts.correction: Split PHARM-1 into two parts. The factual premise — 'legacy has NO server-side pharmacy scoping; pharmacy is client-supplied per call' — is ACCURATE and should be stated as the legacy baseline. The proposed remedy — an M:N pharmacy_staff affiliation enforced as a write-time hard gate returning 403 — is NEW SCOPE (no legacy basis, no legacy field, no legacy check). It must be logged in the change-request register as an intentional improvement (RBAC/scoping hardening), routed to engagement-lead for phase-gate approval, and its acceptance criteria must state it is net-new, NOT legacy reproduction. Note the only legacy precedent (StorePerson→Store) is a soft UI filter, not a gate, and applies to stores only.

verdicts.planClaim: M:N pharmacy_staff table is seeded in I01 (Increment 01) (target 9, line 134).
verdicts.evidence: No legacy basis for a pharmacy_staff table (absences §1: no Pharmacist→Pharmacy or User→Pharmacy affiliation anywhere). Additionally, the ratified I01 as-built record (01-identity-access.md:11-13) lists the six personnel extensions actually modelled (Clinician/Nurse/Pharmacist/Cashier/StorePerson/Management) and the legacy StorePerson→Store M:N — but no Pharmacist→Pharmacy M:N. Asserting I01 seeds pharmacy_staff is an unverified forward dependency on a structure that does not exist in legacy and is not confirmed in the I01 as-built spec.
verdicts.correction: Do not assert pharmacy_staff is seeded in I01 unless/until the change request establishing pharmacy↔staff affiliation is approved and the I01 as-built spec is updated to own that seed. As written this is a phantom cross-increment dependency. If approved as new scope, specify which increment owns the pharmacy_staff schema/seed and update I01's traceability.

verdicts.planClaim: All worklist queries are implicitly scoped via pharmacyUid request param (e.g. GET /api/v1/pharmacy/prescriptions?pharmacyUid=<uid>) (target 9, line 134; endpoints lines 37,53-55).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy worklists/reads ARE scoped by a client-supplied pharmacy/store request param, matching the param-driven shape: GET /goods_received_notes takes @RequestParam store_id and returns findAllByStoreAndStatusIn for the client-supplied store, with NO verification the user belongs to that store (GoodsReceivedNoteResource.java:70-95); pharmacy reads take pharmacy_name/pharmacy_id (PharmacyResource.java:143,165). So scoping-by-param is accurate. Two corrections: (1) legacy uses pharmacy_name/pharmacy_id, NOT a 'pharmacyUid' token — grep for pharmacyUid/pharmacy_uid/storeUid returns no matches (absences §2); uid-based addressing is a modern re-modelling, acceptable but should be noted as a rename. (2) The word 'implicitly scoped' must not imply server-derived scoping — legacy scoping is EXPLICIT client input with no user-affiliation check.
verdicts.correction: Note that 'pharmacyUid' is the modern uid rename of the legacy client-supplied pharmacy_name/pharmacy_id param (acceptable re-modelling, behaviour preserved). Clarify that legacy worklist scoping is purely client-param-driven with no user-affiliation verification; any affiliation check layered on top is the new-scope hardening covered by the PHARM-1 change request.

verdicts.planClaim: LPO state-transition endpoints (verify/approve/submit/receive) are each separately privilege-gated, implying segregation of duties (endpoints lines 79-82; lifecycle DRAFT→VERIFIED→APPROVED→SUBMITTED→RECEIVED line 29).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy enforces NO segregation of duties at the privilege layer for LPO transitions: verify (:92-93), approve (:102-103), submit (:112-113), return (:122-123), reject (:132-133) ALL require the SAME single privilege 'LOCAL_PURCHASE_ORDER-ALL' — there are NO distinct -VERIFY/-APPROVE/-SUBMIT/-REJECT codes (LocalPurchaseOrderResource.java:92-133; absences §5). /local_purchase_orders/request_no is ungated (:173). The same coarse pattern: a single role can drive every transition. The doc's per-transition endpoint list, if read as implying per-transition privilege gating/SoD, contradicts legacy.
verdicts.correction: Acceptance criteria for LPO transitions must reflect that legacy gates ALL transitions with the single coarse 'LOCAL_PURCHASE_ORDER-ALL' authority (no SoD at the privilege layer). If inc-08 wants granular per-transition privileges (genuine segregation of duties), that is a deliberate deviation requiring a change request and engagement-lead approval — it must not be authored as legacy reproduction. Note request_no is ungated in legacy.

newDriftFound: Legacy authorization has NO blanket .anyRequest().authenticated() (SecurityConfig.java:89 commented out) and the authorization filter falls through with no auth set when the Bearer header is absent (CustomAuthorizationFilter.java:105-106). Consequently every legacy endpoint WITHOUT a @PreAuthorize — including the entire PharmacySaleOrder sale flow (:298 commented), GRN list/search (:71,:121 commented), and ALL Supplier endpoints (:106 commented) — is reachable ANONYMOUSLY. The inc-08 doc never acknowledges this 'default-open' posture. inc-08 should consciously decide to CLOSE it (deny-by-default + require auth on all endpoints) and log that as an approved security improvement; it must not silently assume the legacy already denied-by-default. This is the single biggest unstated security drift.

Legacy HMAC secret is the literal string 'secret' and the stored-token integrity re-check is commented out (CustomAuthorizationFilter.java:73,82-90). inc-08 (or I01) must NOT port this; it is a vulnerability, pre-approved as net-new hardening, but the planning doc is silent on it.

JWT TTLs in legacy are access 8h / refresh 24h (CustomAuthenticationFilter.java). Not mentioned in inc-08; relevant if the pharmacy-staff affiliation is to be carried as a claim vs validated per-request — the doc should state the affiliation is validated against the DB per call (consistent with 'no server-side session state'), not embedded in the token.

The only legacy precedent for staff↔location scoping is StorePerson.@ManyToMany Collection<Store> used as a soft UI worklist FILTER (StoreResource.java:72-75; StoreServiceImpl.java:147-163), never a write-time gate, and it covers STORES only. inc-08 should decide explicitly whether to (a) preserve store scoping as a soft filter (legacy parity) or (b) promote it to a hard write gate (new scope) — and whether the new pharmacy_staff gate makes pharmacy stricter than stores, an asymmetry the doc does not address.

PharmacistResource.assign_user_profile and StorePersonResource.assign_user_profile are ungated in legacy and tagged '// to do later' (PharmacistResource.java:91,106). These are admin/affiliation-write endpoints left open in legacy; if the modern build introduces pharmacy_staff affiliation, the endpoint that WRITES that affiliation must itself be gated (ADMIN-ACCESS) — a gap the doc does not call out.

Document-number sequence prefix rename SPT→SPTO/PPTO (target 5/line 121,124; pitfall line 144) is a known legacy collision fix already tracked under ADR-0009 §6 — outside the rbac-scoping lane but adjacent; flagged so the cross-lane reviewer confirms it is logged as an approved change request, not silently applied.

synthesis.verdict: INC08_IS_A_REAL_FULL_BUILD — and this is the categorical opposite of inc-06. Inc-06 was MOSTLY_DONE: the lab/radiology/procedure-theatre code already existed and discovery was about reconciling a planning doc against a largely-built module, so the dominant risk was PHANTOM lifecycle states (ACCEPTED/HELD/VERIFIED) being asserted over a system that did not implement them. Inc-08 is the reverse: the pharmacy and inventory bounded contexts are confirmed EMPTY GREENFIELD STUBS — `backend/src/main/java/com/otapp/hmis/pharmacy/package-info.java` and `.../inventory/package-info.java` each contain ONLY an `@ApplicationModule`-annotated package declaration labelled "Stub for increment 00", with no domain/application/web subpackages. Almost every entity, state machine, stock ledger, transfer chain, and procurement document in this verdict has to be built from scratch against the legacy ZANAHMIS-2 source as the source of truth. The inc-06 lesson STILL applies but inverted: because there is no as-built to constrain the planning doc, the doc has drifted hard toward an idealized modern design (8-state prescription lifecycle, three-way match, salesPharmacy split, pharmacy_staff RBAC, FEFO-on-OTC, destination batch creation on p2p receipt) that has NO legacy basis. The build must extract the real (often messier, two-state) legacy behaviour and treat every "improvement" as a change-request, not as exact-process. RECOMMENDATION: split into 08a (dispensing + stock core, depends on a new clinical::api seam) and 08b (store/procurement/transfers), because 08b has materially more legacy state-machine surface and a hard cross-module dependency on the billing settlement seam that 08a establishes.
alreadyBuilt: Clinical Prescription aggregate (inc-05/06): the ONLY prescription state machine that exists is NOT_GIVEN -> GIVEN (PrescriptionStatus.java:23-26, verified — exactly two values, hyphenated DB strings via PrescriptionStatusConverter). inc-08 must NOT model ACCEPTED/HELD/VERIFIED/APPROVED/SOLD on prescriptions; those columns are dead in legacy. The single transition Prescription.issue() exists with the stock-decrement TODO deferred to inc-08 (Prescription.java:534-537).

Per-line clinical settlement: the `settled` boolean (Prescription.java:216-217, V36), `paymentType`/`membershipNo` denormalisation, markSettled() driven by shared.event.BillSettledEvent, and the SettlementDispatcher/@TransactionalEventListener(BEFORE_COMMIT) wiring all exist (billing inc-04 + clinical). inc-08 reuses this for medicine pay state; it does NOT build a new payStatus mechanism for clinical prescriptions.

billing::api charge + read seams: BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge(ChargeRequest{kind=MEDICINE,...}, TxAuditContext) -> ChargeResult (BillingCommands.java:34) for medicine pricing (route medicine charges through this; do NOT reimplement insurance/plan pricing); cancelCharge / approveInvoicesForBills; BillingQueries.getBillStatus(billUid) -> BillStatus (UNPAID/VERIFIED/COVERED/PAID/CANCELED) for any live dispense bill-gate; SettlementPolicy.requiresPrepayment(...) for the CASH pay-before-service rule.

DocumentNumberService scaffold (billing.application, NOT shared.documentnumber as the as-built note said): DocumentNumberService.next(DocumentType) formats prefix+yyyyMMdd(EAT)+'-'+seq UNPADDED, with a test-overridable Clock (DocumentNumberServiceImpl.java:24-49). VERIFIED LIMITATION: DocumentType enum has ONLY PCN and a single-arg constructor carrying only `prefix` (no sequenceName); the impl switch maps only PCN -> creditNoteRepository.nextPcnNo(). inc-08 reuses the pattern, NOT a finished multi-type service.

DB document sequences ALL pre-seeded in V13 (inc-02): seq_grn_no, seq_lpo_no, seq_ppr_no, seq_psr_no, seq_spto_no, seq_ppto_no, seq_pgrn_no, seq_pprn_no (+seq_pcn_no/seq_prl_no/seq_mrno) — verified V13:20-58, START WITH 1, currently unused. md_document_types registry rows (V14, CR-10 ratified SPTO+PPTO, NO row carries 'SPT'). inc-08 needs NO new sequence-creation migrations for these streams; it starts at V39.

Masterdata SCHEMAS for inc-08 (inc-02, V6/V7): pharmacies, stores, theatres, medicines (price on row), items (pack_size/vat/cost/selling), item_medicine_coefficients (coefficient=medicine_qty/item_qty, CHECK >0, UQ(item,medicine)), suppliers, items_suppliers, supplier_item_prices. CREATED but NOT seeded with business rows — empty schemas awaiting CRUD. No separate units table (uom/category free-text, CR-07).

Shared kernel (inc-00): AuditableEntity (BIGINT id hidden + ULID uid + Spring Data auditing + @Version optimistic lock), Money @Embeddable (NUMERIC(19,2) HALF_UP, default TZS), TxAuditContext record threaded into every module-API command, BusinessDay (OPEN/CLOSED, NoDayOpenException gate), ErrorCode RFC7807 catalogue, shared.event (BillSettledEvent etc.), shared.storage (FileStoragePort/LocalDiskFileStorage for attachments), and shared.audit (AuditRecorder + AuditLog + AuditAction — the REAL application-level audit mechanism; Envers is NOT used).

IAM Pharmacist personnel record (Pharmacist.java, @Table 'pharmacists', OneToOne User, created on PHARMACIST role) exists — but it is a role-personnel record, NOT a pharmacy-location affiliation. There is NO pharmacist->pharmacy table (and legacy has none either).
synthesis.groundTruthModel:
## Inc-08 ground-truth legacy model (supersedes the planning doc where they conflict)

### A. Two parallel dispensing record types (no single pharmacy aggregate)
Legacy has **no pharmacy-owned Prescription/Dispense aggregate**. Dispensing runs off two record types:
1. **Clinical `Prescription`** (doctor-created during Consultation/NonConsultation/Admission). State machine = **exactly two states**: `NOT-GIVEN` (on save, `PatientServiceImpl.java:1532`) → `GIVEN` (on dispense, `PatientResource.java:3230`). accept/hold/reject/verify columns EXIST but are **dead for the medicine flow** (no setter touches them — cross-module grep confirms). Dispense reuses `approvedBy/On` (3233-3235), not `soldBy`.
2. **Walk-in `PharmacySaleOrder`** (header) + `PharmacySaleOrderDetail` (lines) tied to a standalone `PharmacyCustomer`, NOT a clinical Patient.

### B. Clinical dispense (`POST /patients/issue_medicine`, PatientResource.java:3199-3293)
Guards in order: (1) prescription exists; (2) status == `NOT-GIVEN`; (3) issued value valid; (4) **issued == qty** ("You can only issue the prescribed qty" — all-or-nothing). **There is NO bill-status check at this terminal.** Payment is enforced ONLY in the worklist FILTER. Then: status→GIVEN (saved BEFORE the stock check), set issuePharmacy, hard negative-stock guard `PharmacyMedicine.stock < qty` else throw, decrement `PharmacyMedicine.stock` by **getQty()**, write a `PharmacyStockCard` OUT row (`balance`=post-stock, reference "Issued in prescription: id <n>", qtyOut=**getIssued()**), then `deductBatch` FEFO over `PharmacyMedicineBatch`.

### C. CASH pay gate = WORKLIST FILTER, not a hard service gate (clinical)
Enforced ONLY in the worklist endpoints: OUTPATIENT & OUTSIDER require bill `PAID|COVERED` (PatientResource.java:4347,4364); INPATIENT additionally admits `VERIFIED` (4381,4410). Bill status is set at prescription creation: CASH→UNPAID; INSURANCE+covered plan→COVERED (paid in full at plan price, PENDING insurance invoice); INPATIENT+no covered plan→VERIFIED (post-pay/credit, balance retained — this is **inpatient credit, NOT insurer-verifies-at-discharge**). The clinical terminal is bypassable by direct call.

### D. Walk-in OTC lifecycle (DISTINCT from prescription, NOT identical)
Header: `PENDING` → `APPROVED` → `ARCHIVED`, plus `PENDING` → `CANCELED`. **PENDING→APPROVED is a side effect of paying the linked PatientBill via billing's `confirm_bills_payment`** (PatientBillResource.java:269-393) — there is no standalone approve endpoint. Detail carries TWO independent fields: fulfilment `status` (NOT-GIVEN→GIVEN) and `payStatus` (UNPAID→PAID). Dispense (`give_medicine`, PatientResource.java:6212-6322) hard-gates on **header status==APPROVED only** (never re-checks payStatus), forces issued==qty, hard negative-stock guard, decrements aggregate `PharmacyMedicine.stock`, writes a stock-card OUT row "Issued in sale: id <n>" — and **does NOT consume FEFO batches (deductBatch is COMMENTED OUT at :6317)**. cancel allowed only from PENDING; archive only from APPROVED + all details GIVEN; **24h auto-cancel** of stale PENDING and **24h auto-archive** of completed APPROVED orders (PatientServiceImpl.java:3199-3229). OTC line bills hang off a **GENERAL dummy patient** (no='GENERAL', created if absent); amount = flat `Medicine.price * qty`, **paymentType hardcoded CASH** (incoming value ignored), no insurance/co-pay/discount. Customer numbering has TWO divergent formats ('PCST'+id vs 'PCST/'+year+'/'+id). Order no = 'PSO/'+id (no date, no Formater).

### E. Stock model: TWO independent, frequently-DESYNCED stores
1. **Aggregate**: `PharmacyMedicine.stock` (plain double, authoritative, mutated directly read-modify-write). NO StockBalance entity. On pharmacy registration, a stock=0 PharmacyMedicine row + opening stock-card are eagerly created for EVERY medicine (PharmacyServiceImpl.java:67-91).
2. **Per-lot**: `PharmacyMedicineBatch` (no @NotBlank, manufacturedDate/expiryDate nullable, qty double, **no audit cols, no @Version**). Single `qty` field decremented in place — NOT separate received/remaining.
3. **Ledger**: `PharmacyStockCard` append-only (qtyIn/qtyOut/balance doubles + free-text `reference` — **no movement-type enum**; type is conveyed only by the reference string). `balance` is a stored post-movement snapshot of `PharmacyMedicine.stock`, NOT recomputed.

**FEFO** = in-memory over an UNORDERED query (`findAllByPharmacyAndMedicineAndQtyGreaterThan`, no OrderBy). `getEarlierBatch`: if ANY batch has a non-null expiry, pick earliest expiry and **silently EXCLUDE null-expiry batches** (they can be stranded); if NO batch has expiry, fall back to **lowest id** (FIFO). `deductBatch` recurses across batches and **swallows ALL exceptions in an empty catch** (PatientResource.java:3296-3336) — a batch shortfall is silent; only the aggregate `PharmacyMedicine.stock < qty` check is a hard gate. **NO database locking anywhere** (zero @Lock/PESSIMISTIC/SELECT FOR UPDATE across com.orbix.api; no @Version on batch). Negative-stock guard is read-then-check-then-write with no concurrency protection. FEFO logic is DUPLICATED verbatim in PatientResource (writes PrescriptionBatch trace rows) and PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl (no trace rows). Manual `update_stock` (auth MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE) OVERWRITES stock absolutely (not delta), rejects negative, writes 'Stock Update' card, touches NO batches.

### F. Pharmacy↔Pharmacy transfer (3-document chain)
`PharmacyToPharmacyRO` (PPR) → `PharmacyToPharmacyTO` (legacy prefix **SPT**) → `PharmacyToPharmacyRN` (PPRN). RO states: PENDING→VERIFIED→APPROVED→SUBMITTED, then IN-PROCESS (on TO create)→GOODS-ISSUED (on TO issue)→COMPLETED (on RN approve); side branches RETURNED/REJECTED **only from SUBMITTED** — once IN-PROCESS the RO can never be returned/rejected (dead-end). TO created only from a SUBMITTED RO (idempotent — returns existing). TO states PENDING→VERIFIED→APPROVED→GOODS-ISSUED→COMPLETED; **no ownership guard** on TO verify/approve/issue. **SOURCE stock deduction + source stock-card OUT + source FEFO batch deduction happen on `TO.issue()` (APPROVED→GOODS-ISSUED), NOT on RN** (PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:215-283). transferedQty is accumulated via `add_batch` (PENDING-only, qty>0, SKU match, cumulative ≤ orderedQty). RN is a **two-state machine PENDING→COMPLETED** (NO verify/approve; verified/approved columns never written). DESTINATION stock increment + destination stock-card IN happen on RN approve_receipt — **but destination PharmacyMedicineBatch rows are NEVER created (block commented out, PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:221-232)** — transferred stock has no batch/expiry at destination. There is a window where source is debited but destination not yet credited. Source/destination are different documents AND different transactions.

### G. Pharmacy↔Store transfer (3-document chain, with unit conversion)
`PharmacyToStoreRO` (PSR, pharmacy requests, moves no stock) → `StoreToPharmacyTO` (legacy prefix **SPT** — the collision twin) → `StoreToPharmacyRN` (PGRN, the PHARMACY's own goods-received note). **STORE stock decrements ONLY in TO.issue()** (StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:221-289): hard negative-stock reject, decrement StoreItem.stock, StoreStockCard OUT row, FEFO over StoreItemBatch. **PHARMACY stock increments ONLY in RN approve_receipt** (StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:201-242): adds receivedPharmacySKUQty to PharmacyMedicine.stock, PharmacyStockCard IN row (only if qty>0), and **DOES create one PharmacyMedicineBatch per StoreToPharmacyBatch** (unlike the p2p path). **Unit conversion is real here**: `pharmacySKUQty = storeSKUQty * ItemMedicineCoefficient.coefficient` (InternalOrderResource.java:595; StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:417-424), hard-fails if no coefficient. CRITICAL: the RN PENDING→COMPLETED guard lives in the CONTROLLER (InternalOrderResource.java:725-748), not the service — calling the service directly double-posts (latent bug). No over-receipt guard. RN re-parents StoreToPharmacyBatch rows from TO detail onto RN detail. The manually-entered transfer batch identity is NOT reconciled against the FEFO-consumed StoreItemBatch (decoupled). Store uses the SAME StoreStockCard ledger discipline (single ledger + scalar StoreItem.stock — NO separate balance table).

### H. Procurement: LPO → GRN (two-document linear, NO three-way match)
`LocalPurchaseOrder` (LPO): initial state **PENDING** (no DRAFT) → VERIFIED → APPROVED → SUBMITTED → (RECEIVED, flipped by GRN approval). REJECTED/RETURNED reachable **only from PENDING or VERIFIED**; RETURNED has no re-open (effectively terminal). LPO edit only while PENDING and only `validUntil` is mutable. LPO detail price is **copied from SupplierItemPrice** (not the request); a (supplier,item) with no price row is HARD-rejected; active flags exist but are NOT enforced. `GRN` HEADER state machine = **PENDING → APPROVED only** (no header VERIFIED/REJECTED/RETURNED — those appear only in a copy-paste worklist filter). Verification is **per-LINE** (GoodsReceivedNoteDetail: NOT VERIFIED → VERIFIED); a line cannot be VERIFIED unless `receivedQty <= orderedQty` AND `sum(batch.qty) == receivedQty` (the ONLY real reconciliation). approve() hard-gates that EVERY line is VERIFIED. GRN create requires request store == LPO store AND LPO status==SUBMITTED (guard in resource). **One GRN per LPO** (no partial/multi-receipt mechanism). GRN.approve() is one @Transactional method doing, per line with receivedQty>0: StoreItem.stock += receivedQty, StoreStockCard IN row, copy each GRN batch into a NEW StoreItemBatch (no merge), **write a Purchase ledger row (qty=receivedQty, amount=receivedQty*price)** — then flip LPO→RECEIVED, GRN→APPROVED. **THERE IS NO THREE-WAY MATCH and no SupplierInvoice entity anywhere.** Supplier.code is user-supplied (no sequence). All money/qty is double.

### I. Document numbering
All server-assigned numbers = `Formater.formatWithCurrentDate(prefix, MAX(id)+1)` = `PREFIX + yyyyMMdd + '-' + rawId` (UNPADDED, no fiscal reset, race-prone). Verbatim legacy prefixes: GRN, LPO, PPR (p2p RO), PPRN (p2p RN), PSR (p2s RO), PGRN (s2p RN). **TO prefix is literal 'SPT' for BOTH transfer types — the confirmed collision.** Two ownership models: TO/RN/GRN numbered server-side in create(); **RO numbers (PPR, PSR) are CLIENT-SUPPLIED** (frontend previews via request_no, posts back — server never setNo). PharmacySaleOrder.no = 'PSO/'+id (no date, no Formater). GRN double-saves with no='NA' placeholder first (off-by-one quirk).

### J. RBAC / scoping
Method-level @PreAuthorize only; **NO `.anyRequest().authenticated()` (commented)** so any un-annotated endpoint is anonymous-reachable. Coverage is deliberately UNEVEN: the entire PharmacySaleOrder flow is UNGATED (save_pharmacy_sale_order @PreAuthorize commented at :298), GRN list/search ungated, all Supplier endpoints ungated. Live pharmacy codes: only 'ADMIN-ACCESS' (pharmacies/save) and 'MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE' (update_stock). LPO/GRN transitions are gated by a SINGLE coarse code each ('LOCAL_PURCHASE_ORDER-ALL', 'GOODS_RECEIVED_NOTE-ALL') — **NO segregation of duties at the privilege layer**. JWT carries only subject+privileges — **no pharmacy/store claim**. **NO server-side pharmacy session scoping and NO pharmacist->pharmacy affiliation table exist** (PHARM-1 confirmed). The operating pharmacy is client-supplied per call. The only staff↔location precedent is StorePerson.@ManyToMany Collection<Store>, used as a SOFT UI worklist FILTER for stores only, never a write gate.
drift: PHANTOM — 8-state prescription lifecycle (PENDING→ACCEPTED→HELD→VERIFIED→APPROVED→SOLD + terminal REJECTED/CANCELLED). Legacy medicine flow is exactly NOT-GIVEN→GIVEN; accept/hold/reject/verify columns are dead. No SOLD state (dispense reuses approvedBy). Exact inc-06 dormant-columns-as-live-machine trap.

DRIFT — 'hard service-layer sell() gate on per-line payStatus=PAID, not a worklist filter' for clinical dispensing. Legacy issue_medicine has NO terminal pay check at all; the only enforcement is the worklist FILTER on PatientBill.status. There is no accept() to be 'callable on PENDING'. Hardening the bypassable terminal is defensible but is a DEVIATION requiring a CR, not exact-process.

PHANTOM — issuePharmacyUid vs salesPharmacyUid split with stock decrementing salesPharmacyUid ('fill at A, pull from B'). salesPharmacy is NEVER assigned in legacy (only a dead field declaration). One pharmacy per dispense = issuePharmacy = stock source. The A/B cross-pharmacy parity target tests invented behaviour.

DRIFT — OTC PharmacySaleOrder has 'identical status lifecycle to Prescription'. False: OTC header is PENDING→APPROVED→ARCHIVED(+CANCELED) with payment-driven approve and 24h auto-sweeps; detail has TWO fields (fulfilment status + payStatus). Not identical; omits ARCHIVED, CANCELED, and the scheduled auto-transitions entirely.

DRIFT/PHANTOM — applying the unified FEFO StockBatch model to OTC sale-order dispense. Legacy OTC deductBatch is COMMENTED OUT (:6317): OTC decrements aggregate stock + stock card only, NEVER touches batches. FEFO-on-OTC is net-new (deliberate fix or accidental over-application — must be CR'd).

DRIFT — StockBalance entity with PESSIMISTIC_WRITE lock on decrement 'reproducing legacy'. Legacy aggregate is a plain double field PharmacyMedicine.stock with ZERO locking (0 grep matches), race-prone by design. The StockBalance re-model is acceptable; the pessimistic lock is NET-NEW concurrency hardening, not legacy reproduction.

DRIFT — lockFefoForDispense acquires SELECT FOR UPDATE ordered 'expiry_date ASC NULLS LAST', negative-stock guard after lock. Legacy: no locking, no SQL ordering (in-memory over unordered query), and the null-expiry semantics are NOT 'NULLS LAST' — legacy SILENTLY EXCLUDES null-expiry lots whenever any dated lot exists. 'NULLS LAST' changes which lot dispenses.

DRIFT — p2p transfer: 'StockMovement rows written ONLY on RN COMPLETED; zero movement on source until complete()'. False: source stock-card OUT + source FEFO deduction happen at TO goods-issue (a DIFFERENT document and transaction). Only the destination credit is at RN complete. Two distinct posting points collapsed into one.

PHANTOM — coefficient applied on p2p TRANSFER_OUT/TRANSFER_IN. The pharmacy↔pharmacy path moves quantities 1:1 (transferedQty == receivedQty); no coefficient anywhere in the p2p services. (Coefficient IS real on the pharmacy↔STORE path only.) Applying it to p2p would change the transferred quantity.

DRIFT — GRN header lifecycle PENDING→VERIFIED→APPROVED + terminal REJECTED/RETURNED. Legacy GRN HEADER = PENDING→APPROVED only; VERIFIED belongs to the LINE (NOT VERIFIED→VERIFIED). REJECTED/RETURNED never assigned to a header (copy-paste worklist filter).

PHANTOM — ThreeWayMatch (LPO qty == GRN qty == SupplierInvoice qty; payment-release gate; /three-way-match/validate endpoint). ZERO legacy basis: no SupplierInvoice entity, no invoice-qty field, no payment-release gate exist. Legacy reconciliation is two-way (receivedQty<=orderedQty) + batch-sum==receivedQty. Must be removed from exact-process scope and re-scoped as new feature with engagement-lead approval.

DRIFT — 'all pharmacy/procurement endpoints @PreAuthorize'd from a 177-code set'. Coverage is deliberately uneven (whole PharmacySaleOrder flow, GRN list/search, all Supplier endpoints UNGATED); no anyRequest().authenticated(). The '177-code' figure is the inc-06 phantom recurring — ratified IAM record is 35 distinct codes; 177 counts @PreAuthorize SITES (26 live, 9 commented-dead).

PHANTOM — pharmacy_staff M:N affiliation table validated as a write-time hard gate returning 403, seeded in I01. No Pharmacist→Pharmacy / User→Pharmacy field exists in legacy; no pharmacy_staff table; savePharmacySaleOrder does no affiliation check. The only precedent (StorePerson→Store) is a soft UI filter for stores only. The factual premise (no server-side scoping, pharmacy is client-supplied) is ACCURATE; the proposed remedy is net-new scope.

DRIFT — LPO state transitions 'each separately privilege-gated implying segregation of duties'. Legacy gates verify/approve/submit/return/reject all with the SINGLE 'LOCAL_PURCHASE_ORDER-ALL' code; no SoD. request_no is ungated. Granular per-transition codes would be a deliberate deviation.

DRIFT — 'all eight document types assigned server-side inside the insert tx'. True for GRN/LPO/TO/RN (6 types) but FALSE for PPR and PSR request orders, which are legacy CLIENT-SUPPLIED (preview-and-post-back; server never setNo). Moving them server-authoritative is a behaviour change to disclose.

CORRECTED — StockBalance/StoreStockBalance + StockMovement/StoreStockMovement two-table re-model. Legacy is ONE ledger (PharmacyStockCard/StoreStockCard) + a scalar stock field — no separate balance table. Acceptable re-model but requires data-architect sign-off and a parity test proving the single-ledger + scalar semantics are preserved; must not be presented as reproduced legacy structure.

CORRECTED — '1/3 * 9 = 3.000000' coefficient parity oracle is mathematically wrong: a NUMERIC(19,6) coefficient stores 0.333333, and 0.333333 * 9 = 2.999997. The parity expected value must be the actual NUMERIC(19,6) round-trip result, not 3.000000.

CORRECTED — 'INPATIENT visible at VERIFIED because insurer verifies at discharge'. VERIFIED is the inpatient CREDIT/post-pay state (balance retained, non-insurance PENDING invoice), NOT insurer verification. Insurance-covered patients get COVERED, not VERIFIED. settledOnly must treat COVERED as settled too.
confirmedAccurate: The 3-document pharmacy↔pharmacy chain naming and flow direction: PharmacyToPharmacyRO raised by requesting pharmacy → PharmacyToPharmacyTO created by delivering pharmacy from a SUBMITTED RO → PharmacyToPharmacyRN created back at requesting pharmacy. Build as written (state details per ground-truth model).

The 3-document pharmacy↔store chain entity set: PharmacyToStoreRO (PSR, pharmacy requests) → StoreToPharmacyTO (transfer) → StoreToPharmacyRN (PGRN, the pharmacy's own GRN where pharmacy stock increments). Build as written, with the correction that the RN's only stock effect is a pharmacy-side increment.
Document-number output FORMAT for all eight types: {PREFIX}{yyyyMMdd}-{seq}, suffix UNPADDED, no fiscal-year reset, global-monotonic. Verbatim prefixes GRN/LPO/PPR/PPRN/PSR/PGRN. Reproduce exactly.

The SPTO/PPTO collision fix is correct and properly change-controlled: legacy emits literal 'SPT' for BOTH transfer types (confirmed); ADR-0009 §6 (verified) assigns StoreToPharmacy TO→SPTO, PharmacyToPharmacy TO→PPTO, gated on product-owner sign-off. 'Any SPT prefix in tests/seed is a defect' is correct. CR-10 ratified in V14 (no row carries 'SPT').

Legacy used MAX(id)+1 (SELECT MAX(id)+1, non-atomic, race-prone) for document numbers — confirmed. Replacing it with dedicated PostgreSQL sequences via DocumentNumberService (ADR-0009 §5, CR-09) is a legitimate, approved concurrency fix that preserves the output format. The single-insert / no-double-save design correctly drops the legacy GRN save-then-assign defect.

PHARM-1 factual premise: the front-end passes the operating pharmacy (legacy pharmacy_name/pharmacy_id, modern pharmacyUid rename) on every dispensing/stock/transfer call; there is NO server-side pharmacy session state and NO pharmacy claim in the JWT. Worklist scoping is purely client-param-driven. This is exactly correct.

ItemMedicineCoefficient and cross-unit multiplication (storeSKUQty * coefficient, hard-fail if no coefficient) are REAL legacy behaviour on the pharmacy↔store path. The double→BigDecimal NUMERIC(19,6) change is the pre-approved ADR-0009 §3 data-type migration with full-precision (no intermediate rounding) — correctly preserves the legacy no-rounding behaviour.

Per-line payStatus UNPAID→PAID is real, but ONLY on PharmacySaleOrderDetail (walk-in); for clinical prescriptions the pay signal is the linked PatientBill.status. Unifying both onto a per-line concept is acceptable re-modelling IF COVERED and VERIFIED are preserved as non-blocking equivalents (binary UNPAID/PAID alone loses them).

OTC sale dispense decrements stock + writes a stock-card (StockMovement) row — accurate at the aggregate level. The pharmacy-sales revenue-by-payment-mode breakdown (BILL-5) is correctly self-flagged as an OPEN GAP / new scope, not legacy reproduction.

The hard negative-stock reject (throw when stock < requested qty) IS real legacy behaviour on both the dispense and the transfer-issue paths — preserve it. (The pessimistic LOCK around it is the net-new part.)

PharmacySaleOrder/PharmacySaleOrderDetail are the OTC/walk-in sale entities with no doctor Rx — correct, with the correction that they tie to a standalone PharmacyCustomer (not the clinical OUTSIDER patient class) and bill via a GENERAL dummy patient.

GRN/LPO atomic-write-in-one-transaction (no async event boundary) is accurate: GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.approve does all stock effects synchronously in one @Transactional method. LPO→RECEIVED is flipped by GRN approval, not by an LPO method.
newDrift: Purchase ledger row is OMITTED from the plan.
