GRN.approve() writes a Purchase row per line with receivedQty>0 (qty=receivedQty, amount=receivedQty*price) inside the same transaction, only when the GRN is linked to an LPO (GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.java:167-183). This feeds the legacy purchase/daily-purchase reports. inc-08 must reproduce it or consciously defer via CR.

Stock-card / movement rows are written ONLY when a line's receivedQty>0 (GRN approve, p2p/p2s issue). A zero-received line writes NO ledger row. The plan's unconditional RECEIPT-per-line implies phantom zero-qty rows. Preserve the conditional.

GRN detail line state machine NOT VERIFIED→VERIFIED and the batch-sum integrity rule (sum(GRN detail batch qty)==receivedQty before a line can be VERIFIED) are the REAL legacy reconciliation gate — needs its own stories. The plan collapses verification to a non-existent header VERIFIED state.

One-GRN-per-LPO constraint and GRN-create preconditions (request store == LPO store, LPO status==SUBMITTED) are load-bearing and missing from the plan. No partial/multi-receipt or back-order mechanism exists.

LPO edit immutability: only editable while PENDING and only validUntil mutable; LPO detail price is copied from SupplierItemPrice (a (supplier,item) with no price row is hard-rejected); active flags exist but are NOT enforced. Safety-critical (financial) — must be acceptance criteria; do not silently add active-flag gating.

p2p destination batch gap: PharmacyMedicineBatch rows are NEVER created at the destination on p2p receipt (commented out). The plan's StockBatch model implies destination batches ARE created on TRANSFER_IN. This is net-new (likely the correct clinical fix) — must go through CR, not be silently improved. Contrast: the pharmacy↔STORE RN DOES create destination batches.

p2p/p2s deductBatch swallows all exceptions (empty catch); the only hard gate is aggregate stock. The plan's 422 INSUFFICIENT_STOCK hard-fail is a behaviour change — defensible, but the parity harness must not expect an error where legacy emits none, and the silent batch under-deduction must be CR'd as a fixed defect.

FEFO null-expiry exclusion + secondary sort: legacy excludes null-expiry lots when any dated lot exists, and falls back to lowest-id (NOT NULLS LAST). Pin the secondary sort to id ASC and decide explicitly whether to preserve or fix the null-expiry exclusion (a behaviour change).

PrescriptionBatch trace rows: the clinical issue path writes a PrescriptionBatch row recording which lot/qty satisfied each line (lot traceability). The plan has no equivalent dispensing lot-traceability entity — at risk of being dropped silently. The p2p path does NOT write trace rows (per-path divergence the plan erases).

RO state machines are richer than the plan: full RO set (PENDING/VERIFIED/APPROVED/SUBMITTED/RETURNED/REJECTED/IN-PROCESS/GOODS-ISSUED/COMPLETED) with hard prior-status guards; SUBMIT (APPROVED→SUBMITTED) is REQUIRED before a TO can be created — the missing /submit endpoint is a functional gap. TO has its own VERIFIED/APPROVED transitions before goods-issue. RO IN-PROCESS is a dead-end for return/reject. RN is two-state PENDING→COMPLETED with NO verify/approve (adding them would be PHANTOM).

add_batch/delete_batch sub-flow and the over-order guard (cumulative transferedQty <= orderedQty) are entirely absent from the plan; transferedQty is NOT entered on the TO header but accumulated via add_batch. RN snapshots TO quantities and re-parents batch rows — load-bearing for how destination batches are produced (pharmacy↔store) and unmentioned.
Two coexisting TO-detail write paths in legacy (controller add_batch AND service saveDetail) both mutate detail qty fields — decide keep-both vs consolidate (consolidation is a behaviour change).

Worklist endpoints across ALL flows (transfers, GRN, OTC) are FILTERS, not gates; real enforcement is hard service/controller rejects. inc-08 must explicitly carry the inc-06 'hard gate vs filter' distinction into every worklist so a filter is not re-implemented as the enforcement mechanism. OTC getPharmacySaleOrders returns PENDING/APPROVED as a filter.

OTC delete-detail gate (status IN PENDING/NOT-GIVEN AND bill UNPAID; rejects if a GIVEN payment detail exists; a PatientCreditNote branch is dead code) and clinical delete_prescription credit-note path (delete only when PENDING/NOT-GIVEN; raises a PENDING PatientCreditNote if a GIVEN payment exists; contains a j=j++ no-op bug) — the whole reversal/credit-note story is missing from the plan.

Opening-stock eager creation: registering a Pharmacy auto-creates a stock=0 PharmacyMedicine row + opening stock-card for EVERY medicine in the catalog. The plan is silent — decide reproduce vs lazy creation (lazy is a behaviour change).
Manual stock-update OVERWRITE semantics (absolute set, not delta; rejects negative; no batch effect) listed by the plan only as 'ADJUSTMENT' without the overwrite/batch-desync detail.

PharmacySaleOrder ('PSO/'+id) and PharmacyCustomer ('PCST'+id / 'PCST/'+year+'/'+id dual format) numbering are in scope but absent from the numbering section — they use NO Formater, no date, no '-' separator and must not be coerced into {PREFIX}{yyyyMMdd}-{seq}. The GENERAL dummy-patient OTC billing dependency is also unmodelled.

Legacy security default-open posture (no anyRequest().authenticated(); filter falls through anonymously; HMAC secret literally 'secret'; token-integrity recheck commented) is unacknowledged. inc-08/I01 must consciously CLOSE this (deny-by-default) as approved hardening — not port the vulnerability, and not assume legacy already denied-by-default.

The qty==issued invariant (clinical decrements getQty() but the stock card records getIssued(); they match only because issued is forced to equal qty) must be preserved if the modern model unifies on a single remaining-qty decrement.
GRN.approve() returns null despite its signature (legacy bug) — the rebuild should return the approved GRN; QA must not write a parity test asserting an empty approve response.
synthesis.recommendedScope:
## Recommended scope & sequence for inc-08

### Verdict on the split: YES — split into 08a and 08b (per the build-plan risk note)
The two sub-domains have different dependency profiles and different risk surfaces. 08a unblocks the clinical stock-decrement TODO that has been deferred since inc-05/06 and is the smaller, higher-clinical-priority slice; 08b carries the bulk of the state-machine surface and the procurement money-ledger. Sequence 08a first because 08b's stock model (StoreItemBatch → PharmacyMedicineBatch) and the shared FEFO/stock-ledger primitives are best designed once, in 08a, then reused.

### Prerequisite (architecture, BEFORE either slice — I own these decisions)
1. **New `clinical::api` published prescription read+dispense port** (the confirmed critical gap — PrescriptionPort is intra-module in clinical.application; clinical::api exposes only consultation contracts). Define: read prescription by uid, a pharmacy worklist contract, and a dispense/decrement seam. Add a Spring Modulith allowed-dependency edge `pharmacy → clinical::api` (mirror billing::api / SettlementDispatcher; no reverse edge, no cycle). This is a clinical+pharmacy joint change — route through engagement-lead.
2. **DocumentType ownership decision.** The enum + DocumentNumberService live in `billing.application` with a single-arg `DocumentType(prefix)` constructor and an exhaustive switch (PCN only). Decide: (a) extend the billing-owned enum + switch with GRN/LPO/PPR/PSR/SPTO/PPTO/PGRN/PPRN and add `nextval` repository methods, accepting billing coupling; or (b) promote DocumentNumberService/DocumentType to the shared kernel. Recommend (b) — it is a cross-cutting concern and inventory should not depend on billing for numbering. Author a short ADR addendum.
3. **Resolve the sequence-name conflict (REAL drift).** ADR-0009 §5/impl-notes name the TO sequences `seq_sto_no` / `seq_ptp_no`, but the as-built V13 seeded `seq_spto_no` / `seq_ppto_no`. The migrations already ran; the as-built names win. Correct ADR-0009 to `seq_spto_no`/`seq_ppto_no` (or document the mapping) so inc-08 wires the existing sequences. Do NOT create new sequences.
4. **Add ErrorCodes**: INSUFFICIENT_STOCK (422), STALE_ENTITY (409, optimistic-lock surface), and a dispense-gate code — gate the dispense hard-gate decision on HDE (see open questions).
5. **Author/extend ADRs**: concurrency model (ADR-0017: pessimistic-write on stock decrement + optimistic @Version on state transitions — explicitly NET-NEW vs legacy's no-lock); stock-ledger re-model (single StockBatch + StockMovement-with-type-enum + scalar/derived balance — data-architect sign-off; map enum values exactly onto legacy reference strings; no WASTAGE unless a legacy disposal path is found).

### 08a — Dispensing + stock core
Scope: medicine masterdata CRUD (medicines/items/coefficients/suppliers as needed for dispensing); PharmacyMedicine aggregate-stock model; StockBatch (FEFO) + StockMovement ledger primitives; clinical prescription dispense (NOT-GIVEN→GIVEN, all-or-nothing, negative-stock gate, FEFO consumption + lot-trace rows, stock-card OUT); pharmacy worklist as a FILTER (PAID/COVERED for OUT/OUTSIDER, +VERIFIED for INPATIENT); manual stock-update (overwrite semantics); opening-stock decision; OTC PharmacySaleOrder full lifecycle (PENDING→APPROVED→ARCHIVED/CANCELED, payment-driven approve via billing, 24h auto-sweeps, GENERAL dummy patient, PSO/PCST numbering, OTC dispense with NO FEFO unless CR'd). Route medicine pricing through billing::api.recordClinicalCharge. Reuse BillingQueries.getBillStatus only if HDE approves a hard dispense gate.

### 08b — Store / procurement / transfers
Scope: Store masterdata + StoreItem/StoreItemBatch/StoreStockCard; LPO→GRN procurement (PENDING-initial LPO state machine, per-line GRN verification + batch-sum rule, one-GRN-per-LPO, store-match/SUBMITTED preconditions, Purchase ledger row, atomic approve); pharmacy↔store transfer (PSR→SPTO→PGRN with coefficient conversion, store-debit-at-TO-issue, pharmacy-credit-at-RN with destination batch creation, move the RN guard into the service); pharmacy↔pharmacy transfer (PPR→PPTO→PPRN, 1:1 no-coefficient, source-debit-at-TO-issue, two-state RN, destination-batch decision); SupplierItemPrice CRUD; all document numbering via the shared DocumentNumberService.

### Sequencing
Prereqs (1-5) → 08a → 08b. Within each slice: schema/migrations (V39+) → aggregates + state machines → module-API commands (TxAuditContext-threaded) → web/OpenAPI → Testcontainers parity tests (round-to-2dp money comparison per ADR-0009 §4; FEFO 1/3·1/7·5/6 coefficient cases; concurrent-document-number uniqueness; BusinessDay close-gate). Audit every state transition via AuditRecorder (net-new, not legacy Envers — label as such). Treat all clinical/financial stories as safety-critical.

### Items that must be RE-SCOPED out of exact-process (CR + engagement-lead approval before any code)
ThreeWayMatch (+ SupplierInvoice entity + payment-release gate + /three-way-match/validate); 8-state prescription lifecycle; salesPharmacy split / A-B cross-pharmacy parity; pharmacy_staff affiliation hard-gate + 403; FEFO-on-OTC; p2p destination-batch creation; pessimistic locking + SELECT-FOR-UPDATE; uniform per-endpoint @PreAuthorize + granular per-transition SoD codes; closing the default-open security posture; server-authoritative PPR/PSR numbering.
openQuestions: DISPENSE HARD-GATE (for HDE + engagement-lead): legacy clinical issue_medicine has NO bill-status check at the terminal (worklist filter only, bypassable). Do we (a) faithfully reproduce filter-only enforcement, or (b) add a hard dispense gate via BillingQueries.getBillStatus (live PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED) — a security/integrity improvement that DEVIATES from legacy? Safety-critical financial decision.

PHARMACY SESSION SCOPING (for engagement-lead + security-architect + business-analyst): legacy has NO pharmacist→pharmacy affiliation and NO server-side scoping (pharmacy is client-supplied per call). Do we (a) reproduce client-trust, or (b) introduce a net-new pharmacy_staff affiliation enforced as a write-time 403 gate? If (b): which increment owns the schema/seed (the plan's 'seeded in I01' is unverified and the table does not exist), and does this make pharmacy stricter than stores (which use only a soft filter)?

THREE-WAY MATCH (for engagement-lead + healthcare/finance owner): confirmed PHANTOM (no SupplierInvoice, no payment-release gate in legacy). Is a real three-way match wanted as a NEW feature? If yes it needs a CR, a SupplierInvoice entity, GRN-detail invoice-qty capture, and a payment-release workflow — all net-new, safety-critical financial scope.

PESSIMISTIC LOCKING + SELECT FOR UPDATE on stock decrement (for engagement-lead): net-new concurrency hardening with no legacy basis (legacy is race-prone, zero locks). Approve as a deliberate correctness improvement (ADR-0017)? Confirm the @Version-on-transitions + pessimistic-on-decrement split is acceptable given legacy's structural idempotency (return-existing-doc) on TO/RN create.

RBAC GRANULARITY + the 177-vs-35 figure (for security-architect + engagement-lead): legacy uses single coarse codes per document family with NO segregation of duties, and leaves whole flows ungated. Do we (a) reproduce coarse gating, or (b) introduce granular per-transition SoD codes and close the default-open posture? The '177-code' figure must be re-derived from the I01 fixture (ratified IAM record says 35 distinct codes) before any acceptance criteria cite it. Confirm PRESCRIPTION-ALL — it is unverified and the legacy dispense flow is ungated.

SPT→SPTO/PPTO PRODUCT-OWNER SIGN-OFF (for engagement-lead): ADR-0009 §6 gates the prefix rename on product-owner sign-off as a release gate. Has it been obtained? Any external report/printed-document/audit-export that hard-codes 'SPT' must be updated. Also ratify the ADR-0009 sequence-name correction (seq_sto_no/seq_ptp_no → as-built seq_spto_no/seq_ppto_no).

DESTINATION BATCH CREATION on pharmacy↔pharmacy receipt (for HDE + engagement-lead): legacy NEVER creates destination batches on p2p RN (commented out) — transferred stock loses batch/expiry traceability at the destination. Reproduce the gap, or fix it (create destination batches like the pharmacy↔store path does)? Clinically the fix is likely correct but it is a behaviour change requiring a CR.

FEFO NULL-EXPIRY SEMANTICS (for HDE): legacy SILENTLY EXCLUDES null-expiry lots whenever any dated lot exists (they can be stranded forever), and uses lowest-id as the secondary sort. Preserve the exclusion quirk, or fix to NULLS LAST (which changes which lot dispenses and which expiry/wastage results)? FEFO-on-OTC (legacy never did it) is the related decision.

OTC NUMBERING + PRICING (for business-analyst + engagement-lead): keep 'PSO/{id}' and the dual 'PCST'+id / 'PCST/'+year+'/'+id customer formats verbatim, or normalize (CR)? And confirm OTC pricing stays flat Medicine.price*qty with paymentType hardcoded CASH (so any 'by payment mode' OTC report is all-CASH unless the hardcode is changed via CR).

PPR/PSR REQUEST-ORDER NUMBERING (for engagement-lead): legacy RO numbers are client-supplied (preview-and-post-back, server never assigns). Move to server-authoritative DocumentNumberService.next() inside the insert tx (a behaviour change), or preserve the preview-then-supply contract?

DOCUMENTNUMBERSERVICE OWNERSHIP (architecture, confirm with engagement-lead): promote DocumentType/DocumentNumberService from billing.application to the shared kernel (recommended — avoids inventory→billing coupling), or extend the billing-owned enum? Affects module dependency graph.
